Title: Data-Analist
Description:
Bij ista Nederland bevinden we ons midden in een fundamentele transformatie. De komende jaren zetten we grote stappen in digitalisering, datagedreven werken en de verdere professionalisering van onze organisatie. Data speelt hierin een steeds belangrijkere rol.




Binnen het team Business Insights werken we eraan om data toegankelijker, begrijpelijker en relevanter te maken voor de hele organisatie. Om deze ontwikkeling te versnellen, zoeken wij een Data-Analist die data weet te vertalen naar concrete inzichten en hiermee bijdraagt aan betere besluitvorming binnen het Management Team en de organisatie.




In deze rol werk je samen met een klein en ervaren team, waarin je de ruimte krijgt om te leren, impact te maken en actief bij te dragen aan de verdere ontwikkeling van data binnen ista.




Dit ga je doen

Als Data-Analist vertaal je data naar stuurinformatie en draag je bij aan de verdere datagedreven ontwikkeling van ista.




Business analyse & data (Power BI)

Ontwikkel en onderhoud je dashboards en rapportages (voornamelijk in Power BI).
Je bouwt mee aan ETL-processen en de doorontwikkeling van het datawarehouse.
Vertaal je businessvragen van verschillende afdelingen naar concrete data-inzichten en rapportages.
Analyseer je data en formuleer je heldere conclusies en aanbevelingen.
Lever je proactief inzichten die bijdragen aan besluitvorming binnen het MT.
Ondersteun je de transformatie van ista-processen door het leveren van relevante business insights.




Inzichten & organisatiebrede ondersteuning

Voer je analyses uit op het gebied van bijvoorbeeld marktontwikkeling, concurrentie, financiën en HR.
Signaleer je trends en verbeterkansen op basis van data.
Automatiseer en optimaliseer je rapportages die nu nog handmatig worden uitgevoerd.
Draag je bij aan het toegankelijk maken van data binnen de organisatie.




PMO-ondersteuning & procesverbetering

Ondersteun je projecten en transformatietrajecten met data en voortgangsinzichten.
Werk je samen met verschillende afdelingen bij planning en voortgangsbewaking.
Help je bij het implementeren en borgen van verbeterinitiatieven binnen processen.




Dit kun je van ons verwachten

Werken bij een organisatie die volop digitaliseert, groeit en professionaliseert.




ista biedt jou:

Een rol waarin je direct impact hebt op besluitvorming en strategie.
Een aantrekkelijk salaris, het ista Keuze Budget en een vaste 13e maand.
De mogelijkheid om je verder te ontwikkelen in data-analyse, Power BI en business insights.
Samenwerking met een ervaren team waarin je veel kunt leren.
Een omgeving waarin data, technologie en duurzaamheid samenkomen.
Een informele werksfeer met korte lijnen en betrokken collega’s.
Faciliteiten voor hybride werken.




Hier herken jij jezelf in

Je beschikt over een minimaal afgeronde HBO opleiding, bijvoorbeeld in Business Administration, Data, Finance of vergelijkbaar.
Je hebt minimaal 2 jaar ervaring met data-analyse en procesoptimalisatie.
Je hebt affiniteit met data, dashboards en het genereren van inzichten.
Je hebt bij voorkeur ervaring met Power BI of vergelijkbare analysetools.
Je bent analytisch sterk en weet data te vertalen naar begrijpelijke conclusies.
Je stelt de juiste vragen om behoeften en achterliggende vraagstukken scherp te krijgen.
Je communiceert helder en weet inzichten overtuigend over te brengen op verschillende niveaus.
Je creëert draagvlak voor jouw analyses en weet om te gaan met weerstand.
Je werkt proactief en draagt bij aan verbetering en structuur binnen processen.
Je beheerst de Nederlandse en Engelse taal goed.
Je hebt interesse in of staat open voor het gebruik van AI en nieuwe technologieën om efficiënter te werken.







Over ista Nederland B.V.

Wij geven gebouwen een toekomst. Dat doen we met producten en diensten die helpen de CO₂-uitstoot van gebouwen te verminderen. We beheren gegevens en processen die ervoor zorgen dat vastgoed duurzamer, efficiënter, veiliger en comfortabeler wordt.




Onze oplossingen zijn digitaal en ondersteunen vastgoedeigenaren, beheerders en bewoners bij de overstap naar duurzame en toekomstbestendige technologieën. Daarbij staat gebruiksgemak centraal: we maken duurzame keuzes toegankelijk en eenvoudig, zodat iedereen kan bijdragen aan de bescherming van het klimaat.




Wereldwijd hebben we meer dan 48 miljoen draadloze apparaten in gebruik en werken ruim 6.600 medewerkers in 21 landen aan energie-efficiënte gebouwen. Onze producten en diensten worden toegepast in meer dan 14 miljoen woningen en commerciële gebouwen bij ruim 470.000 klanten. In 2025 realiseerde de ista Group een omzet van € 1.287 miljoen.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Data Analyst based in Netherlands.

This role is an excellent opportunity to drive business decisions through data, analytics, and automation. You will work closely with technical and business stakeholders to develop dashboards, optimize reporting systems, and build scalable data solutions that improve operational performance. Combining business intelligence, AI, and cloud technologies, you will help transform raw data into actionable insights across multiple functions. The position offers a collaborative, fast-paced environment where innovation and continuous improvement are encouraged. If you enjoy solving complex business problems with data and leveraging modern analytics tools, this role offers both impact and professional growth.

Accountabilities

As a Data Analyst, you will support reporting, business intelligence, and automation initiatives while helping improve data quality, operational efficiency, and strategic decision-making across the organization.


Design, build, and maintain interactive dashboards and business intelligence reports using modern visualization tools.
Analyze operational, sales, recruiting, and financial data to uncover trends, opportunities, and performance insights.
Develop scalable reporting systems, data models, and KPI frameworks to support business growth.
Validate data accuracy and maintain data integrity across multiple platforms and business units.
Build cloud-based workflows and automation processes to streamline reporting and reduce manual effort.
Leverage AI tools to improve reporting efficiency, automate workflows, and support data-driven initiatives.
Perform SQL-based data extraction, transformation, validation, and reporting.
Support API integrations between business platforms and troubleshoot data connectivity issues.
Document reporting processes, technical workflows, and analytics solutions to ensure consistency and scalability.
Collaborate with leadership and cross-functional teams to continuously improve reporting capabilities and operational performance.


Requirements

The ideal candidate combines strong technical expertise with analytical thinking and a passion for leveraging data and automation to solve business challenges.


5+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, or a related field.
Strong proficiency in SQL and Python for data analysis, reporting, and automation.
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills and strong proficiency with Google Sheets.
Experience building dashboards using Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar business intelligence platforms.
Experience working with cloud-based data workflows, REST APIs, and data integrations.
Familiarity with AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, or similar large language models to improve productivity and reporting.
Understanding of automation concepts, workflow optimization, and business process improvement.
Experience supporting business operations through data analysis and performance reporting.
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Information Systems, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related technical discipline is preferred.
Strong analytical mindset, attention to detail, communication skills, and the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment.
Curiosity, adaptability, and a continuous improvement mindset with a willingness to learn new technologies.


Benefits


Competitive compensation with weekly payments.
Flexible remote work opportunity based in India.
Full-time engagement of approximately 30-40 hours per week.
Access to ongoing training, professional development, and upskilling opportunities.
Continuous support and guidance throughout the engagement.
Opportunity to work with modern analytics, AI, automation, and business intelligence technologies.
Collaborative and supportive professional community.
Exposure to a variety of business intelligence and data-driven projects across multiple business functions.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst, Python & SQL - Energy Trading & Optimization, Amsterdam (Hybrid)

Our client is an Amsterdam based energy trading and optimization company on a mission to accelerate the energy transition. The company has grown to over 40 people, with a 12 person tech organization split across software, infrastructure, and data teams.




You'll join a 3 person data team (a Team Lead and two Data Engineers) as the team's 4th member and its first dedicated Data Analyst. You'll work in a fully AWS-based environment, primarily using Python and PostgreSQL, turning incoming market and forecasting data into insight that directly supports trading decisions. This is a long-term, end-to-end project: you'll take ownership of making the underlying data accurate, validated, and usable for the business, with room to grow into more responsibility as a medior analyst.




The role is a mix of analysis and engineering, roughly 75/25: validating and benchmarking incoming data from external providers (including meteorological and energy market data), extracting insights from time series data, building dashboards, and shaping the data pipelines that feed the business's forecasting and trading strategies.




Team

3 people including the lead: 1 Team Lead, 2 Data Engineers. This hire becomes the 4th.




Tech stack

Python, PostgreSQL, AWS (Timestream and DynamoDB experience a plus), Docker and a bit of

Kubernetes a nice to have.




What we're looking for

3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst.
Strong Python skills for data analysis.
Hands-on SQL experience, particularly PostgreSQL.
Experience working in a cloud environment, specifically AWS.
An affinity for data engineering, and comfort with a role that's roughly 75% analysis and 25% engineering.
A proactive self-starter who takes initiative and can work independently.
Knowledge of the energy market is a plus.




Hours and hybrid

Full time, 40 hours per week. 3 days per week in the Amsterdam office, Monday and Friday compulsory, third day flexible.




Salary & contract

55 – 60 K range depending on experience.




Package

Fully covered travel allowance (NS Traject Vrij).
Sports subscription (up to €200/year).
Udemy account plus additional learning budget on request.
Monthly social activities.
Pension contribution.




Interview Process

Phone screening, take home assessment (one week to complete), final interview with the Data Team.




Interested? Please contact me at j.vanbaalen@doghouse.nl

Title: Data Analyst Excel - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Data Analyst Excel

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Conduct in-depth data analysis using Microsoft Excel to extract actionable insights and support decision-making across teams.
Collaborate with project stakeholders to understand data requirements and translate them into meaningful analyses.
Review, clean, and validate datasets to ensure accuracy, consistency, and integrity.
Develop clear, concise reports and visualizations to communicate findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities in complex datasets, contributing to strategic initiatives.
Document methodologies, results, and recommendations with meticulous attention to detail.
Contribute expertise to enhance data-driven decision-making processes within the customer's team.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience performing data analysis in a business environment using Microsoft Excel as the primary tool.
Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy in data processing and reporting.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate analytical findings.
Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively across teams to drive business outcomes.
Solid understanding of Excel functions, formulas, and data visualization tools.
Proficiency in identifying data inconsistencies and implementing corrective measures.
Experience working in fast-paced, data-driven organizations.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience supporting cross-functional teams with data insights to inform business strategy.
Background in presenting analytical outcomes to diverse audiences.
Familiarity with additional data analysis tools or business intelligence platforms.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Wil jij werken met grote hoeveelheden data én analyses ontwikkelen die direct bijdragen aan de groei van Pricewise? Dan is dit jouw kans. Binnen Mavriq en Pricewise hebben we ambitieuze groeiplannen, waarbij data een centrale rol speelt.




Als Data Analyst werk je direct samen met de Head of Marketing en begeef je je op het snijvlak tussen de business en BI. Het BI-team legt de basis voor een diepgaande data-infrastructuur. Jij bouwt hierop voort door onze enorme hoeveelheid data te analyseren, inzichtelijke te maken en te vertalen naar concrete commerciële acties binnen marketing en commercie.




We zoeken iemand die graag meebouwt aan de toekomst. Iemand die nieuwsgierig is, wil experimenteren, en bestaande werkwijzen durft uit te dagen en met behulp van onder andere AI-toepassingen steeds meer waarde uit data weet te halen.




Wat doe je als Data Analyst bij Pricewise?

Als Data Analyst vertaal je ruwe en complexe data naar heldere inzichten en oplossingen. Je werkt op het snijvlak van data, BI en business en helpt collega's om datagedreven beslissingen te nemen.




Dit vind je leuk om te doen

Ruwe en complexe data analyseren en vertalen naar inzichten en concrete oplossingen voor de business, met een focus op digital marketing en commerciële groei.
Kwantitatieve en voorspellende modellen ontwikkelen die bijdragen aan betere besluitvorming.
Dashboards en rapportages ontwikkelen waarmee collega's eenvoudig kunnen sturen op data.
Data pipelines & processen optimaliseren (bijv. voor SEA) en bijdragen aan betrouwbare, goed gestructureerde en breed inzetbare data binnen de organisatie.
Nieuwe toepassingen ontdekken op het gebied van AI en analytics om nog meer waarde uit data te halen.




Dit zoek jij in een baan

Een rijke dataomgeving waarin jouw analyses direct bijdragen aan de groei van Pricewise én miljoenen consumenten helpen betere keuzes te maken.
Een bruto maandsalaris van €4.000 tot €5.500 (exclusief vakantiegeld), passend bij jouw ervaring.
Een aantrekkelijke bonusregeling die aansluit bij de impact die je maakt.
Reiskostenvergoeding, een premievrij pensioen en aantrekkelijke extra's, zoals een sportschoolvergoeding.
Veel aandacht voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling, met volop mogelijkheden om te leren van internationale collega's met expertise in data, analytics en digital commerce.
De mogelijkheid om mee te bouwen aan een snelgroeiende internationale organisatie binnen een dynamisch e-commercelandschap.
Een ondernemende cultuur met korte lijnen, veel verantwoordelijkheid en ruimte om te experimenteren en te innoveren.
Gezellige teamuitjes, borrels en een dagelijks verzorgde gezamenlijke lunch.
Een prettige balans tussen samenwerken op kantoor en geconcentreerd werken vanuit huis.




Dit breng jij mee

Een afgeronde hbo- of wo-opleiding, in Data Analytics, Econometrie of een vergelijkbare richting.
Minimaal twee jaar ervaring in een datarol. Ervaring binnen e-commerce of digital marketing is een pré.
Je bent analytisch, nieuwsgierig en proactief. Je ziet kansen, neemt initiatief en werkt graag samen met collega's uit verschillende disciplines.
Sterke vaardigheden in Python, SQL en Excel. Je schrijft schone, production-ready code en bent goed thuis in data libraries.
Je bent nieuwsgierig naar de business achter de data en begrijpt hoe analyses bijdragen aan commerciële doelstellingen.
Ervaring met BI-tools, zoals Tableau, Looker of PowerBI, en het ontwikkelen van dashboards en rapportages.
Ervaring met (statistische) analyses en het ontwikkelen van voorspellende of kwantitatieve modellen is een pré.
Kennis van data-infrastructuur. Je begrijpt hoe datapipelines en datamodellen werken, kunt werken met tools als BigQuery en schakelt gemakkelijk met data engineers.
Affiniteit met (generatieve) AI. Je hebt ervaring met LLM's en AI-tools of bent enthousiast om je hierin verder te ontwikkelen.




Bedrijfsbeschrijving

Pricewise is een van de grootste vergelijkingssites op het gebied van energie, verzekeringen, internet, tv, leningen en meer. Vanuit ons hoofdkantoor in Amsterdam bieden wij consumenten de mogelijkheid om gemakkelijk over te stappen naar de beste aanbieding in de markt. Via ons vergelijkingsplatform bespaarden al miljoenen mensen honderden euro’s per jaar.




Sinds 2024 is Pricewise onderdeel van Mavriq: een snelgroeiende internationale groep met meer dan vijftien vergelijkingsplatformen in Nederland, Spanje, Italië, Frankrijk, Duitsland en Mexico. Vanuit onze leidende positie bouwen we aan de toekomst van online vergelijken, waarin data een essentiële rol speelt.

HTML:
Wil jij werken met grote hoeveelheden data én analyses ontwikkelen die direct bijdragen aan de groei van Pricewise? Dan is dit jouw kans. Binnen Mavriq en Pricewise hebben we ambitieuze groeiplannen, waarbij data een centrale rol speelt.


Als Data Analyst werk je direct samen met de Head of Marketing en begeef je je op het snijvlak tussen de business en BI. Het BI-team legt de basis voor een diepgaande data-infrastructuur. Jij bouwt hierop voort door onze enorme hoeveelheid data te analyseren, inzichtelijke te maken en te vertalen naar concrete commerciële acties binnen marketing en commercie.


We zoeken iemand die graag meebouwt aan de toekomst. Iemand die nieuwsgierig is, wil experimenteren, en bestaande werkwijzen durft uit te dagen en met behulp van onder andere AI-toepassingen steeds meer waarde uit data weet te halen.


Wat doe je als Data Analyst bij Pricewise?

Als Data Analyst vertaal je ruwe en complexe data naar heldere inzichten en oplossingen. Je werkt op het snijvlak van data, BI en business en helpt collega's om datagedreven beslissingen te nemen.


Dit vind je leuk om te doen

  • Ruwe en complexe data analyseren en vertalen naar inzichten en concrete oplossingen voor de business, met een focus op digital marketing en commerciële groei.
  • Kwantitatieve en voorspellende modellen ontwikkelen die bijdragen aan betere besluitvorming.
  • Dashboards en rapportages ontwikkelen waarmee collega's eenvoudig kunnen sturen op data.
  • Data pipelines & processen optimaliseren (bijv. voor SEA) en bijdragen aan betrouwbare, goed gestructureerde en breed inzetbare data binnen de organisatie.

Title: Data analyst intern
Description:
About The Joint Impact Model Foundation (https://www.jointimpactmodel.org/

The Joint Impact Model Foundation is a non-profit responsible for the credible oversight of the development of the Joint Impact Model (JIM) and its uses, as well as to manage the calendar of updates and model development.




Launched by FMO (https://www.fmo.nl/ ), the Netherlands’ development bank, and other international finance institutions in 2021, the foundation works towards harmonization of key indicators to build accountability and steer financing in the US$140tn finance sector.

Using input data such as revenue and power production from investment portfolios, the Joint Impact Model enables users to estimate financial flows through the economy and its resulting economic (value added), social (employment) and environmental (greenhouse gas emissions) impacts, as well as climate risk.




Your role

We are looking for an intern to support the activities of the JIM Foundation.

The Joint Impact Model requires the type of hands-on support typical for many young organizations. You will work alongside young professionals and have many cross-institution and cross-country interactions.

Your role will focus on working on the development of our model and input databases and potentially on our ongoing model application projects, for which we would require you to do research, process data, analyze the data, and show the key findings in an appropriate format. Ad-hoc or additional support may be required to respond to membership technical queries and support technical assistance projects and other support throughout the Foundation.

Carry out other tasks as directed by the full-time analysts and the technical director.




Your background

Minimum Requirements

·      Strong interest in data analysis, sustainable finance, and problem-solving

·      Currently in a bachelor’s or master’s degree with focus on economics, finance, data science or related fields

·      Ability to work independently

·      Proficiency with MS Office, special emphasis in Excel and PowerPoint

·      Excellence in English, both written and spoken

·      Self-starter, strong work ethic, eager to learn

·      Available for a period of 6 months, 4-5 days per week

·      Own laptop and mobile phone

Bonus Skills

·      Programming and AI skills.

·      Experience in impact-sector.

·      Fluency in languages beyond English (especially Spanish or French).

·      Familiarity with impact measurement, Environmental, Social and Governance, or Sustainable Development Goal frameworks.




The internship is suitable for bachelor or master student. The remuneration is EUR 750 net a month (based on 40 hours a week). You must already be studying and living in the Netherlands, and you must be enrolled for the complete duration of the internship. We do not reimburse transport. Two days a week are office days (we work from FMO's office in the Hague).

JIM Foundation is an equal opportunity employer that values diversity in all its forms. We do not discriminate on the grounds of race, color, ethnic or national origin, nationality, sex, pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding or related leave, gender identity or gender expression, sexual orientation, religion or belief, political opinion, age, disability or chronic illness, civil or marital status, full-time or part-time status, temporary or permanent contract status, the exercise of statutory leave rights, or any other ground protected by applicable law.




Start date 15 September 2026

Application: Send your CV and a brief motivation including information about availability to info@jointimpactmodel.org. We will review applications on an ongoing basis.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Rotterdam (three days onsite).

Salary €65,000 to €80,000 plus benefits.




This is a great opportunity to step into a business-critical data analyst role where your insights will directly influence priorities, decision-making and growth. You will join at a moment of change, with the chance to shape how data is used across the organisation and take real ownership of analytical projects.




The Company

They are a growing organisation operating in a dynamic market environment, where data plays an increasingly important role in strategy and performance. The business values clear communication, initiative and practical insight over theory alone. With a lean data setup, they offer a close-knit environment where your work is visible and impactful. Collaboration with senior stakeholders is a core part of how they operate.




The Role

You will be responsible for delivering actionable insights and managing BI-focused data initiatives across the business.
You will work closely with stakeholders to understand needs, set priorities and translate questions into analysis.
You will take ownership of both larger analytical initiatives and smaller ad hoc projects.
You will support the evolution of reporting and dashboards using SQL-based data models and Power BI.
You will help reassess team focus and objectives following recent organisational changes.

Your Skills and Experience

Strong commercial experience in SQL for data analysis and data modelling.
Hands-on experience building reports and dashboards in Power BI.
Confidence working independently and managing analytical projects end to end.
Proven ability to engage with stakeholders and communicate insights clearly.
Exposure to Python or advanced analytics is beneficial but not essential.




What They Offer

A competitive salary between €65,000 and €80,000 with flexibility based on experience.
Recently updated salary bands with clear room for growth.
Hybrid working with a strong in-office collaboration culture in Rotterdam.
A high-impact role with autonomy, ownership and visibility at senior level.




How to Apply

If you are a Data Analyst looking for a role where you can truly influence how data is used, apply now to learn more.

Title: Junior Data Analyst Retail
Description:
Note: This job posting is only open to native level or near-native level Dutch speakers. If you are not proficient in Dutch, please have a look at our other job postings instead!)

Your role in our team

As a Junior Data Analyst Retail, you support client teams with analysis, dashboards, KPI insights and data quality checks. You work with data from retail processes such as sales, customer behavior, inventory, promotions, e-commerce or supply chain and help make insights understandable for business stakeholders. At CGI, you grow by working in projects with experienced data consultants, project managers and technical specialists.

How You Strengthen The Team


Analyze datasets and translate findings into clear insights and visualizations.
Build and maintain dashboards and reports in Power BI, Tableau or comparable tooling.
Perform basic data quality checks and document definitions, assumptions and issues.
Support requirements, user stories, KPI definitions and stakeholder questions.
Work with senior consultants, data engineers, business analysts and client teams.
Contribute to presentations, workshops and analyses for retail and consumer-services clients.


What You Bring


1-2 years of experience, internship or traineeship in data analysis, BI, business analysis or analytics.
Solid foundation in SQL, Excel and at least one BI tool such as Power BI, Tableau or Looker.
Affinity with retail, e-commerce, supply chain, customer analytics or commercial analysis.
Able to explain data outcomes clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Accurate, eager to learn, communicative and comfortable with client contact.
Professional Dutch and English, or demonstrably quick to develop to that level.


We would also value


Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300) or demonstrable Power BI experience.
Microsoft Azure Data Fundamentals (DP-900), Azure AI Fundamentals (AI-900) or comparable fundamentals.
Basic Python or R for data cleaning, analysis or automation.
Experience with KPIs such as revenue, margin, conversion, inventory, customer segments or promotion effectiveness.
Experience with Jira, Azure DevOps, Confluence or working in an agile team.


Where will you work?

You will join CGI’s retail team and work on assignments where data, business questions and technology come together. You will have room to learn, while contributing from the start to analyses, dashboards and insights that help clients make better decisions.

What We Offer


A learning environment where you can grow quickly towards data consulting in retail.
Guidance from experienced consultants and access to CGI’s broader knowledge network.
Varied assignments where data directly influences commercial and operational decisions.
Room to obtain certifications and further develop your technical and consulting skills.
A team culture centered on collaboration, knowledge sharing and ownership.


Why CGI?

CGI is one of the world's largest IT consultancy firms. Our people keep the Netherlands running. Government agencies, banks, (aero-) space, infrastructure organizations and energy and manufacturing companies are clients of CGI. We work on interesting projects that touch the daily lives of millions of people. We are present regionally, augmented with global domain knowledge, our ecosystem, and colleagues spread all over the world. We are proud of the impactful projects that we, together with our colleagues, make a success.

At CGI, we place great value on diversity and inclusion, as this not only promotes collaboration but often leads to better results. We look forward to your contribution to our team!


Work in small self-managing teams consisting of experts in your field
Take control of your own career with the space to continue developing yourself
You work with the latest technologies
An excellent salary and attractive secondary employment conditions
Immediate permanent contract
Part-time possibilities
And the opportunity to become a CGI shareholder


Join our team of experts and apply now!

We will contact you as soon as possible. Questions about the position? Ask them to Floris Diemel, Director Consulting Services via floris.diemel@cgi.com.

Do you have any questions about the recruitment process? Please contact Recruitment Business Partner Kimberley Thoonen-Broens by phone at 06 15 69 04 59 or via kimberley.broens@cgi.com.

We werken niet samen met externe recruitment bureau’s. Acquisitie wordt daarom niet op prijs gesteld.

Your future duties and responsibilities

Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.

Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.

That same commitment to fairness extends to how we use technology. To support our recruitment team, AI tools may be used to help assess applications though they never replace human judgement. All hiring decisions remain entirely in the hands of our recruitment professionals.

Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
Vacature

Business Data Analyst

Breda / Rotterdam

32-40

Ben jij een data professional die verder kijkt dan alleen de data? Iemand die niet alleen analyses levert, maar ook snapt wat de business ermee wilt? Dan zoeken wij jou.

Over Opcharge

Opcharge is een snelgroeiende speler die slimme laadoplossingen ontwikkelt voor gemeenten en bedrijven. Vanaf dag één zijn we volledig gericht op duurzame mobiliteit. Onze ambitie is duidelijk: de energietransitie versnellen door de uitrol van slimme en toekomstbestendige laadinfrastructuur.

We bevinden ons in een fase waarin geen dag hetzelfde is. Dat betekent veel ruimte voor initiatief, korte lijnen en de kans om écht impact te maken. We geloven in eigenaarschap, samenwerken en continu verbeteren. Eind 2026 zetten we de volgende stap met een nieuw kantoor in hartje Rotterdam – een mijlpaal die past bij onze groeiambities.

De functie

Opcharge groeit hard en data speelt daarin een centrale rol. Als Business Data Analyst ben je onderdeel van de Data & IT afdeling en werk je dagelijks samen met business en engineering. Je zorgt dat data uit ons warehouse niet blijft liggen, maar wordt omgezet naar inzichten die er echt toe doen, van operationele verbeteringen tot strategische keuzes op MT-niveau.

Je Verantwoordelijkheden Bestaan Uit


Je analyseert data om trends, patronen en kansen te identificeren
Je werkt effectief samen met business- en technische teams om inzichten te vertalen naar concrete acties
Je ontwikkelt en verbetert rapportages en dashboards die data toegankelijk maken
Je bouwt voorspellende modellen die bijdragen aan strategische keuzes rondom de uitbreiding van ons laadpalenportfolio
Je ontwerpt en bouwt robuuste datastructuren in Snowflake


Wat breng jij mee?


Je hebt 1 tot 5 jaar werkervaring binnen het datadomein
Je werkt projectmatig en neemt eigenaarschap over voortgang en resultaat
Je bent vaardig in SQL en hebt ervaring met BI-tools zoals Power BI. Kennis van Python is een pré
Je bent sterk in presenteren en weet de businessbehoefte achter data te achterhalen door de juiste vragen te stellen
Je reflecteert op je werk en gebruikt feedback om je analyses en aanpak continu te verbeteren


Arbeidsvoorwaarden

Wat bieden wij?


Salaris tussen €4.200 en €5.000 bruto per maand bij een werkweek van 40 uur
Een functie voor 32 tot 40 uur per week
Flexibel werken mogelijk, met 3 dagen per week op kantoor
Werken in een snelgroeiende, ondernemende organisatie met echte impact
Veel ruimte voor eigen initiatief en procesverbetering
Een informele en betrokken werksfeer

Title: Senior Analyst (Data Analytics)
Description:
Senior Analyst (Data Analytics) Central Amsterdam, Hybrid Working, International Technology Business.

Altum Consulting are recruiting for a Senior Analyst (Data Analytics) to join an international finance team based in central Amsterdam. This is a high-impact role sitting at the intersection of Finance, Data and Technology and will help shape how the business uses data to drive commercial and financial decision-making.

You'll act as the bridge between Finance and Data, leading initiatives that improve the quality, accessibility and automation of financial data. This is an excellent opportunity for someone who can combine strong financial and analytical capabilities with technical data skills (SQL,Python)

Role:

Lead finance data transformation initiatives, improving the quality, consistency and accessibility of financial data.
Design and deliver automated reporting solutions that reduce manual processes and improve decision-making.
Partner with Finance and Business teams to evolve data infrastructure, reporting capabilities and analytics tooling.
Own and govern key financial and operational KPIs, ensuring consistent definitions and trusted reporting across the business.
Develop and maintain financial models to support forecasting, budgeting, scenario planning and strategic initiatives.
Build scalable dashboards and self-service reporting that provide meaningful commercial insights.
Translate business requirements into data solutions, acting as the key liaison between Finance and technical teams.
Identify opportunities to automate existing FP&A processes using data and technology.
Support the ongoing evolution of the company's analytics capabilities, helping establish best practices around data governance and reporting.
Present insights and recommendations to senior stakeholders, influencing business decisions through data-driven analysis.

You'll be/have:

Experience in Group FP&A, Finance Analytics or a similar analytical role.
Strong SQL skills with the ability to write complex queries and work confidently with large datasets.
Python experience for data analysis, automation or process improvement.
Experience acting as the bridge between Finance and Data teams.
Strong financial modelling skills and experience supporting planning, forecasting and commercial decision-making.
Experience improving reporting processes through automation and scalable data solutions.
A solid understanding of data infrastructure, data pipelines and modern analytics environments.
The ability to navigate evolving or imperfect data environments while maintaining a pragmatic, solutions-focused approach.
Excellent stakeholder management skills, with experience partnering with senior business leaders and cross-functional teams.
Strong communication skills, able to translate technical concepts into clear business insights.

Why Join?

You'll be joining an international Technology business where data is central to how decisions are made. This role offers genuine ownership, the opportunity to influence the future finance data landscape, and close collaboration with teams across Finance, Data and Technology.

If you're excited by combining financial expertise with modern data analytics to drive business performance, please reach out to Jen McMurray on the attached details.

Title: BI Analyst Intern
Description:
About Hawkroot

Hawkroot connects businesses with pre-vetted data and technology professionals for project-based engagements. We help organisations across multiple sectors deliver measurable outcomes in analytics, reporting, automation, and digital transformation. We work with clients and professionals across Europe, including the Netherlands, supporting data and analytics project delivery on a fully remote basis.




Role Overview

Hawkroot is seeking a motivated BI Analyst Intern to join our remote team on a part-time basis. This internship is open to final-year students and recent graduates based in the Netherlands who are looking to apply their analytical and reporting skills to real-world business intelligence projects and build practical experience in a professional environment.




Duration: 3 months

Working hours: 20 hours per week, Monday to Friday

Monthly stipend: €1,560 gross per month

Work arrangement: Fully remote

Location: Netherlands




What You Will Do

Support the development and maintenance of BI dashboards and interactive reports using Power BI or Tableau
Collect, clean, and structure data from multiple sources to ensure accuracy and consistency across reporting outputs
Write and execute SQL queries to extract and manipulate data from relevant databases
Analyse business data to identify trends, patterns, and key performance indicators
Assist in translating business requirements into clear and actionable data visualisations
Support the documentation of BI processes, data definitions, and reporting standards
Conduct data quality checks and flag inconsistencies or anomalies to the team
Collaborate with team members to deliver accurate and timely business intelligence outputs




What We Are Looking For

Final-year student or recent graduate in Business Intelligence, Data Science, Computer Science, Information Systems, Mathematics, or a related field
Hands-on experience or strong academic knowledge of Power BI, Tableau, or similar BI tools
Working knowledge of SQL for data extraction and manipulation
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel including pivot tables, advanced functions, and data visualisation
Familiarity with data modelling concepts and reporting best practices
Strong analytical thinking with high attention to detail
Clear written and verbal communication skills in English
Self-motivated and able to manage tasks independently in a remote setting
Based in the Netherlands or holding valid authorisation to work in the Netherlands




What This Role Offers

Hands-on involvement in live BI and reporting projects from day one
Practical experience building dashboards and data visualisations in a professional setting
Exposure to SQL, Power BI, Tableau, and Excel in real business intelligence workflows
A supportive remote team with experienced BI and data professionals
Monthly stipend of €1,560 gross per month
A strong and credible addition to your professional portfolio and CV




Equal Opportunity

Hawkroot is committed to equal treatment and non-discrimination across all stages of hiring. We welcome applications from final-year students and recent graduates of all backgrounds, nationalities, and experiences. All candidates based in the Netherlands or holding valid authorisation to work in the Netherlands are encouraged to apply.

Title: Business Intelligence Intern
Description:
Metyis is growing! We are looking for a Business Intelligence Intern to join our Data and Analytics team in Amsterdam.

Who We Are

Metyis is a global and forward-thinking firm operating across a wide range of industries, developing and delivering AI & Data, Digital Commerce, Marketing & Design solutions and Advisory services. At Metyis, our long-term partnership model brings long-lasting impact and growth to our business partners and clients through extensive execution capabilities.

With our team, you can experience a collaborative environment with highly skilled multidisciplinary experts, where everyone has room to build bigger and bolder ideas. Being part of Metyis means you can speak your mind and be creative with your knowledge. Imagine the things you can achieve with a team that encourages you to be the best version of yourself.

We are Metyis. Partners for Impact.

What We Offer


2 months of intensive training from our super skilled BI team, on topics like data warehousing, data modelling and Power BI reporting (Data transformation, DAX, report building).
Followed by 4 months of relevant working experience on a live business case.
Interact with clients regularly to drive their business towards impactful change.
Play a key role in the team by creating new business solutions via data-driven insights.
Receive guidance by a dedicated mentor to make sure you make the most out of your time at Metyis.
Experience being part of a fast-growing international and diverse team.



What You Will Do


Assist in the design, development and maintenance of BI reporting systems.
Understand with data warehousing and data modelling activities.
Participate in the creation of data-driven solutions to support business needs.
Collaborate with data scientists, business analysts and software engineers to understand and implement reporting requirements.
Participate in report reviews and providing constructive feedback.
Stay up to date with the latest developments in all the BI tech stacks and best practices.



What You’ll Bring


Currently pursuing a Master's degree in Business Analytics, Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to derive insights from complex data sets.
Basic understanding of SQL or BI is helpful.
Ability to work in a team environment.
A passion for data analysis and a desire to learn and grow, in the field of business intelligence.



At Metyis, we are driven by curiosity and collaboration. We value diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging (DEIB) in all its forms as it makes us stronger as an organisation and promotes creativity and innovation. We welcome all talents and are committed to creating a workplace where every employee can make a meaningful impact and grow.

Title: Sustainability Analyst - Analytics & Reporting
Description:
City : Rotterdam State : Zuid-Holland (NL-ZH) Country : Netherlands (NL) Requisition Number : 46742

At Bunge, sustainability is not a separate agenda—it is embedded in how we operate, source, process and deliver oilseed and grain products across the globe for food, feed and renewable fuel. As aSustainability Analystwithin our Global Sustainability team, you will play a key role in transforming complex sustainability data into meaningful insights that support business decisions, environmental performance management and external reporting.

This is an exciting opportunity for someone who enjoys combining analytics, sustainability and business impact. Unlike many sustainability roles that focus on a single country or region, this position offers exposure to Bunge's global operations of 150 processing plants and supply chain footprint handling 200 mln tonnes of agricultural commodities across 50 countries, enabling you to contribute to sustainability performance on a broader scale.

What You'll Do

As part of the Sustainability Analytics & Reporting team, you will help ensure the availability, quality and integrity of sustainability data and analytics used across the organization.

Your responsibilities will include:


Developing and improving analytical tools and reporting models related to environmental performance and forecasting.,
Supporting and peer-reviewing recurring and ad-hoc sustainability reporting deliverables for internal and external stakeholders.
Analyzing large sustainability datasets relating to energy, greenhouse gas emissions (Scope 1, 2 and 3), water, waste and other sustainability performance indicators.
Collaborating with stakeholders across Sustainability, Operations, Finance, Business Technology and other business functions to enhance data quality and reporting processes.
Supporting the development and continuous improvement of dashboards and reporting solutions, both through in-house Business Intelligence teams and through self-made solutions using Excel, Tableau and upcoming AI-driven analytical tools.
Contributing to the measurement, analysis and reporting of environmental sustainability performance.
Preparing reports, visualizations and presentations that translate data into actionable business insights.
Supporting internal controls and audit activities by maintaining high standards of data quality, documentation and reporting accuracy.
Identifying opportunities to improve, automate and streamline sustainability reporting processes.


What Makes This Role Unique

This is not a regional level sustainability role.

As part of our global Sustainability team, you will work across multiple regions, business units and operational environments, helping to drive sustainability insights and reporting across Bunge's worldwide footprint.

You will contribute to topics that are shaping the future of the food and agriculture industry, including environmental performance, sustainability reporting and evolving regulatory requirements. Your work will support decision-making at both operational and strategic levels, helping Bunge achieve its sustainability ambitions.

What We're Looking For

You are naturally analytical, curious and comfortable working with large and complex datasets. You enjoy understanding the story behind the numbers and can translate data into clear, actionable insights.

Additionally, you bring:


A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Sustainability, Environmental Sciences, Finance, Accounting, Data Analytics, Business Management, Agronomy, Food Process Engineering or a related field.
Minimum 2 years working experience in sustainability reporting, sustainability analytics or sustainability auditing, involving CSRD and GHG Protocol, ideally with exposure towards agriculture commodities.,
Strong Excel skills and experience working with reporting, analytics or Business Intelligence tools such as Tableau, with a keen interest into using AI pragmatically.
The ability to work with complex datasets while maintaining a high level of accuracy and attention to detail.
Strong problem-solving skills and a structured, analytical mindset.
Project management skills with the ability to manage multiple workflows, track deadlines and coordinate deliverables across multiple teams.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with stakeholders across functions and geographies.
Professional proficiency in English. Knowledge of Dutch, Portuguese and/or Spanish is welcomed.
Eligible to work in the Netherlands.


What We Offer

At Bunge, you'll join a collaborative and international environment where sustainability, innovation and data-driven decision-making come together.

We offer:


A highly visible role within Bunge's Global Sustainability organization.
The opportunity to contribute to sustainability initiatives with global reach and impact.
Exposure to a wide range of stakeholders across different regions, functions and businesses.
Continuous learning and professional development opportunities.
A dynamic and purpose-driven work environment within one of the world's leading agribusiness and food companies.
Competitive compensation and benefits.
Attractive office next to Rotterdam Blaak station.


Interested?

Click theApplybutton and let’s connect.

We look forward to hearing from you.

At Bunge (NYSE: BG), our purpose is to connect farmers to consumers to deliver essential food, feed and fuel to the world. As a premier agribusiness solutions provider, our team of :34,000 dedicated employees partner with farmers across the globe to move agricultural commodities from where they’re grown to where they’re needed—in faster, smarter, and more efficient ways. We are a world leader in grain origination, storage, distribution, oilseed processing and refining, offering a broad portfolio of plant-based oils, fats, and proteins. We work alongside our customers at both ends of the value chain to deliver quality products and develop tailored, innovative solutions that address evolving consumer needs. With 200+ years of experience and presence in over 50 countries, we are committed to strengthening global food security, advancing sustainability, and helping communities prosper where we operate. We Are One Team – Collaborative, Respectful, Inclusive

HTML:
City : Rotterdam State : Zuid-Holland (NL-ZH) Country : Netherlands (NL) Requisition Number : 46742

At Bunge, sustainability is not a separate agenda—it is embedded in how we operate, source, process and deliver oilseed and grain products across the globe for food, feed and renewable fuel. As aSustainability Analystwithin our Global Sustainability team, you will play a key role in transforming complex sustainability data into meaningful insights that support business decisions, environmental performance management and external reporting.

This is an exciting opportunity for someone who enjoys combining analytics, sustainability and business impact. Unlike many sustainability roles that focus on a single country or region, this position offers exposure to Bunge's global operations of 150 processing plants and supply chain footprint handling 200 mln tonnes of agricultural commodities across 50 countries, enabling you to contribute to sustainability performance on a broader scale.

What You'll Do

As part of the Sustainability Analytics & Reporting team, you will help ensure the availability, quality and integrity of sustainability data and analytics used across the organization.

Your responsibilities will include:

  • Developing and improving analytical tools and reporting models related to environmental performance and forecasting.,
  • Supporting and peer-reviewing recurring and ad-hoc sustainability reporting deliverables for internal and external stakeholders.
  • Analyzing large sustainability datasets relating to energy, greenhouse gas emissions (Scope 1, 2 and 3), water, waste and other sustainability performance indicators.
  • Collaborating with stakeholders across Sustainability, Operations, Finance, Business Technology and other business functions to enhance data quality and reporting processes.
  • Supporting the development and continuous improvement of dashboards and reporting solutions, both through in-house Business Intelligence teams and through self-made solutions using Excel, Tableau and upcoming AI-driven analytical tools.
  • Contributing to the measurement, analysis and reporting of environmental sustainability performance.
  • Preparing reports, visualizations and presentations that translate data into actionable business insights.
  • Supporting internal controls and audit activities by maintaining high standards of data quality, documentation and reporting accuracy.
  • Identifying opportunities to improve, automate and streamline sustainability reporting processes.

What Makes This Role Unique

This is not a regional level sustainability role.

As part of our global Sustainability team, you will work across multiple regions, business units and operational environments, helping to drive sustainability insights and reporting across Bunge's worldwide footprint.

You will contribute to topics that are shaping the future of the food and agriculture industry, including environmental performance, sustainability reporting and evolving regulatory requirements. Your work will support decision-making at both operational and strategic levels, helping Bunge achieve its sustainability ambitions.

What We're Looking For

You are naturally analytical, curious and comfortable working with large and complex datasets. You enjoy understanding the story behind the numbers and can translate data into clear, actionable insights.

Additionally, you bring:

  • A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Sustainability, Environmental Sciences, Finance, Accounting, Data Analytics, Business Management, Agronomy, Food Process Engineering or a related field.
  • Minimum 2 years working experience in sustainability reporting, sustainability analytics or sustainability auditing, involving CSRD and GHG Protocol, ideally with exposure towards agriculture commodities.,
  • Strong Excel skills and experience working with reporting, analytics or Business Intelligence tools such as Tableau, with a keen interest into using AI pragmatically.
  • The ability to work with complex datasets while maintaining a high level of accuracy and attention to detail.
  • Strong problem-solving skills and a structured, analytical mindset.
  • Project management skills with the ability to manage multiple workflows, track deadlines and coordinate deliverables across multiple teams.
  • Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with stakeholders across functions and geographies.
  • Professional proficiency in English. Knowledge of Dutch, Portuguese and/or Spanish is welcomed.
  • Eligible to work in the Netherlands.

What We Offer

At Bunge, you'll join a collaborative and international environment where sustainability, innovation and data-driven decision-making come together.

We offer:

  • A highly visible role within Bunge's Global Sustainability organization.
  • The opportunity to contribute to sustainability initiatives with global reach and impact.
  • Exposure to a wide range of stakeholders across different regions, functions and businesses.
  • Continuous learning and professional development opportunities.
  • A dynamic and purpose-driven work environment within one of the world's leading agribusiness and food companies.
  • Competitive compensation and benefits.
  • Attractive office next to Rotterdam Blaak station.

Interested?

Click theApplybutton and let’s connect.

We look forward to hearing from you.

At Bunge (NYSE: BG), our purpose is to connect farmers to consumers to deliver essential food, feed and fuel to the world. As a premier agribusiness solutions provider, our team of :34,000 dedicated employees partner with farmers across the globe to move agricultural commodities from where they’re grown to where they’re needed—in faster, smarter, and more efficient ways. We are a world leader in grain origination, storage, distribution, oilseed processing and refining, offering a broad portfolio of plant-based oils, fats, and proteins. We work alongside our customers at both ends of the value chain to deliver quality products and develop tailored, innovative solutions that address evolving consumer needs. With 200+ years of experience and presence in over 50 countries, we are committed to strengthening global food security, advancing sustainability, and helping communities prosper where we operate. Bunge has its registered office in Geneva, Switzerland and its corporate headquarters in St. Louis, Missouri. Learn more at Bunge.com.

Every day our people exemplify these values, which represent Bunge at its core:

  • We Are One Team – Collaborative, Respectful, Inclusive
  • We Lead The Way – Agile, Empowered, Innovative
  • We Do What’s Right – Safety, Sustainability, With Integrity

If this sounds like you, join us! We value and invest in people who believe in our purpose and are excited to live it every day – people who are #ProudtoBeBunge

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Are you a data/business professional passionate about using data to make meaningful impact? Read on and apply now for the role as Data Analyst at Anthony Veder!

Navigating the Future

Working at Anthony Veder means operating at the heart of the transition to a more sustainable world. We are always looking for new ways to challenge the status quo, developing new technological and logistical solutions to help our clients reach their goals.

We work in a culture of trust, openness, and creativity. Always keeping an eye on the long term. This is how we became pioneers in the maritime industry. To meet the vision to be an ambitious leader in gas shipping service solutions, Anthony Veder has identified the need for data-driven innovations and –decisions within the organization. This means that using data and insights should become a core part of our overall strategy, culture and business processes. The Data & Analytics team was founded to drive the transition to a data-driven organization.

The Data & Analytics team is responsible for creating value for Anthony Veder using data and analytics while providing a solid data foundation for our operation, both governance and architecture. The team supports the organization by managing a data platform that provides a single source of truth to every data consumer and by providing the insight to solve the challenges of our business. Also, the team is facilitating the adoption of these insights into the operational processes at Anthony Veder.

Your areas of expertise


Understand business needs to identify challenges and risks better and make informed decisions on providing data insights.
Collaborate with the team to continuously improve the way we work and process work to maximize our value to the organization.
Gain a deeper understanding of the challenges and opportunities within our organization, deliver actionable insights for these and support the teams in organizing around this.
Contribute to improving data literacy and create a culture of data-driven decision making within Anthony Veder.
Contribute to establishing standards, guidelines and procedures to assure efficient and effective delivery of insights to the organization.
Data modelling and data platforming with the ability to perform first line support and small changes on our data platform.



Additionally, What You Can Expect Is


A culture that expects and supports entrepreneurship.
A position that offers a lot of autonomy and expects a high level of ownership for that autonomy.
The opportunity to have a direct impact on the way the organization uses data.



What We Offer


A dynamic organization with an entrepreneurial character and informal and open culture.
A competitive salary.
An employer-financed pension scheme.
28 vacation days with the option to buy five more.
Combining working from home and at our beautiful office in Rotterdam's shipping district.



What We Ask


A higher professional education (HBO) or scientific/research education (WO, Bachelor or Master in a relevant domain such as business, IT, engineering or shipping.
5+ years of experience in a similar role.
Ability to use Power BI and SQL, pre would also be coding skills with Python and Git.
Excellent verbal and written command of English.
Analytical mindset and ability to organize, refine and manage your work.



Do you have what it takes to be part of the AV family? Apply now and find out!

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Senior Data Analyst

Amsterdam, Netherlands

Competitive salary and benefits




This is an opportunity to join a high-impact data team at the heart of a large-scale digital platform. You will work on complex, real-world problems that directly influence product direction, commercial strategy, and customer experience. This role offers strong exposure to senior stakeholders and the chance to shape how data drives decision-making across the business.




The Company

They are a leading digital platform operating at scale across a high-traffic consumer marketplace. With millions of monthly users, they enable both individuals and businesses to connect, transact, and grow. Their product spans mobile and web, and they operate in a fast-paced, data-rich environment. Data plays a central role in how they optimise user experience, monetisation, and long-term growth.




The Role

Partner with product, commercial, and marketing stakeholders to define analytical priorities and deliver insights that drive decision-making
Translate ambiguous business questions into structured analysis and actionable outcomes
Build dashboards and reporting that focus on meaningful KPIs across revenue, growth, and customer behaviour
Analyse large-scale datasets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities across the platform
Influence stakeholders at all levels through clear and compelling communication of insights
Support experimentation and measurement strategies to improve product and commercial performance
Collaborate with data and engineering teams to improve tracking, data quality, and metric definitions




Your Skills & Experience




Strong commercial experience in data analysis within a product-led digital environment such as e-commerce or marketplaces
Advanced SQL skills, including working with complex datasets and optimised queries
Hands-on experience with Python for analysis, modelling, or automation
Familiarity with modern cloud data platforms such as BigQuery, Databricks, or AWS
Experience using BI tools such as Tableau to create impactful dashboards
Strong understanding of key business concepts including customer lifecycle, monetisation, and growth metrics
Confident communicator with the ability to influence stakeholders and translate data into business value
Comfortable working in ambiguous environments and proactively identifying opportunities




What They Offer




Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package
A collaborative, cross-functional working environment
Opportunity to influence business and product strategy at scale
Strong career progression and exposure to senior leadership
Access to modern data tools and technology




How to Apply

If you are a commercially minded Senior Data Analyst looking to make a tangible impact, apply now to find out more.

Title: Data Analist / BI Specialist
HTML:
Als Data Analist / BI Specialist zorg jij ervoor dat organisaties hun data omzetten in waardevolle inzichten. Je analyseert complexe datasets, vertaalt deze naar heldere rapportages en dashboards en adviseert stakeholders over datagedreven beslissingen. Met moderne BI-tools zoals Power BI, Tableau of Qlik maak je informatie toegankelijk en begrijpelijk. Daarmee help je onze klanten – zoals de Belastingdienst, Gemeente Den Haag, Centraal Justitieel Incassobureau en VGZ – om hun beleid en processen te verbeteren en toekomstgericht te sturen.


Waarom past deze vacature bij jou?

Je hebt een passie voor data en ziet het als jouw missie om cijfers en informatie te vertalen naar verhalen die impact maken. Je bent analytisch sterk, communicatief vaardig en kunt schakelen tussen business en techniek. Je vindt het leuk om samen te werken met collega’s en stakeholders en voelt je thuis in een omgeving waar data centraal staat.


Wat kan je van ons verwachten?

Bij CIMSOLUTIONS krijg je de kans om te werken aan uitdagende en innovatieve data- en BI-projecten bij toonaangevende organisaties. Wij investeren in jouw ontwikkeling en bieden naast een inspirerende werkomgeving:

  • Een vast dienstverband voor onbepaalde tijd, een max. salaris van € 6.000, bonusregeling, leaseauto, pensioenregeling en maandelijkse onkostenvergoeding;
  • Opdrachtkeuzevrijheid: Jij bepaalt in overleg waar je aan werkt, met de begeleiding van een personal coach;
  • Per jaar € 5.000,- opleidingsbudget en daarnaast ook nog eens 5-10 dagen studietijd;
  • Kennissessies met vakgenoten en inhoudelijke events;
  • Minimaal 25 vakantiedagen, met de mogelijkheid om extra dagen op te bouwen of te verkopen;
  • Sociale events zoals sportdagen, weekend weg en gezellige borrels;
  • Een vestiging bij jou in de buurt.


Persoonlijke ontwikkeling

Bij CIMSOLUTIONS geloven we dat leren de sleutel is tot succes. Learn, create and make it work is niet alleen onze slogan, maar zit in ons DNA. Daarom investeren we continu in jouw groei. Je krijgt de ruimte om jezelf te ontwikkelen tot bijvoorbeeld Senior Data Analist, BI Architect of Data Platform Specialist. Je wordt begeleid door ervaren collega’s, krijgt toegang tot kennisplatforms en blijft altijd scherp op de nieuwste ontwikkelingen binnen jouw vakgebied.


CIMSOLUTIONS heeft een actief diversiteitsbeleid en streeft naar een evenwichtige verdeling van professionals naar onder andere leeftijd, gender, etnische achtergrond en cultuur, arbeidsvermogen, kennis en competenties. Daarbij benadrukken wij dat gelijke kansen voor iedereen een belangrijk uitgangspunt zijn in onze selectie.


Wat breng jij mee?

  • Een afgeronde hbo‑ of wo‑opleiding in Bedrijfskunde, Informatiekunde, Business Analytics of vergelijkbaar;
  • Minimaal 4 jaar aantoonbare ervaring in een Agile omgeving met het analyseren en interpreteren van data en het vertalen van inzichten naar concrete adviezen;
  • Vaardig in het ontwerpen van dashboards en visualisaties die inzichten helder presenteren met tools zoals PowerBi, Qlik of Tableau;
  • Sterke kennis van en ervaring met SQL en/of Databricks voor analyseren van grote datasets;
  • Kennis van actuele ontwikkelingen op het gebied van data-analyse, data-ethiek en relevante wet- en regelgeving;
  • Uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden, zowel mondeling als schriftelijk;
  • AI-affiniteit is een pré.


Waarom CIMSOLUTIONS?

  • 15 keer Top Employer!
  • Uitstekend salaris;
  • Vrije keuze leaseauto;
  • € 5000,- opleidingsbudget;
  • Aantrekkelijke bonussen;
  • Veel sociale evenementen;
  • Uitdagende projecten;
  • Gevarieerde opdrachten;
  • Gerenommeerde klanten.


Heb je interesse?

Wij vinden het leuk om op ons hoofdkantoor of één van onze lokale vestigingen of online kennis te maken. Solliciteer via onderstaande button of neem voor meer informatie contact op met ons recruitment team via:

  • Mail: recruitment@cimsolutions.nl;
  • Telefoon: 0499-366 000;
  • of bezoek onze website: www.cimsolutions.nl/werken-bij/

Title: BI Specialist / Data Analyst
Description:
Epassi’s purpose is to boost everyday well-being.

We are a leading provider of employee benefit solutions in Europe. We were established in Finland in 2007, and in 2008 we were the first company to launch a mobile-payable employee benefit payment solution in Europe. Since then, we have consistently grown, diversifying our products and introducing our services into new markets. Epassi has been awarded by the Financial Times as one of the fastest-growing companies in Europe on multiple occasions.

Epassi's purpose is to boost everyday wellbeing. We are a leading provider of employee benefit solutions across Europe, established in Finland in 2007. We were the first company in Europe to launch a mobile-payable employee benefit payment solution in 2008, and have consistently grown since — diversifying our products and entering new markets. Epassi has been recognised by the Financial Times as one of Europe's fastest-growing companies on multiple occasions.

We are currently hiring a BI Specialist / Data Analyst to be based in Amsterdam. In this role, you will translate complex data into actionable business insights that drive strategic decision-making across the organisation. You'll design, develop, and maintain reporting solutions and dashboards, working closely with stakeholders across departments to ensure data accuracy, accessibility, and relevance. You act as the bridge between data and actionable insights — based in Amsterdam in hybrid setup (3 days in office, 2 days remote).

Responsibilities


Design, develop and maintain BI dashboards, reports and data models in Power BI, connecting to data in Databricks.
Translate business requirements into analytical solutions and data products.
Ensure data integrity, consistency and governance across all reporting layers.
Act as a trusted data advisor to business stakeholders across departments.
Collect, clean and transform data from multiple sources using SQL and ETL tools.
Build and maintain automated reports and interactive dashboards for management and operational teams.
Conduct ad-hoc data analyses to support business questions and strategic initiatives.
Collaborate with IT and data engineering on data warehouse and pipeline improvements.
Document data definitions, logic and methodologies to support data literacy across the organisation.


Requirements


Minimum 3-5 years of hands-on experience in a BI or data analyst role, ideally within a SaaS/tech organisation.
Advanced Power BI skills, including DAX, data modelling and report design.
Strong hands-on expertise in SQL - several years of experience with the tool.
Experience with ETL/ELT pipelines and data warehousing concepts.
Demonstrated ability to turn raw data into business-ready insights and communicate findings to non-technical audiences.
Proven track record of building dashboards and data models used in production.
Experience working with cross-functional stakeholders and translating business needs into data requirements.
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Business Intelligence, Information Management, Computer Science or a related field.
English — professional working proficiency (required). Dutch — advantageous but not mandatory.


Preferred


Microsoft Power BI certification (PL-300).
Experience working with Databricks as a data platform.


Your Track Record


You have owned analytics for a business area or market.
You have delivered insights that influenced real decisions.
You have built reporting trusted by senior stakeholders.
You have worked long enough in roles to develop and improve analytics, not just deliver quick outputs.


Key competencies


Analytical mindset: You approach problems with structure and attention to detail, turning ambiguous data challenges into clear, actionable conclusions.
Communication: You present complex data findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences, making insights accessible and actionable.
Proactivity: You are curious and self-driven — able to spot opportunities in data without being asked, and take initiative to turn them into insights.
Collaboration: You are a team player who builds trust across departments, working seamlessly with IT, finance, operations and beyond.
Data ownership: You take responsibility for data quality, governance and documentation, ensuring the business can rely on the information you provide.


Why join us


Be part of an ambitious growth journey and join a global community of driven, bold, entrepreneurial Epassians.
Work with a clear purpose — boosting everyday wellbeing — and create a meaningful, positive impact on individuals and society.
Thrive in an open and inclusive culture with low hierarchy, built on respect, camaraderie, and shared success.
Grow personally and professionally in a supportive environment where employee wellbeing is a true priority.
Join a fast-growing tech company with a strong financial foundation, with real opportunities to make an impact.
Help shape our position as the number one player in Europe's employee benefit market.

Title: Senior Data Analyst

Senior Data Analyst

Amsterdam, Netherlands

Up to 100k annually




This is an opportunity to take on a highly visible Senior Data Analyst role where your insights will directly influence product, commercial strategy, and customer experience at scale. You will work with complex datasets and senior stakeholders, shaping decisions that impact millions of users across a leading digital platform.




The Company

They are a well-established digital platform business operating at significant scale across the Benelux region. With millions of monthly users, they connect consumers and businesses through a marketplace model while supporting sustainable and circular commerce. Their teams are cross-functional, data-driven, and focused on continuous product and commercial innovation. Analytics plays a central role in enabling smarter decisions across the organisation.




The Role

Partner with product, marketing, and commercial teams to translate business challenges into actionable analytical plans
Identify growth opportunities and risks through deep analysis of user behaviour, conversion, and revenue trends
Design and build dashboards that clearly communicate performance and key business metrics
Analyse large, complex datasets to uncover meaningful insights and inform decision-making
Influence stakeholders across all levels through clear storytelling and data-driven recommendations
Support experimentation and measurement frameworks to evaluate product and marketing initiatives
Collaborate with data and engineering teams to improve data quality, tracking, and metric definitions




Your Skills and Experience

Strong commercial experience in data analytics within a digital, product-led, or marketplace environment
Advanced SQL skills and confidence working with large-scale datasets
Experience using Python for analytical work, modelling, and automation
Hands-on experience with cloud data platforms such as BigQuery, Databricks, or AWS
Proficiency with BI tools such as Tableau or similar for building impactful dashboards
