Senior Data Analyst

Amsterdam, Netherlands

Up to 100k annually


This is an opportunity to take on a highly visible Senior Data Analyst role where your insights will directly influence product, commercial strategy, and customer experience at scale. You will work with complex datasets and senior stakeholders, shaping decisions that impact millions of users across a leading digital platform.


The Company

They are a well-established digital platform business operating at significant scale across the Benelux region. With millions of monthly users, they connect consumers and businesses through a marketplace model while supporting sustainable and circular commerce. Their teams are cross-functional, data-driven, and focused on continuous product and commercial innovation. Analytics plays a central role in enabling smarter decisions across the organisation.


The Role

  • Partner with product, marketing, and commercial teams to translate business challenges into actionable analytical plans
  • Identify growth opportunities and risks through deep analysis of user behaviour, conversion, and revenue trends
  • Design and build dashboards that clearly communicate performance and key business metrics
  • Analyse large, complex datasets to uncover meaningful insights and inform decision-making
  • Influence stakeholders across all levels through clear storytelling and data-driven recommendations
  • Support experimentation and measurement frameworks to evaluate product and marketing initiatives
  • Collaborate with data and engineering teams to improve data quality, tracking, and metric definitions


Your Skills and Experience

  • Strong commercial experience in data analytics within a digital, product-led, or marketplace environment
  • Advanced SQL skills and confidence working with large-scale datasets
  • Experience using Python for analytical work, modelling, and automation
  • Hands-on experience with cloud data platforms such as BigQuery, Databricks, or AWS
  • Proficiency with BI tools such as Tableau or similar for building impactful dashboards
  • Strong understanding of business metrics including conversion, retention, and customer lifetime value
  • Ability to translate ambiguous problems into clear analytical approaches
  • Confident communicator who can influence stakeholders and challenge thinking constructively


What They Offer

  • Competitive salary and benefits package
  • Opportunity to work on high-impact projects influencing product and commercial strategy
  • Collaborative, cross-functional working environment
  • Clear scope for career progression within a data-driven organisation
  • Exposure to large-scale data and modern data tooling


How to Apply

If you are interested in this Senior Data Analyst opportunity, please apply with your CV to be considered.

Title: Data Analist
HTML:
📊 Maak jij van data waardevolle inzichten?


Bij Vepco B.V. geloven we dat goede beslissingen beginnen met betrouwbare data. Daarom zijn we op zoek naar een Data Analist die onze organisatie helpt om data om te zetten in concrete inzichten en verbeteringen.


Ben jij analytisch sterk, werk je graag met dashboards en rapportages én wil je een directe bijdrage leveren aan de optimalisatie van bedrijfsprocessen? Dan maken we graag kennis met je.


Vepco is opgericht in 2009 en gevestigd op de Moerdijk. We laten containers soepel bewegen tussen haven en klant, zowel per weg als per boot. Dit doen we samen met ons zusterbedrijf Containerplein. Met de beste 24/7 service, eigen vloot en eigen personeel bieden we first-class (digitale) planning en flexibiliteit voor onze klanten. Onze ruggengraat is de strategische hub in Moerdijk, waar onze vloot is gestationeerd en waar we een flexibele buffer hebben voor op- en overslag. Dit vullen we aan met een strategische spot in Antwerpen voor lege containers en in Rotterdam voor volle containers.


Wat ga je doen?

Als Data Analist ben jij de schakel tussen data en de business. Je verzamelt, analyseert en interpreteert gegevens en vertaalt deze naar bruikbare informatie voor collega's en management.


Je houdt je onder andere bezig met:

• Het analyseren van bedrijfs- en procesdata;

• Het ontwikkelen en onderhouden van dashboards en rapportages;

• Het signaleren van trends, afwijkingen en verbetermogelijkheden;

• Het adviseren van collega's op basis van data-analyses;

• Het optimaliseren van processen door middel van data-gedreven inzichten;

• Het samenwerken met verschillende afdelingen om informatie toegankelijk en bruikbaar te maken.


Wie ben jij?

Je bent nieuwsgierig, analytisch en krijgt energie van het oplossen van complexe vraagstukken. Je ziet verbanden waar anderen alleen cijfers zien en weet jouw analyses helder over te brengen.


Daarnaast beschik je over:

• HBO werk- en denkniveau;

• Ervaring met data-analyse en dashboarding (bij voorkeur Power BI);

• Sterke communicatieve vaardigheden;

• Een proactieve en nauwkeurige werkhouding.


Wat bieden wij?

Bij Vepco kom je terecht in een groeiende organisatie waar jouw werk direct zichtbaar is. Je krijgt veel ruimte om mee te denken, initiatief te nemen en jezelf verder te ontwikkelen.


Je kunt rekenen op:

• Een uitdagende functie voor 40 uur per week;

• Een informele organisatie met korte lijnen;

• Veel eigen verantwoordelijkheid;

• Ruimte voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling;

• Passende arbeidsvoorwaarden;

• Een enthousiast team waarin samenwerken centraal staat.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Department

WDTech - R&D

We are Walker & Dunlop. We are one of the largest providers of capital to the commercial real estate industry, enabling real estate owners and operators to bring their visions of communities — where people live, work, shop, and play — to life. We are committed to creating meaningful social, environmental, and economic change in our communities.

Department Overview

WDTech is W&D’s in-house technology team – a group of collaborative and highly skilled technology professionals, all of whom are leading experts in real estate data, data science, and technology.

WDTech Engineering builds solutions that impact not only our products but also the people and processes across our organization. A commitment to innovation and a passion for disrupting the old-fashioned real estate industry are our highest priorities.

The Impact You Will Have

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will play a key role in delivering trusted data insights and analytics that support business and product decision-making across the organization. This role is responsible for analyzing complex datasets, developing reporting solutions and dashboards, and translating data into actionable insights that improve operational effectiveness and support strategic initiatives.

Working closely with Data Analysts, BI Analysts, Data Engineers, Data QA Engineers, Data Scientists, and Product Managers, you will help ensure data products are accurate, scalable, and aligned with evolving business needs as Walker & Dunlop continues to grow as a data-driven organization.

Primary Responsibilities


Analyze and curate data sources to support business and product stakeholders in making informed decisions.
Lead key components of the analytical lifecycle, including data exploration, analysis, reporting, and insight delivery.
Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for technical and non-technical audiences.
Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards, reports, and analytical solutions.
Perform data wrangling, transformation, and analysis using SQL and enterprise analytical databases.
Define and document datasets, business logic, and reporting definitions to support consistent data usage.
Partner with data and product teams to support data governance, data quality, and data management processes.
Analyze user engagement, operational, and product data to identify trends and opportunities for improvement.
Mentor junior analysts and promote best practices in analytics, visualization, and stakeholder collaboration.
Perform other duties as assigned


Education And Experience


Bachelor’s degree preferred, preferably in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Information Systems, Mathematics, or a related field.
6+ years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or analytics roles with demonstrated ownership of end-to-end analytical projects.
Strong experience working with enterprise analytical databases such as Snowflake, Redshift, BigQuery, or similar technologies.
Experience developing dashboards and reporting solutions using Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization tools.
Experience with SQL, data transformation, and analytical reporting workflows.
Experience with Python and geospatial analysis preferred.
Real estate, financial services, or related industry experience preferred.


Knowledge, Skills And Abilities


Deep expertise in SQL and data analysis techniques.
Strong proficiency with data visualization and reporting tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
Understanding of data modeling, ETL processes, and analytical best practices.
Ability to communicate technical concepts and analytical findings clearly to business and technical audiences.
Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
Ability to work collaboratively across technical and business teams in a fast-paced environment.
Familiarity with AI-enabled tools that support reporting, documentation, and analysis workflows preferred.
High level of accountability, attention to detail, and commitment to continuous improvement.
Ability to show ownership of your work, take on challenges and acknowledge growth opportunities, and demonstrate patience when learning new processes
Courtesy, respect, and thoughtfulness in teaming with colleagues and other stakeholders


What We Offer 


You will have the opportunity to accelerate in our rapidly growing organization.
Agile working environment with flexible working hours and location, career advancement, and competitive compensation package.
We are a family friendly company.
We arrange social activities to help our employees and families become familiar with each other and our culture.
In addition to other benefits, we offer a pension scheme and 100% complimentary and confidential mental health services.


EEO Statement

We are committed to equity in all steps of the recruitment and employment experience. We believe in equal access to opportunities in our workplace. We do not tolerate discrimination, including harassment, based on any characteristic protected by applicable law, such as race, color, national origin, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, sex, age, disability, veteran or military status, and genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We strive to be a safe place to ask questions, build professional relationships, and develop careers.

SPAM

Please be wary of recruitment scams. An indication of a scam might be a request for sensitive or bank information at the time of application or emails coming from a non walkerdunlop.com email address. Please call us at 301.215.5500, if you have any concerns about information requested during or after the application process.

Title: Data Analist / BI Specialist
HTML:
Als Data Analist / BI Specialist zorg jij ervoor dat organisaties hun data omzetten in waardevolle inzichten. Je analyseert complexe datasets, vertaalt deze naar heldere rapportages en dashboards en adviseert stakeholders over datagedreven beslissingen. Met moderne BI-tools zoals Power BI, Tableau of Qlik maak je informatie toegankelijk en begrijpelijk. Daarmee help je onze klanten – zoals de Belastingdienst, Gemeente Den Haag, Centraal Justitieel Incassobureau en VGZ – om hun beleid en processen te verbeteren en toekomstgericht te sturen.


Waarom past deze vacature bij jou?

Je hebt een passie voor data en ziet het als jouw missie om cijfers en informatie te vertalen naar verhalen die impact maken. Je bent analytisch sterk, communicatief vaardig en kunt schakelen tussen business en techniek. Je vindt het leuk om samen te werken met collega’s en stakeholders en voelt je thuis in een omgeving waar data centraal staat.


Wat kan je van ons verwachten?

Bij CIMSOLUTIONS krijg je de kans om te werken aan uitdagende en innovatieve data- en BI-projecten bij toonaangevende organisaties. Wij investeren in jouw ontwikkeling en bieden naast een inspirerende werkomgeving:

  • Een vast dienstverband voor onbepaalde tijd, een max. salaris van € 6.000, bonusregeling, leaseauto, pensioenregeling en maandelijkse onkostenvergoeding;
  • Opdrachtkeuzevrijheid: Jij bepaalt in overleg waar je aan werkt, met de begeleiding van een personal coach;
  • Per jaar € 5.000,- opleidingsbudget en daarnaast ook nog eens 5-10 dagen studietijd;
  • Kennissessies met vakgenoten en inhoudelijke events;
  • Minimaal 25 vakantiedagen, met de mogelijkheid om extra dagen op te bouwen of te verkopen;
  • Sociale events zoals sportdagen, weekend weg en gezellige borrels;
  • Een vestiging bij jou in de buurt.


Persoonlijke ontwikkeling

Bij CIMSOLUTIONS geloven we dat leren de sleutel is tot succes. Learn, create and make it work is niet alleen onze slogan, maar zit in ons DNA. Daarom investeren we continu in jouw groei. Je krijgt de ruimte om jezelf te ontwikkelen tot bijvoorbeeld Senior Data Analist, BI Architect of Data Platform Specialist. Je wordt begeleid door ervaren collega’s, krijgt toegang tot kennisplatforms en blijft altijd scherp op de nieuwste ontwikkelingen binnen jouw vakgebied.


CIMSOLUTIONS heeft een actief diversiteitsbeleid en streeft naar een evenwichtige verdeling van professionals naar onder andere leeftijd, gender, etnische achtergrond en cultuur, arbeidsvermogen, kennis en competenties. Daarbij benadrukken wij dat gelijke kansen voor iedereen een belangrijk uitgangspunt zijn in onze selectie.


Wat breng jij mee?

  • Een afgeronde hbo‑ of wo‑opleiding in Bedrijfskunde, Informatiekunde, Business Analytics of vergelijkbaar;
  • Minimaal 4 jaar aantoonbare ervaring in een Agile omgeving met het analyseren en interpreteren van data en het vertalen van inzichten naar concrete adviezen;
  • Vaardig in het ontwerpen van dashboards en visualisaties die inzichten helder presenteren met tools zoals PowerBi, Qlik of Tableau;
  • Sterke kennis van en ervaring met SQL en/of Databricks voor analyseren van grote datasets;
  • Kennis van actuele ontwikkelingen op het gebied van data-analyse, data-ethiek en relevante wet- en regelgeving;
  • Uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden, zowel mondeling als schriftelijk;
  • AI-affiniteit is een pré.


Waarom CIMSOLUTIONS?

  • 15 keer Top Employer!
  • Uitstekend salaris;
  • Vrije keuze leaseauto;
  • € 5000,- opleidingsbudget;
  • Aantrekkelijke bonussen;
  • Veel sociale evenementen;
  • Uitdagende projecten;
  • Gevarieerde opdrachten;
  • Gerenommeerde klanten.


Heb je interesse?

Wij vinden het leuk om op ons hoofdkantoor of één van onze lokale vestigingen of online kennis te maken. Solliciteer via onderstaande button of neem voor meer informatie contact op met ons recruitment team via:

  • Mail: recruitment@cimsolutions.nl;
  • Telefoon: 0499-366 000;
  • of bezoek onze website: www.cimsolutions.nl/werken-bij/

Title: Data Analist | Consultancy | Haarlem
Description:
Voor een consultancyorganisatie met een sterke focus op data en digitalisering zijn wij op zoek naar een Data Analist. In deze rol help je verschillende opdrachtgevers bij het omzetten van data naar waardevolle inzichten en stuurinformatie. Je werkt aan uiteenlopende vraagstukken binnen zowel commerciële als (semi)publieke organisaties.

Je bent verantwoordelijk voor het analyseren, structureren en visualiseren van data, waarbij je inzichten begrijpelijk maakt voor uiteenlopende stakeholders. Je schakelt veelvuldig met zowel business als IT en weet bruggen te slaan tussen techniek en besluitvorming.

Je komt terecht in een ambitieuze en informele werkomgeving waar samenwerking, kennisdeling en persoonlijke ontwikkeling centraal staan. Er is volop ruimte om initiatief te nemen en je verder te ontwikkelen binnen het data‑domein.

Jouw verantwoordelijkheden:

Analyseren en interpreteren van complexe datasets uit verschillende bronnen
Ontwikkelen van dashboards, rapportages en visualisaties
Vertalen van businessvraagstukken naar datagedreven inzichten
Adviseren van stakeholders over verbeteringen op basis van data
Verbeteren van datakwaliteit en optimaliseren van dataprocessen
Samenwerken met interne en externe stakeholders, waaronder IT-teams
Identificeren van kansen voor verdere datagedreven optimalisatie
Bijdragen aan de ontwikkeling van datamodellen en datastructuren

Jouw profiel:

Minimaal 2 jaar ervaring in een data- of analytics rol
Ervaring met SQL en datamanipulatie
Bekend met tools zoals Power BI, Tableau, Qlik of vergelijkbaar
Kennis van Python en/of R is een pré
Begrip van datamodellering, ETL-processen en datakwaliteit
Sterk analytisch vermogen en oog voor detail
Communicatief vaardig en in staat om complexe informatie helder over te brengen

Wat wij bieden:

Een salaris passend bij jouw ervaring
Maandelijkse bonus + jaarlijkse bonus
Auto van de zaak of mobiliteitsregeling
Flexibele werkvorm: thuis, bij de klant of op kantoor in Haarlem
Mogelijkheden voor certificeringen, opleidingen en doorgroeien
Maandelijkse activiteiten en een hecht teamgevoel

Interesse?

Wil je meer weten over deze rol of direct kennismaken? Neem contact op met Remy van Kesteren via +31 6 27 15 29 27 of stuur een e‑mail naar r.vankesteren@progressive-nl.com.


Let op: vacaturefraude




Helaas komt vacaturefraude steeds vaker voor. We waarschuwen je voor mogelijke misleiding:
* Wij zullen nooit via WhatsApp of in een videogesprek vragen om jouw persoonlijke gegevens (zoals een kopie van je ID, bankgegevens of BSN).
* Twijfel je over de echtheid van een vacature of contactpersoon? Neem dan altijd rechtstreeks contact met ons op via de officiële contactgegevens op onze website.

Important: job fraud

Unfortunately, job fraud is becoming more common. Beware of such scams:
* We will never ask for personal information (such as a copy of your ID, bank details, or social security number) via WhatsApp or during a video call.
* If you're unsure whether a vacancy or contact person is legitimate, please reach out to us directly using the official contact details on our website.

Title: AI Analyst
HTML:
About us

At Main Capital Partners, we believe in the power of smart, driven individuals to shape the future of software. With over €6.5 billion in assets under management, we are a strategic investor focused on innovative software companies across Benelux, DACH, Nordics, France, and the US. Our mission is to support sustainable growth in the software sector, one of the most dynamic and impactful industries today.

We’re proud of our strong track record and our reputation as one of Europe’s most distinctive and high-performing investors. With a portfolio of 50+ companies and over 150 add-on acquisitions, we’re growing fast—and so is our team of 80+ professionals across offices in The Hague, Antwerp, Düsseldorf, Stockholm, Paris, and our affiliated office in Boston.

Due to the increase in the number of companies in our portfolio and the growing importance of AI within the software landscape, we are further expanding the Performance Excellence team. We are therefore looking for an AI Analyst.


Your opportunity: AI Analyst

As an AI Analyst, you will work closely with the Product, Tech & AI Lead within Performance Excellence and support both portfolio companies and deal teams in identifying, evaluating, and executing AI initiatives that create measurable business impact.

You will help portfolio companies translate AI opportunities into concrete initiatives, ensuring that these projects are designed, implemented, and monitored with clear KPIs and measurable outcomes. By tracking key AI metrics and performance indicators, you help ensure that AI initiatives contribute directly to operational improvements, growth, or efficiency gains.

In addition, you will support investment teams during the evaluation of new investment opportunities by assessing AI capabilities, risks, and opportunities within potential target companies. You will help build a clear view of how AI is currently used, the value it can create, and any potential risks or limitations that should be considered before investment decisions are made.

From an investment perspective, you contribute to value creation and risk management by translating AI insights into actionable recommendations for both deal teams and portfolio company leadership.


Tasks include:

  • Support AI value creation initiatives across the portfolio by identifying, evaluating, and monitoring AI use cases within portfolio companies;
  • Help portfolio companies design and implement AI initiatives with clear objectives, measurable KPIs, and defined success metrics;
  • Monitor and track AI performance metrics to ensure initiatives deliver tangible business impact;
  • Collaborate with deal teams to assess AI opportunities and risks in potential investment targets during due diligence;
  • Analyze the AI maturity and data readiness of prospective and existing portfolio companies;
  • Support portfolio companies in identifying practical AI use cases that improve operations, products, or customer experience;
  • Contribute to the development of AI best practices, playbooks, and frameworks that can be reused across the portfolio;
  • Help translate AI insights into concrete improvement roadmaps and value creation plans;
  • Stay up to date with developments in AI technologies, tooling, and governance, and share relevant insights with portfolio companies and internal teams.


Why you would be a good fit for us:

  • You have completed a university education (WO) in Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Computer Science, (Technical) Business Analytics, or a similar field;
  • You enjoy working at the intersection of technology, data, AI, and business impact, translating AI opportunities into practical initiatives with measurable results;
  • You are comfortable operating on both a strategic and hands-on level: from identifying AI opportunities and defining KPIs to supporting implementation and monitoring impact;
  • You enjoy switching between AI strategy, data analytics, and investment perspectives, and you thrive in a dynamic environment where you can make a tangible impact;
  • You have 1–3 years of relevant experience, for example as an AI Analyst, Data Scientist, AI Consultant, Analytics Consultant, or in a similar role where you worked on AI or data-driven initiatives;
  • You have a solid understanding of AI and machine learning concepts, including model development, evaluation metrics, and how AI solutions create business value;
  • Experience with data analytics, experimentation, or monitoring performance metrics/KPIs for AI or data products is a strong plus;
  • You are comfortable analyzing AI capabilities, data maturity, and potential risks within organizations;
  • You have strong analytical and problem-solving skills and enjoy structuring complex technological topics into clear insights for business stakeholders;
  • You have strong social and communication skills and integrity, and you enjoy working closely with deal teams and management teams;
  • Excellent command of English and Dutch, both spoken and written, is required. German or French is a plus.


Why work with us:

  • Entrepreneurial, fun, young, and dedicated team within an informal and open corporate culture;
  • Gain experience, knowledge, and insight into a leading sector-focused investment fund in the fast-growing software sector;
  • Get involved in dynamic and interesting projects quickly;
  • Many responsibilities early on;
  • A steep learning curve and career progression;
  • Competitive benefits package, including (healthy) lunches every day and a generous Company Fitness plan.

Title: Business Data Analist — regio Amsterdam (32–40 uur)
Description:
Deze positie vergt vloeiend Nederlands.




Voor een snelgroeiende organisatie in gezond werken, waar meerdere bedrijven samensmelten tot één geheel. Die groei vraagt om inzicht. Daarom zoeken wij namens hen de allereerste Business Data Analist: een bouwrol, geen beheerrol.




Jouw rol

Je start met een schone lei. Geen bestaand dashboard om te onderhouden, geen definities om te erven. Jij bepaalt wat er gemeten wordt, hoe het heet en hoe het eruitziet. Het datawarehouse wordt door een externe partij gebouwd; jij bepaalt wat erin gaat en wat eruit komt. Je werk landt direct op de directietafel en je schakelt dagelijks met teamleads, management en directie.




Wat ga je doen

Eén managementdashboard neerzetten dat organisatiebreed gebruikt wordt
Een KPI-framework ontwikkelen met eenduidige definities
Rendement inzichtelijk maken per klant, regio en dienst
Datakwaliteit aantoonbaar verbeteren en registraties aanscherpen
Cijfers vertalen naar adviezen waar het management op stuurt




Wat bieden wij

Salaris tot circa 6.000 bruto per maand op basis van 40 uur
Dienstverband van 32 tot 40 uur, hybride met veel flexibiliteit
Een organisatie in opbouw waar jouw keuzes jaren meegaan
Korte lijnen en directe samenwerking met management en directie




Wie ben jij

Minimaal drie jaar ervaring met data-analyse en BI-tooling, bijvoorbeeld Power BI
HBO of WO werk- en denkniveau
Uitstekende beheersing van het Nederlands
Je durft door te vragen tot het klopt en vertelt met data een verhaal dat de business begrijpt




Interesse

We bespreken graag met je of deze rol bij je past. Solliciteer direct of mail naar hello@useforce.ai

Title: Data engineer
Description:
Krijg jij energie van data die je van ruwe bron tot waardevol inzicht brengt? Zie je een dashboard niet als eindstation, maar als startpunt voor betere beslissingen?




Over de functie

Als Data Engineer / BI Specialist help je klanten om data om te zetten in waardevolle inzichten. Je ontsluit data uit verschillende systemen, bouwt datamodellen en ontwikkelt dashboards en rapportages. Je bent betrokken bij het hele proces: van het verzamelen en modelleren van data tot het opleveren van inzichten voor de eindgebruiker.




Steeds vaker begeleiden jullie klanten van brondata tot rapportage. Hiervoor wordt Microsoft Fabric als centraal dataplatform ingezet. Dit platform is volop in ontwikkeling, waardoor je veel invloed hebt op de inrichting en verdere uitbouw. De focus ligt voornamelijk op de back-end. Heb je daarnaast interesse in Power BI en dashboarding, dan is dat een mooie pré.




Jouw werkzaamheden:

Ontsluiten en modelleren van data uit verschillende bronnen.
Ontwikkelen van ETL-processen en dataoplossingen.
Bouwen en optimaliseren van BI-dashboards en rapportages.
Vertalen van businesswensen naar technische oplossingen.
Signaleren van verbeterkansen en meedenken over de BI-roadmap.
Samenwerken met collega's en klanten om de juiste inzichten te realiseren.




Over de organisatie

Deze organisatie helpt bedrijven hun processen slimmer en efficiënter in te richten met maatwerksoftware, Microsoft-technologie, AI en het Power Platform.Je komt terecht in een informele organisatie met korte lijnen, betrokken management en een hecht team.




Er is veel kennis aanwezig, collega's helpen elkaar graag en het personeelsverloop is laag. Daarnaast krijg je veel vrijheid, ruimte voor eigen initiatief en volop mogelijkheden om jezelf verder te ontwikkelen via opleidingen en certificeringen.




Wie ben jij

Hbo-opleiding, bij voorkeur ICT of Bedrijfskundige Informatica.
Minimaal 3 jaar ervaring met BI en data engineering.
Sterke communicatieve vaardigheden; consultant mindset is een pré.
Ervaring met Microsoft Fabric en Power BI is een pré.
Ervaring met Azure, Databricks of SQL (minimaal één vereist); bereid de overige technieken te leren.
Leergierig, flexibel en verantwoordelijk.




Aanbod

Salaris tot €7.000 bruto per maand.
Goede pensioenregeling.
Flexibele werktijden en thuiswerkmogelijkheden.
Bedrijfsfitness.
Dagelijks lunchbuffet en vers fruit.
Ruime opleidings- en certificeringsmogelijkheden.




Interesse?

Herken jij jezelf in bovenstaande beschrijving en ben je klaar voor een nieuwe stap in je carrière? Solliciteer dan direct! Stuur je cv naar ons toe, dan nemen we snel contact met je op.

Title: Business Insights Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Shape the future with data!

We are building a truly insight-driven organization where data and AI power smarter decisions across the business. Join our team to turn complex data into meaningful insights and help drive impact for patients, customers and the organization. Join us as a

Business Insights Analyst

Welcome in our team

Our Data & AI team is at the heart of a leading global pharmaceutical company’s new Business & Commercial Operations organization, enabling the business to become truly insight-driven and future-ready. We bring together data, advanced analytics, and AI capabilities to transform complex information into actionable insights that directly shape business strategy and decision-making.

About The Role

As a Business Insights Analyst, you will play a pivotal role in this transformation, partnering closely with cross-functional teams to unlock the full value of data in a highly dynamic and regulated pharmaceutical environment. From commercial performance to customer and patient understanding, you will help translate complex healthcare and market data into clear, meaningful insights that drive impact across our organization.

You will join a dynamic and collaborative team that combines deep analytical expertise, strong business understanding and an innovative mindset, working at the intersection of data, technology and business to accelerate growth, support better decision-making and ultimately improve patient outcomes. This role requires close collaboration with internal stakeholders across commercial, digital, medical, market access, finance and operations teams, as well as coordination with external research and data partners where applicable.

Tasks And Responsibilities Include But Are Not Limited To


Generate actionable insights from internal and external data sources to support strategic planning and operational decision-making.
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional teams by identifying trends, risks and opportunities.
Deliver integrated insights into marketing effectiveness, brand performance, sales force effectiveness and omnichannel engagement.
Translate complex analyses into clear recommendations for non-technical stakeholders through dashboards, reports and presentations.
Contribute to forecasting accuracy and business planning through trend analysis, predictive modeling and forward-looking insights.
Provide insights into customer, patient and health pathways to identify opportunities to improve engagement, experience and outcomes.
Lead or support qualitative and quantitative market research and tracking initiatives to deepen customer understanding.
Support experimentation and learning by evaluating initiatives, communicating findings and recommending actions to stakeholders.


Your profile


Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Statistics, Data Science or a related field.
Minimum 3-5 years’ experience in business analysis, data analysis or a similar role.
Fluent Dutch and English.
Proficiency with data analysis and visualization tools such as SQL, Power BI, Excel, Qlik Sense or Tableau.
Knowledge of statistical analysis, with Python or R considered a strong advantage.
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail, with the ability to organize, interpret and communicate complex data accurately.
Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to translate insights for non-technical audiences.
Experience working with cross-functional teams and managing multiple stakeholders effectively.
Ability to turn data into clear, business-relevant insights and recommendations; experience with market research, behavioral science and data privacy/governance is an advantage.


What We Offer

We welcome you to a truly global, dynamic and challenging environment with great opportunities for personal development. Our benefits depend on the job location but are in any case very competitive.

For Questions About This Vacancy

Please send an email including the vacancy number and job title to the following email address  RecruitmentNL@MSD.com

Required Skills

Actionable Insights, Business Insights, Business Strategies, Change Management, Data Analysis, Data Analysis Tools, Data-Driven Decision Making, Data Science, Information Technology Applications, Market Research

Preferred Skills

Statistical Analysis

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Data Engineer




Job Description

As a Data Engineer at MANUS, you will work together with a bright, multidisciplinary team of engineers to build a robust data foundation for the future of robotics and AI. Our goal is to facilitate the collection of high-quality, multimodal datasets to train embodied AI: AI that interacts with and understands the physical world.

In this role, you are the subject matter expert on the team regarding data orchestration and infrastructure. You will work closely with our Embodied AI Engineers to determine what data needs to be collected: ranging from video and audio to various sensor streams: and how it should be structured to be "AI-ready." Your focus will be on the end-to-end data lifecycle: from orchestrating local collection at the source to managing the efficient transition and storage of that data in the cloud. You have full responsibility for your own projects and are expected to work independently.




Your Responsibilities

Multimodal Data Orchestration: Acting as the team’s authority on capturing diverse data types (video, sensors, etc.) and ensuring they are perfectly structured for machine learning training loops.
Pipeline Management: Designing and maintaining the pipelines that manage local data collection and the subsequent upload and ingestion into our cloud ecosystem.
Cloud Architecture: Building and maintaining scalable cloud environments (AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure) optimized for storing and processing massive, multi-modal datasets.
Synchronization & Alignment: Solving the complex challenge of time-syncing video frames with high-frequency sensor data to ensure "frame-perfect" training sets.
Collaboration: Working side-by-side with AI engineers to align data collection strategies with model requirements.




Our Ideal Candidate

Strong background in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in a data-focused or backend role.
Advanced proficiency in Python and SQL.
Experience handling multimodal data: such as video, audio, or time-series sensor data.
Deep expertise in architecting cloud-based data storage and processing solutions.
Excellent communication skills: you can bridge the gap between technical data requirements and high-level AI goals.
Excellent proficiency in both spoken and written English.




A Plus Would Be

Experience with Robotics (ROS/ROS2) or AI training pipelines.
Knowledge of local data capture systems and edge-to-cloud synchronization.
Familiarity with containerization (Docker, Kubernetes) and Terraform.
Experience with Git, Jira, and Confluence.




What do we offer

€5.000 - €6.200 per month, depending on skill level
27 vacation days based on a full-time working week (40 hours).
Holiday allowance of 8%
Modern pension.
A warm, informal workspace within a growing organisation
Working in a technical environment with great products and talented colleagues.
You can join our cooking club every Thursday for lunch + playing card or board games.




Who are we?

At MANUS, we’re redefining what’s possible with human-machine interaction. Our next-gen data gloves deliver ultra-precise, drift-free, and occlusion-free hand and finger tracking — capturing even the most subtle movements in real time.

Powered by an advanced EMF tracking system, our technology eliminates common issues like sensor drift and positional offset. That means seamless control, accurate spatial alignment, and zero guesswork — whether you're piloting a robot or training AI.

Our tracking data sets the gold standard for embodied AI. By giving machines access to human-level dexterity, we're enabling them to move, grasp, and interact with the world as naturally as we do.

From robotics and AI to VR, entertainment, and advanced research — the world’s most innovative companies trust MANUS to help build the next generation of intelligent systems. Our technology is trusted by 2,000+ leading research institutions and technology companies worldwide. We are also officially integrated into NVIDIA Isaac Lab as a native teleoperation input for high-fidelity manipulation, placing MANUS at the foundation of the embodied AI development stack.




Are you excited about this vacancy? Get in touch with us!

Title: Senior Data Science Consultant
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.





Job Description



To support its development, Sia is recruiting senior consultants within its data science team whose vocation will be to take charge, in synergy with our different practices, of the work and missions requiring the implementation of complex models. to support our clients in their issues related to:



Machine learning and statistics : development of explanatory and predictive decision support models (supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms, econometrics, forecasting, quantitative diagnosis, etc.)
Operational research : design and implementation of optimization solutions under constraints and simulation models
Big Data : algorithmic knowledge for processing large volumes of unstructured data

As part of your operational interventions, and your contributions to the internal development of the firm, you:



Develop and strengthen our offers through training, working groups, dissemination of internal and external media
Participate in the firm's influence by actively contributing to the publication plan (articles, studies, etc.), and represent the firm at various professional clubs, exhibitions and events.
Take part in the development of the activity through the design and development of commercial proposals, as well as by identifying new opportunities for collaboration with our clients.
Manage end-to-end projects , on which you ensure regular reporting to the attention of the manager and the client in order to guarantee the good respect of our commitments.
Manage the customer relationship on your assignments and build a solid relationship with your client side contacts, at middle management level, who recognize you as an expert on the subjects dealt with.
Supervise consultants , juniors to confirmed and fulfill the role of referent and relay of the manager for the follow-up of the sites, the validation of the deliverables, the evaluation of the achievements, and above all you ensure the development of their soft and hard skills.


Qualifications


You have a master degree or phd in a quantitative field (e.g. computer science, econometrics, (bio-)statistics, operations research) and you have at least 3 years of relevant experience on data science subjects.
You have an excellent level in statistics and quantitative analysis (theoretical and practical knowledge, intuition, ease of understanding models and applying them)
You master programming in Python and have a solid foundation in scripting languages (R, matlab, JS, bash) or compiled (C ++, Java) and / or have experience in environments UNIX / LINU

We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.



Job Description

To support its development, Sia is recruiting senior consultants within its data science team whose vocation will be to take charge, in synergy with our different practices, of the work and missions requiring the implementation of complex models. to support our clients in their issues related to:

Title: Data Analyst project BEL, 4 months
Description:
Introduction

Do you want to contribute to a big data research on the societal benefits of energy retrofits in homes? Energy transition in Dutch social housing involves retrofitting millions of dwellings in coming years. The aim is to contribute to the climate goals and –increasingly important– to reduce energy poverty and improve the health of the residents. The effectiveness of energy retrofits in achieving these goals however strongly depends on tenants’ behavioural responses.

Our multidisciplinary project BEL provides detailed insight in these responses to support acceleration of the energy transition. We combine big data analysis with microeconomic behavioural modelling, econometrics, machine learning and natural language processing. We exploit longitudinal 2014-2021 data of 1 million social houses and households (40% of the social sector).

Job Description

This is a four-month position. The Data analyst will contribute to training existing econometric models in R and Python and running scenarios, as well as preparing new data to be used in a PhD project that will start in 2027. See www.bel-tue.nl for more information about the project.

Job Requirements


MSc degree in exact or computer sciences.
Experience with training econometric models on big data, experience with working with Big data of CBS.
Proficiency in R and Python.
Affinity with energy efficiency topics and behavioural microeconomic modelling.


Conditions of Employment

An Exciting Position Within An International Yet Personal University. You Are Right In The Middle Of The Students, On a Green Campus Within Walking Distance Of The Central Station. Besides Beautiful Architecture, You Will Find Varied Workplaces And Excellent Sports Facilities. We Also Offer You


A monthly salary of minimum € 3,706 to maximum € 5,760 gross for full-time employment, depending on your knowledge and experience (scale 10 Collective Labour Agreement for Dutch Universities).
In addition to vacation pay, a structural end-of-year bonus of 8.3%.
A favorable arrangement for more holidays or a sabbatical.
A selection model for additional fringe benefits.
Working hours in consultation for an optimal work-life balance.
Scope for your talent with advancement prospects and excellent development opportunities such as mentoring, workshops and coaching.
Partially paid parental leave and reimbursement for commuting expenses, working from home and the internet.
A generous employer contribution to the favorable ABP pension plan.
Unlimited access to the modern on‑campus TU/e Student Sports Center at an exceptionally affordable rate.


Here you can discover even more information about our conditions of employment. Build on your career at TU/e!

About Us

We are a leading international university where scientific curiosity meets a hands-on mindset. We work in an open and collaborative way with high-tech industries to tackle complex societal challenges. Our responsible and respectful approach ensures impact — today and in the future. TU/e is home to over 13,000 students and more than 7,000 staff, forming a diverse and vibrant academic community.

Our university is located in Brainport Eindhoven — a world‑leading tech region with more than 7,000 high‑tech companies and strong R&D activity. Known for breakthroughs in AI, photonics, semiconductors and advanced manufacturing, Brainport is a place where technology serves people and society. Learn more about the Brainport region here.

Information

Do you recognize yourself in this profile and would you like to have more information about the position, please contact the hiring manager: Ioulia Ossokina, Associate Professor TU/e, i.v.ossokina@tue.nl.

Visit our website for more information about the application process. You can also contact Leo van Houten, HR-Advisor, l.v.houten@tue.nl or +31 40 247 8269.

Are you inspired and would like to know more about working at TU/e? Please visit our career page.

Application

If you are interested, please use the ‘apply’ button to send us your CV and letter of application with your motivation and a description of your qualifications. Ensure that you submit all the requested application documents. We give priority to complete applications.

Screening of candidates begins as soon as applications are received and continues until the position is filled. Where applicable, internal candidates will be given priority over external candidates where they are equally suitable.

We look forward to receiving your application!

Please note


You can apply online. We will not process applications sent by email and/or post.
A pre-employment screening (e.g. knowledge security check) can be part of the selection procedure. For more information on the knowledge security check, please consult the National Knowledge Security Guidelines.
Important for non-EU applicants: Please be aware that for this position, specific residence permit requirements apply. If you are a non-EU national, you may not be eligible to legally work in this role under current Dutch immigration regulations. We strongly advise you to contact our Staff Immigration Team (staffimmigration@tue.nl) before applying to check your eligibility and to receive further guidance.
Please do not contact us for unsolicited services.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Want to influence the bigger picture? At Saab, you can realise amazing innovations that keep people and societies safe. Together, we can truly make an impact – and your part matters.

Your role

Operational Development within Procurement, SAAB Surveillance, is in a strong transformation phase where data, analytics, and digital tools are key enablers to improve efficiency, quality, and performance across procurement operations. We are now looking for a Data Analyst, who will be part of the Operational Development team supporting Procurement in Surveillance.

In this role, you will work closely with procurement operations, strategic buyers, and operational procurement teams, as well as cross-functional stakeholders, to transform data into actionable insights that drive operational improvements, process efficiency, and better decision-making. You will collect, structure, analyze, and interpret data from ERP and procurement-related systems to identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, trends, and improvement opportunities across procurement processes, supplier interactions, and operational workflows. You will also develop and maintain analytical solutions using SQL and BI tools such as QlikSense or similar platforms, enabling transparency and supporting fact-based decision-making. The role combines hands-on analytics with strong collaboration in a highly operational environment. You will be part of a team where collaboration, curiosity, and delivery focus are central. We value openness, continuous learning, and a strong delivery mindset, where everyone contributes to driving real operational change.

This position can be based in Solna or Gothenburg.

Main responsibilities:


Drive data-driven operational improvement initiatives within Procurement Operations, SAAB Surveillance
Analyze operational procurement data to identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, trends, and improvement opportunities
Work closely with procurement operations teams to understand processes, performance, and daily challenges
Support operational development initiatives through data analysis and insight generation
Build dashboards and reporting solutions using QlikSense or similar BI tools
Extract, transform, and analyze data using SQL, primarily from ERP systems
Develop KPIs and performance metrics to support operational excellence in procurement
Communicate insights, findings, and recommendations to operational and cross-functional stakeholders
Contribute to digitalization, automation, and process improvement initiatives within procurement operations
Continuously identify best practices and translate them into practical operational improvements



Your profile

We're looking for a structured, analytical, and hands-on individual who thrives in an operational environment. You are curious, proactive, and motivated by improving how things actually work in practice, not only in theory. You have a strong "do-it attitude" and enjoy solving real operational problems through data. With solid technical skills and strategic thinking, you're comfortable diving into complex challenges and translating them into clear, actionable insights. You take initiative without waiting for direction, and you're not afraid to roll up your sleeves and get things done. You work naturally close to the business, engaging with stakeholders across functions and turning technical knowledge into practical solutions. You're collaborative, communicative, and able to navigate a dynamic environment where priorities shift quickly. You enjoy change and actively contribute to improving processes, tools, and ways of working, combining your technical expertise with strategic perspective to drive real impact.

Qualifications:


Master of Science (MSc) in Industrial Engineering, Industrial Economics, Mechanical Engineering, Data Science, Information Systems, or similar
Experience from manufacturing, industrial operations, procurement operations, or supply chain environments
Strong proficiency in SQL, Excel, and data analysis
Experience with BI tools such as QlikSense, Power BI, Tableau, or similar
Good understanding of operational procurement processes and supply chain workflows
Experience working with ERP systems (preferably IFS)
Ability to handle, structure, and analyze large and complex datasets
Strong communication skills in both English and Swedish
Several years of relevant experience within manufacturing, procurement, or supply chain is required



Meriting experience:


Experience with operational excellence, Lean, or continuous improvement methodologies
Experience with process automation tools
Experience with AI tools and business adoption in industrial settings
Data modeling or advanced analytics experience in operational contexts



This position requires that you pass a security vetting based on the current regulations around/of security protection. For positions requiring security clearance additional obligations on citizenship may apply.

What you will be part of

Explore a wealth of possibilities. Take on challenges, create smart inventions, and grow beyond. This is a place for curious minds, brave pioneers, and everyone in between. Together, we achieve the extraordinary, each bringing our unique perspectives. Your part matters.

Saab is a leading defense and security company with an enduring purpose, to help nations keep their people and society safe. Empowered by its 28,000 talented people, Saab constantly pushes the boundaries of technology to create a safer and more sustainable world.

Saab designs, manufactures and maintains advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors and underwater systems. Saab is headquartered in Sweden. It has major operations all over the world and is part of the domestic defense capability of several nations. Read more about us here

Title: Data Analyst, Controlling
Description:
We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our team in Packaging Solutions’ BU Northern Europe Controlling team. This role is part of our ambition to build a modern, AI-enabled analytics capability where data analysts act as proactive partners in business decision-making.

Hybrid role, with location in Jönköping, Stockholm, Lahti, Helsinki, or Riga.

Your role

As a Data Analyst, you focus on turning data into profitability-improving actions. You work closely with controllers and business stakeholders to analyze performance, identify improvement opportunities, and support decisions related to pricing, cost optimization, and resource allocation.

This is not a back-office reporting role. You are expected to actively engage with stakeholders, ask the right questions, and continuously improve how data and analysis are used to support profitable business outcomes.

The Data Analyst reports to the Head of Controlling, BU Northern Europe, Packaging Solutions.

Key Responsibilities


Analyst is expected to design structured datasets and models together with IT, not just use them
Analyze financial and business data to identify trends, drivers, and deviations
Explore and apply AI and advanced analytics tools to improve efficiency, quality, and insight
Ensure data quality, consistency, and “single source of truth” across reports and forward looking analyses
Build, maintain, and continuously improve reports and dashboards for decision-making
Combine and structure data from multiple systems and sources
Collaborate closely with controllers and business stakeholders to understand their needs and translate them into meaningful analysis
Support controllers with deeper analysis, scenario modelling, and ad hoc business questions
Communicate insights clearly and confidently to non-financial stakeholders
Identify opportunities to automate recurring analyses and reporting



What You Bring


Relevant academic background (e.g. finance, economics, data, engineering, or similar)
Initial experience and/or strong interest in data analysis, reporting, or finance, with practical experience or curiosity towards modern AI tools
Experience with BI tools (e.g. Power BI), Excel, and data handling
Competence in AI-assisted analytics and vibe coding, enabling fast experimentation, insight generation, and data tool development, with a strong foundation in SQL and Python
Ability to interact confidently with controllers and business stakeholders, and explain data-driven insights in a clear and structured way
Capability and proof of use-cases with AI, automation, and new analytical tools
Analytical mindset with a willingness to challenge numbers and ask “why”
Structured, proactive, and collaborative way of working
Fluency in English



What We Offer

You will be part of our diverse working culture which supports you in exploring new perspectives and developing professionally. You will contribute to a more sustainable world, as we believe that anything made from fossil-based materials today can be made from a tree tomorrow. Sustainability and innovation are at the core of our strategy. To keep you going safely, we support your physical and mental wellbeing.

How can you apply for this role?

You can only submit your application via our recruitment tool. We hope to hear from you as soon as possible, but no later than 17 of August, 2026 and please send your application in English. Depending on the role, we might invite you to a video interview and/or aptitude tests.

If you have questions about the position, please contact the hiring manager Sami Muhonen, sami.muhonen@storaenso.com and questions about the recruitment process can be directed to our Talent Acquisition Partner Veera Kaislasalo at veera.kaislasalo@storaenso.com.

As it’s the holiday season, our process may be a bit slower than usual. We’ll make sure to get back to you with your questions by August at the latest.

Welcome to the The renewable materials company!

Stora Enso is a global leader in renewable materials with a strong focus on packaging. Our purpose is to replace non-renewable materials with renewable solutions. Together with our customers, we design and deliver competitive, high-quality packaging materials and solutions, made from fresh and recycled fibers, accelerating the transition to a circular bioeconomy. Stora Enso has approximately 19,000 employees and our sales in 2025 were EUR 9.3 billion.

We believe that diversity strengthens our competitiveness and leads to better decision‑making. Diverse teams with different backgrounds help us explore new perspectives and challenge our ways of thinking. We actively work to increase diversity in the workplace and welcome applicants from all backgrounds. Stora Enso is an equal opportunity employer that follows non‑discriminatory practices.

Learn more about working in Stora Enso: Working at Stora Enso – Career for a better climate. Every day. - YouTube

Diverse teams with different backgrounds help us explore new perspectives and challenge our ways of thinking. We actively work to increase diversity in the workplace and welcome applicants from all backgrounds. Stora Enso is an equal opportunity employer that follows non‑discriminatory practices.

Learn more about working in Stora Enso: Working at Stora Enso – Career for a better climate. Every day. - YouTube

Title: Full-Time
Description:
We are the consultant company that travels beyond the speed of light to turn your data into possibilities. We know that the only way of discovering the limits of the possible, is to venture a little way past them into the impossible. That’s why we are leaders in Business Intelligence and Advanced Analytics. By always hunting down new knowledge and skills, we can pinpoint current competition in the always-evolving world of IT. We are driven by learning new technologies. That's how we help businesses, together with our fluency in complex IT technologies. A winning concept? Yes, if you want to communicate with both business and IT. Right now, we are looking for a Business Intelligence consultant who wants to use Qlik och Power BI as your right hand. Read more and apply today!

About The Role

Advanced data solutions are at the core of everything we do at SpaceBase. Our main goal is to find smart ways of using technology that will help build a better tomorrow for everyone, everywhere. As a consultant, you will open new galaxies for our clients. With Azure as your sidekick, you make sure that our customers understand their overall cloud readiness and support them through a structured learning plan to ensure state-of-the-art delivery.

We want all our clients to be more data-driven. You will help them do just that by developing deep relationships with our clients and voicing your knowledge by sharing insights and best practices. To help them reach their full potential, you will apply your technical expertise to architect solutions that meet business – and IT needs. This means creating a Data Platform, AA/AI roadmaps, and ensuring the long-term technical viability of new deployments. You will also infuse key analytics technologies (e.g. Azure Synapse, Azure Data Factory, Azure Data Lake, Databricks, Azure Data Explorer, Power BI, etc.) At Spacebase, the clients may vary, but they are equally important. That’s why you will pursue solutions in collaboration with different types of customers – in different industries – to reach set goals.

We are looking for you...


who would move mountains to learn something new. It may sound a little bit quirky, but you are a person who wants to stay up to date with new technologies and best practices. You're highly motivated, outgoing, flexible, and a fast learner who advocates for agile methodologies, data transparency, and collaboration. You are a high achiever and would work around the clock to achieve deadlines – if that means delivering solutions that would exceed our client's expectations.


As a Business Intelligence Consultant, you will be responsible for transforming raw data into valuable insights that guide business decisions. You’ll create dynamic dashboards and detailed reports, providing stakeholders with the information they need to make informed choices. Your expertise in data visualization will allow you to present complex data in a clear, intuitive way, helping clients understand key metrics and trends. You’ll work closely with clients to tailor your analyses to their specific needs, ensuring that they have the actionable intelligence required to drive their business forward.

Requirements


Develop and maintain interactive dashboards and detailed reports that provide actionable insights.
Collaborate with clients to understand their business objectives and reporting needs.
Utilize advanced data visualization techniques to present data in a clear and engaging way.
Perform data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for improvement.
Continuously optimize reporting processes to enhance data accuracy and efficiency.


Important To Have


Strong skills in SQL, Qlik and Power BI.
Proven experience in data visualization and report development.
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights.
Excellent communication skills to convey data-driven insights to non-technical stakeholders.


Why Spacebase?

With our creative minds and innovative veins, you will partner up with our clients in every step of their project. Do you want to plan a new data and analytics enterprise environment, present disruptive ideas, or fine-tune the last part of a project? Here, you can do it all – if you can do it with data. Let's make the world a better place by building a complex back-end and smooth, user-friendly front-end. Together we will use data as our new sunshine.

Apply

Title: Decision Analyst
Description:
About The Role

As a Decision Analyst at Instabee, you’ll play a key role in helping us make smarter, faster decisions across the business. You’ll partner with teams across the company from product, operations, to tech and beyond - to understand challenges, gather insights, and co-create solutions. Whether it’s structuring an ambiguous problem, evaluating new data sources, or communicating a compelling recommendation, you’ll bring clarity and impact. This role is perfect for someone who loves solving problems, is comfortable navigating complexity, and sees data as a powerful tool, not an end in itself.

Some of the things you’ll do


Tackle complex and ambiguous problems by bringing structure and taking clear, decisive action
Translate complex data and information into concise, actionable insights using both qualitative and quantitative methods
Collaborate across diverse teams to co-create smart solutions and drive real change
Communicate insights clearly and confidently to stakeholders at all levels, from junior teams to senior leadership
Continuously explore and integrate new internal and external data sources to strengthen our decision-making foundation
Analyze data from multiple sources to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities
Partner with teams to understand business challenges and build tailored tools or frameworks that improve how we work
Act as a go-to person for analytical support—sharing knowledge, guiding ad-hoc analysis, and elevating decision quality across the company


Your future team

You’ll join a tight-knit team working on Instabee’s most critical challenges. We collaborate across departments, drive clarity in ambiguity, and value real-world impact over theoretical perfection. Curiosity, humility, and a strong drive to make things better are what bring us together.

What We’re Looking For


Bachelor’s degree within engineering, information technology, finance, economics or other relevant field
Experience presenting solutions to senior management
Experience working within a data heavy environment for at least 4 years
Experience working with solving business problems and improving ways of working
Experience driving real impact, leading stakeholders to materialise the change that the insight points to
Experience using AI coding tools
Experience working with data processing tools including Front End visualization tools (e.g. Tableau, PowerBI, PowerPoint, Google Slides) and generic tools (e.g. SQL, Python, Excel/Google Sheets)


The cherry on top


You bring a strong blend of business and data understanding—and know how to connect the two
You’re curious, always learning, and stay on top of evolving best practices
You care about creating real-world results and know when “good enough” is enough
You’re confident challenging the status quo and backing it up with structured thinking
You communicate clearly and effectively in English—even when things get complex


If this is you, then we can’t wait to meet you!

What You’ll Get

We can promise you that you will be challenged, develop your skills and have the opportunity to work with truly amazing and competent colleagues. If you like change and want to set new processes and ways of working—this is the place for you!

Of course, we have all the necessary and standard benefits in place, but in addition to this, we also offer:

🌇 Sky-High Office: Modern and newly renovated with a 360-degree view around Stockholm

🐶 Puppy Friendly: Bring your dog to work—we’ve got a whole floor for our furry friends!

🏓 Fun & Games: Ping pong, shuffleboard, foosball—even a karaoke room!

👶 Parental Pay: Paid leave for six months to spend time with your little one

🎂 Birthday Day Off: Celebrate your birthday with a well-earned break

⏰ Flexible Hours: Hybrid setup and hours that work with your life

Get to know us

In 2022, Instabee was founded after combining Budbee and Instabox - two leading last-mile delivery companies with three consumer-facing brands: Budbee, Instabox, and Porterbuddy. Instabee serves millions of consumers across Europe with convenient deliveries and is on track to become the leading European e-commerce enabler. Always with the consumer at heart.

We’re fast, both in terms of parcel delivery and when it comes to growing our business. So far our journey has taken us from Sweden to the rest of Scandinavia and parts of continental Europe, but we have no plans on stopping anytime soon. As we continue to expand, the complexity of Instabee grows in parallel. With that in mind, we’re now looking for you to help us on the journey ahead.

This recruitment process consists of background checks via Svensk Bakgrundsanalys as a review of previous public criminal records. The result is public information that is handled confidentially. This is mandatory for all recruitment processes at Instabee in Sweden.

Please note that, due to the summer holiday period, we will begin reviewing applications in August. We appreciate your patience and will be in touch with all candidates once the recruitment process resumes.

Instabee Group, with consumer-facing brands Budbee, Instabox, and Porterbuddy, brings together years of experience in delivery. Today, Instabee Group is present in five markets, serving thousands of online merchants and millions of consumers with seamless home deliveries and convenient parcel locker deliveries, using the best fuel available. With smart technology, a strong focus on sustainability, and a secure supply chain, Instabee has become a trusted partner for online merchants and a preferred choice for consumers.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
At TUI, we're looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst to support the development and delivery of business intelligence solutions by gathering requirements, building reports and dashboards, and analyzing data from multiple sources, within the context of TUI's data transformation journey, so that business users across Markets & Airlines can access timely, accurate, and actionable insights to drive commercial decisions and improve business performance.

About Our Offer


Personal benefits: Attractive remuneration, discretionary bonus schemes, generous travel benefits, extensive health & well-being support, and more.
Flexible working: Work is something you do, not somewhere you go. We encourage a healthy work-life balance with a dynamic working environment.
A career to shape: Access the TUI Learning Hub to level-up and reach your ambitions.
Broaden your network: We champion intercultural collaboration and provide opportunities to work on global projects and teams.
Community: Get involved with incredible local charity and sustainability initiatives like the TUI Care Foundation.


About The Job


You'll work in close partnership with Business and Technical teams to analyze, design, and co-create new data products, visualizations, and reporting solutions that are standardized, scalable, and robust to support consistent insight delivery across the business.
You'll develop and maintain Tableau dashboards and reports that meet business requirements and adhere to TUI's design standards and best practices, ensuring standardized metrics and dimensions are used correctly and universally.
You'll write efficient queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from various source systems to support reporting needs across multiple markets.
You'll test and validate data accuracy in reports and dashboards, ensuring quality and consistency across reporting solutions that impact business decisions.
You'll support business users with report interpretation, providing training and guidance on how to effectively use BI tools and dashboards.
You'll collaborate with Data Engineers and Senior Business Intelligence Analysts to understand data structures and implement reporting solutions that align with the overall data architecture.
You'll identify, document and resolve issues with data quality or reporting functionality, proposing solutions and working with the team to implement improvements as part of continuous enhancement initiatives.


About You


You have a degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or Business Analytics, or equivalent experience in data analytics or business intelligence.
You're proficient in SQL with the ability to write and optimize queries for data extraction and analysis.
You have experience with visualization tools, particularly Tableau, including dashboard creation and report development.
You understand data warehouse concepts and dimensional modelling principles.
You can translate business requirements into technical specifications that meet user needs.
You have analytical and problem-solving abilities with attention to detail and commitment to data quality.
You communicate effectively, explaining technical concepts to non-technical users across various business functions.
Knowledge of additional BI tools beyond Tableau and experience with data visualization best practices would be beneficial.
Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP), version control systems, and data governance principles would be advantageous.
You work collaboratively in team environments, contributing to continuous improvement initiatives.


From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.

We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

Title: Data Scientist / Data Analyst - Automotive
Description:
WeQuel is looking for a Data Scientist / Data Analyst

We are currently seeking experienced Data Scientists and Data Analysts for assignments within the automotive industry in the Stockholm/Södertälje area. You will work in a data-driven environment where your analytical models and tools directly support strategic business decisions – from pricing and asset management to lifecycle planning for next-generation vehicle platforms.

You are someone who combines strong technical skills with a genuine curiosity for how data can solve real business problems, and you are comfortable working across both analytical and engineering disciplines.

We are looking for candidates with experience in one or more of the following:


Building analytical models that support business decisions, such as forecasting, pricing, or lifecycle planning
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL as core working tools
Developing dashboards, analytical prototypes, or APIs
Working with large datasets and translating findings into actionable insights
Collaborating with data engineering teams to ensure reliable and scalable outputs
Relevant degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, or Engineering


Meritorious:


Experience from the automotive industry
Background in residual value modelling, financial analytics, or asset management
Experience working in agile, cross-functional teams
Familiarity with cloud environments or BI tools such as Power BI or Snowflake


Location: Stockholm/Södertälje

Language requirements: Fluent English required, Swedish is a merit

What We Offer

At WeQuel, you will become part of a consulting team that puts people first. We believe that success is built through engagement, collaboration, and continuous development. We offer a flat organization with short decision-making processes, a strong sense of community, and the opportunity to actively shape your own professional growth.

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
We are Sweden's leading app and marketplace for booking beauty and wellness services. As a SaaS company with industry-leading software, we empower more than 16,000 professionals across the country. Our platform brings together nearly every salon and clinic in Sweden, making it easy for consumers to book appointments with hairdressers, massage therapists, podiatrists, beauty salons, chiropractors, and many other specialists—all in one place.

On Bokadirekt.se, consumers can discover, book, and pay for treatments and services in just a few clicks. Every month, our platform is visited by 3 million consumers, and more than 2 million bookings are made through our website and app. We’re growing rapidly and are currently a team of more than 100 employees, with offices in Stockholm and Luleå.

About The Role

We are looking for a Product Analyst to join our Data & Analytics team and serve as the dedicated analytics partner to our Product organisation.

As a Product Analyst, you will help our product teams make better decisions through data. You'll work closely with product managers, designers, and engineers to uncover user insights, measure product performance, and drive experimentation across the customer journey. You'll play a key role in establishing best practices for product analytics, event tracking, and experimentation while enabling teams to make confident, data-informed decisions.

Your insights will directly influence the experience of millions of consumers and thousands of professionals using Bokadirekt every month.

Beyond product analytics, we're looking for someone with an AI-native mindset—someone who knows how to leverage AI to work smarter while applying sound judgement to validate AI-generated insights and recommendations. This is an opportunity to shape how analytics, experimentation, and AI contribute to the continued growth of one of Sweden's most widely used consumer platforms.

What You'll Do


Drive product insights across the full customer journey by identifying user behaviour, friction points, drop-offs, and growth opportunities
Own A/B testing end-to-end, from experiment design and statistical validation to analysis and post-mortems
Champion our experimentation framework by coaching product managers, designers, and engineers on experiment design and data-informed decision-making
Collaborate with Product and Engineering teams to define, implement, and continuously improve reliable event tracking standards
Leverage AI tools to accelerate analysis, improve productivity, and apply sound judgement when validating AI-generated insights


Who You Are


Degree in Business, Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field
Proven experience (2–4 years) as a Product Analyst or Data Analyst, ideally in a technology-driven environment
Strong proficiency in SQL
Hands-on experience designing, analysing, and evaluating A/B tests
Experience working with product event tracking in fast-moving environments where processes and data are continuously evolving
Comfortable bringing structure to ambiguous situations and turning insights into action
Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders
Experience with GCP (BigQuery, Looker), Amplitude, Mixpanel, Confidence by Spotify or similar
Professional fluency in English; Swedish is a plus


What We Offer


The opportunity to influence product strategy and user experience through data at one of Sweden's most widely used consumer apps
The opportunity to shape how product decisions are made through experimentation, analytics, and AI
A collaborative culture built on ownership, innovation, and knowledge sharing
A flexible hybrid work model with four days in the office each week, including Wednesdays as our "Inspiration Day," when the whole company comes together for breakfast and shared activities
A modern office in the heart of Stockholm
A competitive salary and benefits package


About The Position

Start date: As soon as possible / By agreement

Location: Our office at Kungsgatan 38 in central Stockholm (hybrid)

Employment type: Full-time, permanent position

Application deadline: August 14

Application

We review applications continuously, and the position may be filled before the application deadline. We therefore encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

Summer is here, and we are also taking some time to recharge. During the holiday period, our response times may be slightly longer than usual, and the recruitment process will resume from week 32.

Thank you for your patience. We wish you a wonderful summer and hope to hear from you soon!

We are a SaaS company offering industry leading software for companies and customers primarily within health and beauty.

We are proud to be Sweden's largest app and marketplace for bookings with over 16,000 affiliated specialists nationwide. We make almost all of Sweden's salons and clinics bookable in one place. For example, hairdressers, massage therapists, pedicurists, beauty salons, chiropractors, and much more.

On Bokadirekt, you as a consumer can search for, book, and pay for treatments and services with just a few clicks. Every month, 3 million consumers visit bokadirekt.se, and 2 million bookings are made via our website and app.
