Title: Androidutvecklare
Description:
Vi är Sveriges största app och marknadsplats för bokning av skönhets- och hälsotjänster. Som ett SaaS-företag med branschledande mjukvara har vi över 16 000 anslutna specialister över hela landet. Vi gör nästan alla Sveriges salonger och kliniker bokningsbara på ett och samma ställe – inklusive frisörer, massörer, fotvårdare, skönhetssalonger, kiropraktorer och många fler.

På bokadirekt.se kan konsumenter söka, boka och betala för behandlingar och tjänster med bara några få klick. Varje månad besöks vår plattform av 3 miljoner konsumenter, och drygt 2 miljoner bokningar görs via vår webbplats och vår app. Bokadirekt växer snabbt och har för närvarande över 100 anställda med kontor i Stockholm och Luleå.

Om rollen

Vi söker en skicklig och passionerad Androidutvecklare för att ansluta till vårt växande tech-team på Bokadirekt – Sveriges ledande app för bokning och betalning inom skönhet och hälsa.

Du kommer att ingå i teamet som ansvarar för att kontinuerligt förbättra och utveckla Bokadirekts Android-app. Appen används av hundratusentals användare för att upptäcka, boka och betala för behandlingar och tjänster över hela Sverige. I nära samarbete med produktägare, designers och backend-utvecklare kommer du att designa och leverera nya funktioner, förbättra prestanda och skapa en smidig och härlig användarupplevelse.

Vi investerar i AI och automatisering för att skapa smartare, mer personliga upplevelser och göra det enklare för våra användare att hitta rätt behandling. Ditt arbete kommer att ha en direkt påverkan på användarna och bidra till framtiden för en av Sveriges mest använda appar.

AI som en naturlig del av hur vi bygger och utvecklar

AI är inte ett separat initiativ hos oss – det är en del av vår strategi och hur vi bygger framtidens Bokadirekt. Vår ambition är att bli ett AI-native bolag, där AI är en naturlig del av både våra produkter och vårt sätt att arbeta. Som Androidutvecklare blir du en viktig del av den resan, där du får möjlighet att experimentera, bygga och implementera AI-drivna lösningar i en produkt som används av miljontals människor varje månad.

Vad du kommer att göra


Utveckla och underhålla moderna Android-applikationer av hög kvalitet med hjälp av Kotlin, Coroutines/Flow och Jetpack Compose.
Samarbeta med designers och produktägare för att leverera smidiga, användarvänliga och visuellt enhetliga upplevelser.
Arbeta nära backend-utvecklare för att designa effektiva API-integrationer och dataflöden.
Arbeta i en modern Android-miljö där vi följer Googles riktlinjer och tillämpar en MVVM-baserad arkitektur.
Bidra till utvecklingen av AI-drivna funktioner och utforska hur AI kan skapa ännu bättre användarupplevelser.
Bidra till arkitektoniska och tekniska beslutsfattanden för Android-plattformen.
Skriva ren, enkel och testbar kod som är lätt att underhålla.
Kontinuerligt förbättra appens prestanda, stabilitet och användbarhet.


Vem du är

Vi Tror Att Du Har


5+ års erfarenhet av att utveckla och underhålla Android-applikationer.
Bred kunskap om Kotlin, Jetpack Compose och moderna Android-bibliotek.
Erfarenhet av att arbeta med RESTful API:er och hantering av lokal data.
Vana vid publicering i Google Play Store och CI/CD-arbetsflöden.
Ett genuint driv att sträva mot att bygga den absolut bästa användarupplevelsen.
En preferens för enkelhet – du undviker överkonstruktion och skriver tydlig, begriplig kod.
Ett proaktivt och nyfiket mindset med en passion för att bygga produkter som människor älskar.
Nyfikenhet på AI och ett intresse för hur tekniken kan förbättra både produkter och utvecklingsprocesser.


Meriterande Om Du Har Erfarenhet Av


Att bygga exceptionella upplevelser med Jetpack Compose
AI-driven personalisering eller rekommendationer.
Betalningar, bokningsflöden eller e-handel.
AndroidX-bibliotek som Navigation och Room, samt API-integrationer med t.ex. Retrofit.


Vad vi erbjuder


Chansen att bygga och utveckla en av Sveriges mest använda mobilappar.
Möjlighet att arbeta med en modern Android-stack där vi kontinuerligt utvärderar och tar till oss nya verktyg och ramverk.
Möjlighet att vara med och bygga ett AI-native bolag där AI är en naturlig del av både produkten och utvecklingsarbetet.
En kultur som bygger på samarbete, ägarskap och innovation.
Modernt kontor i centrala Stockholm.
Konkurrenskraftig lön och förmånspaket.


Om tjänsten


Startdatum: Omgående/ Enligt överenskommelse
Plats: Centrala Stockholm på Kungsgatan 38 (Hybrid)
Anställningsform: Heltid, tillsvidare
Sista ansökningsdag: 14 Augusti


Ansökan

Vi går igenom ansökningar löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag. Skicka därför gärna in din ansökan så snart som möjligt – vi ser fram emot att lära känna dig!

Sommaren är här och även vi passar på att ladda batterierna. Under semesterperioden kan våra svarstider vara något längre än vanligt, och urvalsprocessen återupptas från vecka 32.

Tack för ditt tålamod. Vi önskar dig en riktigt fin sommar och hoppas att vi hörs snart!

We are a SaaS company offering industry leading software for companies and customers primarily within health and beauty.

We are proud to be Sweden's largest app and marketplace for bookings with over 16,000 affiliated specialists nationwide. We make almost all of Sweden's salons and clinics bookable in one place. For example, hairdressers, massage therapists, pedicurists, beauty salons, chiropractors, and much more.

On Bokadirekt, you as a consumer can search for, book, and pay for treatments and services with just a few clicks. Every month, 3 million consumers visit bokadirekt.se, and 2 million bookings are made via our website and app.

  • Möjlighet att arbeta med en modern Android-stack där vi kontinuerligt utvärderar och tar till oss nya verktyg och ramverk.
  • Möjlighet att vara med och bygga ett AI-native bolag där AI är en naturlig del av både produkten och utvecklingsarbetet.
  • En kultur som bygger på samarbete, ägarskap och innovation.
  • Modernt kontor i centrala Stockholm.
  • Konkurrenskraftig lön och förmånspaket.

Om tjänsten

  • Startdatum: Omgående/ Enligt överenskommelse
  • Plats: Centrala Stockholm på Kungsgatan 38 (Hybrid)
  • Anställningsform: Heltid, tillsvidare
  • Sista ansökningsdag: 14 Augusti

Ansökan

Vi går igenom ansökningar löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag. Skicka därför gärna in din ansökan så snart som möjligt – vi ser fram emot att lära känna dig!

Sommaren är här och även vi passar på att ladda batterierna. Under semesterperioden kan våra svarstider vara något längre än vanligt, och urvalsprocessen återupptas från vecka 32.

Tack för ditt tålamod. Vi önskar dig en riktigt fin sommar och hoppas att vi hörs snart!

We are a SaaS company offering industry leading software for companies and customers primarily within health and beauty.

We are proud to be Sweden's largest app and marketplace for bookings with over 16,000 affiliated specialists nationwide. We make almost all of Sweden's salons and clinics bookable in one place. For example, hairdressers, massage therapists, pedicurists, beauty salons, chiropractors, and much more.

On Bokadirekt, you as a consumer can search for, book, and pay for treatments and services with just a few clicks. Every month, 3 million consumers visit bokadirekt.se, and 2 million bookings are made via our website and app.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Om tjänsten

Vill du ta en nyckelroll i förvaltningen och vidareutvecklingen av en verksamhetskritisk data warehouse-lösning i en stor och komplex organisation? Vi söker en erfaren Data Engineer som blir en central del av ett team med gemensamt ansvar för kundens data warehouse, i tätt samarbete med drift, utveckling och verksamhet.

Arbetsuppgifter


Kartlägga och dokumentera informationsflöden samt beroenden till kringliggande system
Använda SQL för att analysera befintlig lösning, förstå samband och identifiera förbättringspotential
Analysera och felsöka inkommande incidenter
Koordinera och samverka med drift- och utvecklingspartner
Agera teknisk kravställare mot utvecklingsteamet
Arbeta med test- och releasehantering
Fungera som arkitekt i befintlig lösning samt stötta inom governance och IT-säkerhet
Driva initiativ för förbättring respektive avveckling


Kvalifikationer (ska-krav)


God kompetens inom SQL och SSIS
God förståelse för informationsmodellering
Erfarenhet av Azure Synapse och övriga Azure-molntjänster
Erfarenhet av ett verksamhetsövergripande beslutsstöd
Förmåga att analysera stora datamängder, se samband och omsätta det till verksamhetsnytta
Självständig och strukturerad arbetsstil
God förståelse för test, release och kvalitetssäkring
Erfarenhet från större, komplexa organisationer med många intressenter
Obehindrad engelska i tal och skrift


Meriterande


Tabulärmodeller och Power BI
Databricks
Agil utveckling och arbete i tågstruktur (SAFe)


Personliga egenskaper

Du trivs i skärningspunkten mellan teknik och verksamhet, har stark analytisk förmåga och navigerar tryggt i stora datamängder. Du är strukturerad och självgående, en god kommunikatör och van att agera teknisk kravställare och samarbeta effektivt med drift, utveckling och andra intressenter i en komplex miljö.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Join the Data & AI journey at Schibsted Media

Data and AI are becoming increasingly important in how we build better products, support smarter decisions, and create value across Schibsted Media.

We're looking for a Data Engineer to join our Data Infrastructure team and help build the shared data platform that powers analytics and AI across Schibsted Media. Our team enables data teams to build trusted, scalable, and well-governed data products.

In this role, you will work at the intersection of data engineering and platform engineering. You'll help improve our data platform while also contributing to trusted data models, modern data pipelines, and engineering practices used across the organization. You will join the Data & AI organization, where we build data capabilities that help teams across Schibsted Media make better decisions, create trusted data products, and enable future AI use cases.

About The Role

We're looking for someone with 4+ years of experience who enjoys building solid data solutions while improving the shared data platform that enables teams across Schibsted Media.

You'll work with technologies such as Snowflake, dbt, Airflow, and Python to develop shared tooling, maintain platform capabilities, and contribute to modern data engineering practices.

You'll also help deploy and manage cloud infrastructure, automate operational tasks, and continuously improve the reliability and scalability of our platform. While prior infrastructure experience is a plus, we're looking for someone who is eager to learn and grow in this area.

What You'll Do


Collaborate with Data & AI teams to understand platform needs and deliver scalable data solutions
Design, build, and improve shared data platform capabilities, developer tooling, data pipelines, and automation using technologies such as Snowflake, dbt, Airflow, and Python
Support and evolve our Privacy Compliance Platform, helping enable secure, compliant, and governed access to data across Schibsted Media
Deploy, maintain, and improve cloud infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code and modern automation practices
Improve reliability, observability, performance, cost-efficiency, and scalability of our data platform
Develop reusable templates, tooling, and documentation that enable self-service across Data & AI teams
Develop maintainable data models that support analytics, reporting, and AI use cases
Contribute to good practices for data modeling, transformation, testing, documentation, and governance
Participate in code reviews, technical discussions, and continuous improvement of our ways of working
Contribute to a culture of ownership, learning, and collaboration


What We're Looking For

Must have


4+ years of experience as a Data Engineer or similar role
Strong SQL skills and a good understanding of modern data warehousing concepts
Experience building data pipelines and working with orchestration tools such as Airflow or similar
Hands-on experience with dbt and modern data transformation practices
Experience with Python for data engineering or automation
Experience with Git and modern software engineering practices
Ability to take ownership of tasks and deliver maintainable solutions
Good communication skills and ability to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. English is our main working language.
Collaborative mindset and willingness to learn from and contribute to the team


Nice to have


Experience with software engineering practices such as automated testing, CI/CD, and code review workflows
Experience with Snowflake or similar cloud data platforms
Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform or similar)
Experience with AWS, GCP, or Azure
Interest in data quality, observability, governance, or platform engineering


Why join us?


Build the platform that enables data and AI across Schibsted Media
Work with modern technologies and experienced engineers across multiple teams
Flexible working hours and hybrid work options with strong trust and autonomy
International environment with offices in Oslo, Stockholm, and Krakow
Strong learning culture with learning budget and active engineering & AI communities
Access to Schibsted's premium media products, including news and podcasts


About You

You enjoy turning technical challenges into simple, maintainable solutions. You're passionate about improving the developer experience and building reusable solutions that help other engineers move faster. You care about building reliable systems, but also understand that great engineering is about collaboration, continuous improvement, and enabling others to succeed.

You're curious, take ownership of your work, and enjoy helping build the platform that supports trusted data products across Schibsted Media.

Title: Analytics Engineer II
Description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

We’re looking for an Analytics Engineer II to join Spotify's Platform Central Data (PCD) squad, a cross-functional Data Engineering and Analytics Engineering team within the Platform Mission. You’ll help build and maintain trusted analytical models, metrics, and data products that power developer productivity, platform health, and leadership decision-making. Working closely with Data Engineers, Product, Engineering, and Platform partners, you’ll translate platform signals into reliable, well-modeled data assets that help Spotify ship faster and safer.

What You"ll Do


Build and maintain analytical data models using dbt (or similar SQL-based transformation frameworks) in BigQuery for a broad set of stakeholders
Build and operate reliable data pipelines using SQL, with a focus on testing, observability, and CI/CD
Help define and evolve key metrics for platform health, developer productivity, and ML/AI platform adoption
Partner with Data Engineers on upstream pipelines and collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Data Science to scope and deliver insights
Improve data quality, performance, and cost efficiency across pipelines and models, including troubleshooting and backfills
Contribute to dashboards and self-serve data products that enable better decision-making across teams
Follow and contribute to data quality, testing, and documentation practices across the analytics layer
Participate in a fair support rotation for key datasets, pipelines, and analytical products


Who You Are


You have 2+ years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, or a related field
You have strong SQL skills and experience with data modelling
You are experienced with dbt (or similar SQL-based transformation frameworks) and a cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, or Databricks SQL
You are familiar with workflow orchestration tools such as Airflow, Dagster, Prefect, or Flyte
You care about data quality, reliability, and testability
You are comfortable working with BI/visualisation tools such as Looker or Tableau
You communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical partners
You are able to prioritize and deliver in a fast-moving environment
You have experience with platform or developer productivity data, experimentation, or ML/AI metrics


Where You'll Be

This role is based in London or Stockholm.

We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.

At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Find our AI notice here: https://lifeatspotify.com/ai-notice

Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

Title: AI Healthcare Data Associate
Place: Ballerup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Ambu A/S is currently looking for a AI Healthcare Data Associate to be based in Ballerup.

We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented AI Healthcare Data Associate to join our Algorithms team developing innovative AI-enabled medical devices.

In this hands-on, operational role, you will play a key part in building and maintaining high-quality datasets that support the development, tuning, testing, and monitoring of AI and machine learning models. You will collaborate closely with the AI Data Team, AI Engineers, Clinical Specialists, Medical Experts, Software Engineers, and Quality Assurance/Regulatory Affairs teams to ensure datasets are accurate, well-annotated, traceable, and compliant with quality and regulatory requirements.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone early in their career who is passionate about AI and healthcare and is looking to gain hands-on experience across the AI data lifecycle within a regulated medical device environment. The role offers exposure to AI development workflows, healthcare data management, and quality-driven product development, providing a strong foundation for a career in AI-enabled medical devices.

Key Responsibilities

AI Data Preparation & AI Development Support


Support the collection, organization, and management of clinical videos, images, annotations, and associated clinical metadata for AI development, tuning, and testing.
Prepare and curate datasets for training, tuning, and testing according to defined requirements.
Maintain dataset inventories, metadata, version control, and traceability records.
Ensure datasets are structured, documented, and suitable for their intended use.
Support assessments of dataset quality, representativeness, and suitability for AI use cases.
Support exploratory data analysis and dataset readiness reviews.
Contribute to bias assessment and continuous data quality improvement initiatives.


Data Quality & Annotation


Perform data cleaning, preprocessing, and quality control activities.
Identify, investigate, and document data quality issues and support resolution activities.
Annotate datasets and review annotations for accuracy, completeness, and consistency according to established guidelines.
Support annotation quality control activities and the creation of high-quality ground truth datasets.
Generate data quality metrics, summaries, and reports.


Documentation, Compliance & Quality


Prepare and maintain data management, annotation, and other AI data-related documentation.
Ensure data traceability throughout the AI data lifecycle.
Support audit readiness, change control, and document review activities.
Contribute to compliance with Quality Management System (QMS) requirements, data governance processes, privacy regulations, and Good Machine Learning Practice (GMLP).


Suggested candidate profile

Qualifications

Skills and experiences can be earned in many different ways, and we understand that the best fit for the role cannot be prescribed on paper. You are however likely to succeed in this position with some of these qualifications:


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Biomedical Engineering, Data Science, Computer Science, Health Data Science, Bioinformatics, Life Sciences, or a related field.
Experience supporting AI/ML projects through data collection, preparation, cleaning, annotation, or dataset management activities.
Experience handling training, tuning, and test datasets.
Experience working with datasets, including documentation and record maintenance.
Familiarity with datasets commonly used in AI-enabled medical devices, preferably video or clinical data.
Understanding of basic AI and machine learning workflows and the AI data lifecycle.
Good understanding of data quality management and data verification principles.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and experience working with large datasets.
Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills.
Strong written and verbal communication skills in English.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience supporting the development of AI-enabled medical devices, Software as a Medical Device (SaMD), or healthcare AI solutions.
Experience with annotation platforms and data labeling workflows.
Experience using GitLab, Azure, cloud storage platforms, or data repositories.
Knowledge of AI data governance principles and GMLP.
Understanding of regulatory requirements relevant to AI-enabled medical devices, including:
EU MDR
FDA guidance for AI-enabled medical devices and GMLP principles
EU AI Act
GDPR
Experience working within a QMS environment.


Key Competencies


Strong analytical mindset
Exceptional attention to detail
Data quality and compliance focus
Critical thinking and problem-solving skills
Documentation excellence
Collaboration and stakeholder management
Ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams
Continuous learning mindset
Strong organizational and planning skills


Ambu – a visionary and international workplace where your efforts matter

Ambu is a company that expands rapidly and has ambitious growth targets. We meet these targets through interdisciplinary teamwork between motivated and highly skilled employees. Your contributions and efforts are crucial to our success and by joining us you will get the opportunity to work in an international head office with an informal working environment.

We offer great opportunities for personal development. Furthermore, we offer a wide range of professional, social and financial employee benefits in addition to exciting job challenges and continuous professional and personal development.

Title: Senior FP&A & Data Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Mercell, we're on a mission to revolutionize public procurement, making it accessible and effortless for everyone involved. Imagine a world where public buyers and visionary suppliers come together seamlessly to make a great deal easier and shape the future. That's what we're all about.

With a dynamic culture built on continuous growth, trust, and collaboration, Mercell offers an environment where your talents can thrive. You will be part of an international environment with ambitious and dedicated colleagues who are passionate about what they do and supported to be themselves.

Together, let's shape the future and create real value for society, one innovative solution at a time.




Your Mission

As a Senior FP&A & Data Analyst, you will join Mercell's Group FP&A team and report to the Director of FP&A. You will play a central role in shaping how Finance supports decision-making across a leading European SaaS business.




This is a hybrid role that combines commercial FP&A, business partnering and modern data analytics. You will help drive forecasting, budgeting and strategic planning while leveraging data, automation and AI to improve how Finance operates and delivers insight to the business.




Working closely with stakeholders across Sales, Customer Success, Product, Operations and Finance, you will translate operational drivers into financial outcomes, build scalable reporting and planning solutions, and help transform data into actionable commercial insights.

Beyond supporting the business, you will challenge existing processes, identify opportunities for automation and continuous improvement, and contribute to building a modern, data-driven Finance function equipped for the future.




Core Responsibilities

Own and continuously develop forecasting, budgeting and long-range planning models across ARR, revenue, billing, cash flow and operating expenses, ensuring robust decision support for executive management.
Design and develop scalable reporting solutions, dashboards and data models using BI and other analytical tools to improve transparency, support decision-making and reduce manual work.
Drive automation and digitalisation of Finance processes by leveraging modern analytics, AI-powered tools and workflow automation to improve efficiency, forecast accuracy and data quality.
Act as a bridge between Finance, Operations and Data teams, helping to improve data governance, process alignment and the quality of business-critical information.
Identify opportunities to simplify and modernise planning and reporting processes through technologies such as Power Automate, AI agents and other emerging tools.
Prepare insightful materials for Executive Leadership Team, Board and investor reporting, transforming complex financial and operational data into clear recommendations.
Continuously challenge existing processes, tools and ways of working, driving a culture of data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement across the Finance function.




Required Qualifications

Strong hands-on experience (ideally 3+ years) in FP&A, business controlling or commercial finance within a SaaS or subscription-based business
Master's degree (or equivalent) in Finance, Economics, Business, Engineering, Data Analytics or another quantitative discipline.
Proven experience building financial models and translating business drivers into forecasting and planning models.
Experience working with modern analytics and reporting platforms to deliver scalable business insight.
Comfortable working with large and complex datasets and partnering with data or engineering teams.
Excellent communication skills in English, with the ability to explain complex analyses to non-finance stakeholders.




Preferred Qualifications

Curious and technology-oriented with a passion for automation, AI and continuous improvement.
Experience with one or more modern analytics and automation technologies such as Tableau Power BI, SQL, Python, Power Automate, Microsoft Fabric or similar platforms is an advantage.
Structured, proactive and comfortable operating in a fast-paced environment.
Experience working in a PE-backed or high-growth technology company is an advantage.




Start date: As soon as possible

Duration: Full time, Permanent

Workplace type: Hybrid - working onsite at the office min. 3 days and from home, balancing flexibility with collaboration

Location: Oslo, Norway or Copenhagen, Denmark or Stockholm, Sweden or Utrecht, Netherlands or Berlin, Germany

Application Deadline: 15 August 2026. However, do not wait to submit your application - we will read applications and talk to potential candidates as we receive them

Title: Data Portfolio Analyst for Markets Data Office
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Copenhagen HQ, Denmark

Looking for your next career opportunity within Data Governance, where you can expand your critical data expertise in a fintech organisation?

Join us in building a new data capability in Markets

At Saxo, we are building the Markets Data Office as part of the Data Governance Programme. The function is structured across four domain areas Products, Operations, Counterparty Risk, and Market Risk reflecting how critical data is used across Markets.

As a Data Portfolio Analyst, you will work within one of these domain areas and help improve how critical data is defined, monitored, and controlled. Your work will contribute to establishing consistent ways of working across the Markets Data Office, while supporting domain-specific needs. While your focus will be within a specific domain area, you will operate as part of a single Markets Data Office with shared standards, tooling, and governance practices.

This is an opportunity to join early, shape how we work, and make a real impact on how data is governed at scale.

What You Will Do

You will work close to business stakeholders, data owners, data stewards, and technology teams to improve the quality and governance of critical data within your assigned domain area. Depending on the domain, this may include data such as instrument reference data, market data, price data, custody information, election data, trades, positions, margin utilisation, or model data.

Your Responsibilities Will Include


Identify and document Critical Data Attributes (CDAs) and support the governance of Critical Data Elements (CDEs) in your domain area
Analyse data to assess quality, identify issues, and support root cause investigations
Define and maintain data definitions, rules, thresholds, and supporting documentation used in monitoring and control activities
Contribute to ensuring data is accurate, consistent, and fit for business use
Monitor quality outputs, track remediation activities, and escalate blockers where data issues are not being resolved
Collaborate with data owners, stewards, and stakeholders across business and technology
Support the implementation of data governance practices and controls


A key part of the role is ensuring that documentation is clear, structured, and maintainable, enabling transparency and consistency across the organisation.

Who You Are

We are looking for someone who is structured, curious, and motivated to work with data in a business context:


Experience in data analysis, data quality, or a related field
Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail
Ability to understand data in the context of business processes
Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across teams
Ability to produce clear and structured documentation
Experience with SQL or similar tools is an advantage
Experience with Great Expectation or Databricks is an advantage


What We Offer


The opportunity to help build a new data function from the ground up
Exposure to key business domains and critical data flows
A collaborative and learning-focused environment
A role where your work directly improves decision-making and operational quality


You will join Markets Data Office in the early stages of its journey, where your role will grow alongside the organisation. This means exposure to new challenges, increasing responsibility, and the opportunity to shape how data is managed across the business.

Inclusion at Saxo

We are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where different perspectives are valued. We encourage applications from all backgrounds and experiences.

We get curious people invested in the world

When you work at Saxo, you become a Saxonian and part of a purpose-driven organisation, where good ideas are always taken seriously, and where you can make a true impact. We are invested in your development, and you can expect a robust career from day one when you join Saxo – no matter which role you take on.

You will join 2,500 other ambitious colleagues across 15 countries and become part of an international organisation. Working in Saxo, you will get to meet colleagues from many different cultures and backgrounds, and you should know that we value diversity and inclusion and see it as a genuine source of strength to drive growth, foster innovation and position us for long-term success.

We encourage an open feedback culture and supportive team environments enabling employees to grow and fulfil their career aspirations.

When you bring passion, curiosity, drive and team spirit, your learning journey will be dynamic and your career opportunities in Saxo will be immense.

At Saxo we don’t just offer a job – we offer an opportunity to invest in your future!

How To Apply

Click here to create an account and upload your resume and a short motivation. We look forward to getting to know you better!

Title: Data Analyst - 10-month maternity cover
Description:
Driving Digital Transformation Through Insightful Analytics – Viby J.

Do you enjoy turning complex data into trusted insights that help an international business make better decisions? We are looking for a Data Analyst to join STARK Group IT in a 10-month maternity cover position from 1 October 2026 until 31 July 2027. As part of one of Northern Europe's largest digital transformation programmes, you will help turn business needs into trusted data, actionable insights, and scalable analytics solutions used across STARK Group.

If you are motivated by creating clarity from complexity and enjoy diving into data with a delivery focused mindset, this could be the role for you.

This role is based in Viby J, Aarhus.

Your Impact And What You Will Deliver

You will join the STARK Group IT Data Management team, consisting of four Data Analysts and two Data Management Specialists, where you will collaborate closely with experienced colleagues and contribute to continuously strengthening our data management and data analysis capabilities.

While you will be part of the Data Management team, your day-to-day work will be closely integrated with project teams and business stakeholders. The role follows a hybrid working model with three days per week in the office and two days working from home. To support collaboration and knowledge sharing, the Data Management team works together from the office every Tuesday. The remaining office days are typically spent alongside your assigned project team and key stakeholders.

In this role, you will work closely with Product Owners, business stakeholders, architects, data engineers, and BI developers. You will play a key role in translating business needs into data requirements, identifying and validating data sources, establishing source-to-target mappings, and helping ensure a common understanding of business rules, KPIs, and data definitions across business and technical teams.

A key assignment during this maternity cover will be supporting reporting initiatives within Athene, STARK Group's large-scale digital transformation programme consisting of 16 interconnected projects across the business. Working across multiple projects and stakeholders, you will help ensure that reporting needs are understood early, data dependencies are identified, and future reporting solutions are supported by reliable and well-structured data.

In this role you will:


Translate business needs into clear data requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria, and supporting documentation.
Identify, assess, and validate data sources, source-to-target mappings and data transformation logic together with business and technical stakeholders.
Use SQL and analytical tools to explore, analyse, and validate data while identifying patterns, discrepancies, and data quality issues.
Define and document KPIs, metrics, calculation logic, business rules, and data definitions to ensure a shared understanding across teams.
Support Agile delivery activities such as backlog refinement, sprint planning, and change assessments by providing analytical input and data expertise.


You will be successful in this role when stakeholders experience you as a trusted analytical partner who brings structure, clarity, and practical insight into complex data topics. By ensuring that requirements are actionable solutions, data mappings and definitions are well understood, and data is reliable and fit for purpose, you help create confidence in both the data and the decisions built upon it.

What You Bring

We are looking for someone who combines strong analytical skills with curiosity, drive, and a desire to make an impact from day one. You are comfortable navigating ambiguity, asking questions when requirements need clarification, and balancing business value, data quality, and pragmatic delivery.

You bring:


Experience working as a Data Analyst, Business Analyst, or in a similar data-focused role, preferably within a complex or international organization
Strong analytical skills, including experience working with requirements gathering, data validation, data mapping data quality, KPIs, metrics, and business rules.
Solid SQL skills and experience analysing and validating data.
Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to communicate effectively across business and technical stakeholders.
A structured, collaborative, and delivery-focused mindset, with the ability to work independently and drive analysis activities from clarification to implementation.


You will work with tools including Azure Databricks, Power BI, Azure DevOps, Kafka, and Open Metadata (Data catalog). To set you up for success in this role, we expect you to bring hands-on experience with several of these technologies and the ability to quickly apply your knowledge in a business context.

In addition, experience with Python, ServiceNow, Scrum or Kanban, or finance-related business processes is an advantage but not a requirement. We expect you to hold a relevant Masters degree within IT, Data & Analytics, Business Administration, Finance, Engineering, or a related field. Equivalent practical experience may also be considered.

Welcome to Group IT

When you join Group IT, you join a collaborative international organisation where technology and business go hand in hand. We are replacing legacy systems and building a modern, modular IT landscape to support STARK Group's continued growth. You will become part of a community that values knowledge sharing, professionalism, and building solutions that make a clear difference to colleagues across Europe.

Want to know more and apply?

We will be happy to answer any questions you may have regarding the position and your options in STARK Group. You are welcome to contact Kristine Grann at Kristine.grann@stark.dk for more information.

As many of our colleagues will be on summer holiday throughout July, please do not expect your application to be reviewed during this period. We will resume the recruitment process in early to mid-August.

STARK Group 

STARK Group is a growing private equity-owned company with a strong legacy. STARK Group was first established in Denmark in 1896 and has today evolved into a leading European retailer and distributor of heavy building materials with net sales of approx. EUR 9 billion. With a dedicated team of more than 20,000 employees and +1,150 branches and distribution centres across the Nordics, Greenland, Germany, Austria and the UK, we live and breathe to serve professional craftsmen with heavy building materials all the way from the Austrian Alps to Tromsø, close to the Arctic circle and everywhere in between. STARK Group is committed to reach net zero emissions by 2050 across the entire business, is a signatory to the UN Global Compact, and works within fully 2030 validated science-based targets to keep global warming below 1.5°C At STARK Group IT, we believe in the richness diversity brings. We invite individuals of all backgrounds to join us in creating and inclusive and welcoming environment. We believe in a workplace where everyone can bring their authentic selves to work. 

 

Agency Policy 

STARK Group IT appreciates the interest of all candidates. However, we prefer to directly engage with individuals seeking employment opportunities. Therefore, we kindly request recruitment agencies, head-hunters, and third-party staffing services to refrain from contacting us regarding this job posting. All applications should be submitted directly by the candidates through our official application process. We value direct communication and look forward to reviewing your application.

Title: BI Intern
Description:
BI Intern to BORG Automotive

BORG Automotive is a leading European supplier of both remanufactured and new automotive spare parts, serving the independent aftermarket with quality, sustainability, and innovation.

Your Role

As a BI Intern, you will be an integral part of our BI team in Silkeborg, contributing to strengthening data-driven decision-making across the organization. This is a 6-month internship offering hands-on experience in a professional Business Intelligence environment.

You will support the development, optimization, and maintenance of BI solutions while collaborating closely with colleagues across the business. We offer a flat organizational structure where your input is valued and your learning is a priority.

Key Responsibilities

Support the development and maintenance of dashboards and reports.

Assist in data extraction, transformation, and validation from multiple data sources

Contribute to improving data quality and documentation of BI solutions

Work with stakeholders to understand business requirements and translate them into insights

What We’re Looking For

Currently studying within Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, Data Science, ecnomics, business administration or a related field from a relevant university or educational institution

Basic understanding of databases and data handling

Experience with tools such as BI Front-end tools, Excel, SQL, or similar is an advantage

Strong analytical mindset and willingness to learn

A collaborative mindset and a proactive, solution-oriented attitude

Fluent in English

What We Offer

A 6-month internship in our Silkeborg office within a dynamic and international company

A flat and open organizational structure where initiative is encouraged

A culture that supports learning, development, and collaboration

Hands-on experience with real business data and challenges

Ready to Make an Impact?

If you’re looking to apply your academic knowledge in a real-world BI environment and gain valuable experience, we’d love to hear from you.

Join BORG Automotive - where the most important part is you is not just our motto, but how we work.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you a Data Engineer with 2-7 years of experience ready to take the next step? And would you like to work directly with clients while keeping up to date with the newest technologies? Then read on & apply to become part of a dynamic young team of 60 data experts who help companies gain new insights using a wide palette of modern tools.


Contract: Full-time, permanent
Location: Central Copenhagen, near Vesterport Station



Work Assignments

The role requires you to be both a hands-on builder and a point of contact for our clients' data challenges. Specifically, you will:


Build robust data pipelines and models that streamline the ingestion, transformation, and delivery of data.
Implement and develop modern data platforms through DevOps & CI/CD practices.
Collaborate with our clients’ engineers, analysts, & business users to solve business issues and ensure lasting value.



You will learn from our many data engineers with 7-20 years of experience, while taking on a mentoring role for our less experienced consultants.

What You Bring To The Team

Ideally, you have:


2-7 years of experience working as a data engineer.
Proficiency in Python, SQL, APIs, and/or software development.
An analytical approach to problem-solving, with a strong interest in how to drive business improvement using data.
Clear communication skills and the curiosity to engage with clients on projects.
Experience with some of these technologies: Snowflake, Databricks, Fabric, dbt, Azure, AWS, or GCP.
A relevant degree.
A current base near Copenhagen and fluency in Danish and/or English.



If you're excited about this role but you don’t tick all the boxes, we still encourage you to apply.

Why Devoteam Data Driven?

You will join a fantastic team of talented, curious data experts in a collaborative and dynamic workplace. Beyond working with nice colleagues on impactful projects, we offer comprehensive benefits to support your growth:


Continuous Growth & Community: We invest in your talent and dedicate time for learning to ensure you have up-to-date certifications in our technologies.
Work-Life Balance: We prioritize your personal time. In addition to your six weeks of paid vacation, we offer the flexibility of taking unlimited unpaid time off.
Social Events: We offer a variety of social events throughout the year. Recently we have done Laser tag, Pottery, Disc Golf, Book Club, Curling, Board Game Night, Foosball Tournament, Ice Hockey, among many other things.



Our Impact

Below are a few examples of our work:


Migrated Novo Nordisk's global supply chain analytics solution from Alteryx-based workflows to a governed, cloud-native architecture using dbt, Snowflake, and Azure DevOps to support enterprise-scale analytics and collaboration.
Developed a comprehensive end-to-end data warehouse solution, featuring automated pipelines that structured dimensional data from diverse operational systems for unified analytics for a client.
Assessed the current data landscape in Novonesis and implemented a new self-service framework to enable business users to utilise the power of the modern data stack.



Application Process

Apply by uploading your CV no later than August 9th.

We expect to invite selected candidates for interviews between mid-August and mid-September 2026. Note that our recruitment team is off 18/7-9/8.

We encourage all qualified candidates to apply, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or disability.

About Devoteam

Devoteam is a leading digital consultancy with a strong international presence, represented in 25 countries (primarily in Europe) with over 11,000 employees globally. In Denmark, our team comprises around 350 people and is spread across three business units - Data Driven (our unit), a ServiceNow unit, and a Management Consulting unit - each focused on different aspects of digital transformation.

Title: Unsolicited: Business Intelligence & Data Science
Description:
Work With BI/data Science In An International Software Company
Are you motivated by making a difference and contributing to tomorrow’s BI/data science solutions? In which case you might be our new colleague.

You will create a strong foundation for strategic decisions
In your Business Intelligence or data science role, you will be working with various solutions that support Systematic’s business areas.

This involves the development of products and solutions for our customers and other business units – all in close collaboration with highly skilled colleagues.

Some of the tasks you will be working with involve:


Identifying and understanding the needs of both the customer and the product organisation through close dialogue
Using data to create value for our customers
Developing, coding and implementing BI/data science solutions
Cleaning and analysing data, and implementing and developing machine learning models
Facilitating workshops and building data models that support domains, user behaviour and software
Being part of a dynamic working environment characterised by collaboration, development and quality.



You can expect to work with a range of technologies, such as Microsoft Power BI, Python, Azure Cloud, Microsoft SQL Server, T-SQL, Microsoft SSIS and SSAS, data warehousing, data modelling and analysis.

High level of interdisciplinary expertise and collaboration
We are committed to acquiring knowledge and developing methods based on a well-functioning group of BI/data science colleagues.

We enjoy a very high level of job satisfaction, and the dynamics between experienced experts and young talents contribute to a high level of development and progress.

We work closely together across the different disciplines and areas of expertise, with teams comprising project managers, consultants, data scientists, software architects and business developers.

A key characteristic of the teams at Systematic is that we dare to challenge and develop ourselves and each other. We focus on knowledge-sharing in our work, and always find more innovative ways of doing things.

You understand the importance of BI and data science
You are creative, good at spotting opportunities and think out of the box. You have a solid understanding of the interplay between people, technologies and processes.

You have strong communication skills combined with sound business acumen, and you enjoy being involved in a wide variety of assignments.

You stay calm when faced with numerous tasks and requirements, and you maintain an overview and understand how to prioritise the tasks at every stage of the project.

You are organised, analytical and you are driven by the quality of your work. You enjoy presenting new ideas and have what it takes to garner the support of your colleagues and business partners.

At the same time, you are a team player who enjoys the prospect of actively contributing to the team and participating in development activities.

You are fluent in English, both orally and in writing. It is an advantage if you speak Danish, but this is not a requirement.

We work with security classified projects and therefore, as a Systematic employee, you need to have security clearance. Read more here:
https:
//systematic.com/en-gb/careers/security-clearance/ .

Any questions?

Please feel free to get in touch by emailing us at job@systematic.com. Unsolicited applications will continually be assessed according to current vacancies.
We store your details for up to six months, and if in that period your profile matches our requirements for a new job, you will be contacted to arrange an interview.



Connect with us!

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About Weld




The next generation of data infrastructure will be built for AI agents. Weld is built to be operated programmatically through APIs and MCP, so developers and AI agents can build and run the data foundations that modern companies depend on. We move data at scale across hundreds of integrations and increasingly serve as the backbone for agentic data workflows.




You can try Weld at weld.app.




The role




Most of your time is with customers. You onboard new customers, design their data setups on Weld with them, model the data that lands in their warehouse so it is ready for analytics and for agents to operate on, and unblock them when things break. You join pre-sales calls when prospects want to talk to someone technical, and those conversations focus on making their data usable by AI agents and on plugging Weld into their agentic workflows. When customers go deeper, designing transformation layers, structuring data for agent consumption, or building agent-driven systems on top of their warehouse, you consult with them and build alongside them. You do this work using the tools that make modern engineering fast: Claude Code for writing and refactoring, the Anthropic API for the agents and internal tooling that scale your work, and Weld itself, which you will operate through APIs and agents, the way our customers do. What you learn from customers shapes the product. You have a direct line to the founders and the engineering team, and your field insights influence what we build next, especially in how Weld exposes itself to agents through APIs, MCP, and tool interfaces.




What you will work on




Onboard new customers and design their data setups on Weld, from pipelines to warehouse models
Join pre-sales calls and technical evaluations, help prospects understand how Weld fits their stack, and shape early implementations
Diagnose pipeline issues end to end across our integrations and customer warehouses, and unblock customers fast
Help customers model their warehouse data, so it is clean, reliable, and ready for analytics or agent consumption
Consult with customers building agentic systems on top of Weld, helping them design transformation layers and structure data for agent consumption
Ship internal agents and tooling with Claude Code and the Anthropic API to scale how we onboard, support, and serve customers
Turn recurring customer problems into product improvements, and bring field insights back to the team to shape how Weld serves agentic workflows




We are looking for someone who




Has a solid understanding of modern data infrastructure, including ETL, reverse ETL, and data warehousing
Has experience with SQL and data modeling
Has built systems that interact with APIs programmatically
Has shipped something to production with LLMs or agents
Uses Claude Code, Cursor, or similar AI dev tools every day, and has opinions about them
Understands how modern agents are built: tool use, sub-agents, skills, MCP, and structured outputs
Has thought seriously about context engineering and how to evaluate agent behavior in production
Writes Python comfortably when needed
Communicates clearly and enjoys working directly with customers
Experience with TypeScript is a plus
A background in a customer-facing technical role such as professional services, solutions engineering, forward-deployed engineering, or analytics consulting is a plus




How to apply




Apply via LinkedIn with a short note. Include:

• A link or short write-up of something you have built with LLMs or agents. What it does, why you built it the way you did, and what you would change if you started over.

• A sentence or two on how you use AI tools in your own workflow today.




Working at Weld




Working at Weld means real ownership of what you work on, the freedom to make decisions, and a short distance between your work and what customers see. The role is based in our office in central Copenhagen, next to the Opera. We offer a competitive salary and equity, company off-sites, 6 weeks paid holiday, company-paid pension and health insurance, a MacBook Pro, and a company card from day one to buy what you need.

Title: Student Assistant, Digital Automation & AI Adoption
Place: Gladsaxe, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you curious about how data, automation and AI can improve real pharmaceutical production processes? Join us as a Student Assistant and help transform operational workflows through smart digital solutions in a GMP-regulated environment. This is your opportunity to work hands-on with data while contributing to meaningful improvements in healthcare production.

Your new role

As a Student Assistant focusing on Digital Automation & AI Adoption, you will support Process Support in Environmental Monitoring Operations with both daily activities and digital improvement initiatives. You will work at the intersection of pharmaceutical production, data analysis and automation, helping identify opportunities where digital tools can create real value.

You Will Have The Opportunity To


Map workflows and identify repetitive tasks suitable for automation or digital support
Support development of simple tools, dashboards or automation solutions
Explore and evaluate AI-enabled use cases in operations
Assist with testing, documentation and implementation of digital solutions in line with GMP (Slettes)
Analyse and visualise operational data to support decision-making


Your new department

You will join Process Support within Environmental Monitoring Operations, a team responsible for supporting daily operations, compliance and continuous improvement in an aseptic filling facility. The department works closely with production and stakeholders across functions to ensure high-quality standards while continuously optimising processes through digitalisation and smarter ways of working.

Your Skills And Qualifications

We are looking for a curious and structured student with a strong interest in applying digital solutions in a real operational setting.

To Succeed In This Role, You


Are currently studying within data science, engineering, IT, machine learning, business analytics or a similar field
Are proactive and willing to take ownership, while also knowing when to ask questions.
Have experience with tools such as Python, SQL, Power BI, Power Automate, Excel or similar technologies
Have a basic understanding of AI, automation or data analytics concepts
Are detail-oriented and understand the importance of accuracy in a regulated environment
Can communicate technical ideas clearly and collaborate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders


Working at Novo Nordisk

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

What We Offer

At Novo Nordisk, you’ll join a global healthcare company with a unique culture and strong results. We offer continuous learning, career development, and benefits tailored to your life and career stage.

Salary and Benefits: This position is hourly paid. For this position, the hourly base rate is 209,27 DKK, based on an assessment of relevant factors such as experience, educational background, demonstrated competencies, and the specific skills required for the position. In addition to the hourly base rate, Student Assistants are entitled to a pension scheme (currently 10% employer contribution and 5% employee contribution).

Contact

For further information about the position, please contact Simon Barlebo Wenneberg at SBWB@novonordisk.com.

Deadline

2nd of August

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

What We Offer

At Novo Nordisk, you’ll join a global healthcare company with a unique culture and strong results. We offer continuous learning, career development, and benefits tailored to your life and career stage.

Salary and Benefits: This position is hourly paid. For this position, the hourly base rate is 209,27 DKK, based on an assessment of relevant factors such as experience, educational background, demonstrated competencies, and the specific skills required for the position. In addition to the hourly base rate, Student Assistants are entitled to a pension scheme (currently 10% employer contribution and 5% employee contribution).

Contact

For further information about the position, please contact Simon Barlebo Wenneberg at SBWB@novonordisk.com.

Deadline

2nd of August

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.

Er god til at kommunikere med både kolleger, kunder og leverandører.

Du bliver en del af Init – en virksomhed i vækst, som har en ambition om at samle de bedste virksomheder og mennesker inden for Industriel IT og Automation. Title: BI Lead - Data & Analytics Enablement
Place: Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
We’re looking for a BI Lead to take ownership of how we structure, deliver, and scale analytics across the organisation.

What you'll do

In this role, you will lead our Business Intelligence capability, ensuring that data is transformed into clear insights, strong decision support, and business impact. You will operate at the intersection of analytics, data modelling, and business, and play a key role in shaping how we work with data across teams.

As our new BI Lead, you will:

Own and evolve our BI capability


Define and drive the overall approach to Business Intelligence
Establish standards for data models, reporting structures, KPI definitions, and data usability
Ensure consistency across reports, dashboards, and datasets
Shape how AI and agentic capabilities are applied within BI — setting direction on where they add value


Lead analytics, reporting, and data modelling


Own and evolve core datasets, Power BI and Power Apps solutions
Take ownership of how data is structured, modelled, and exposed for reporting
Raise the bar for how insights are communicated and used


Act as senior business partner


Work closely with senior stakeholders to shape key questions and analyses
Challenge assumptions and bring analytical rigour into discussions
Enable teams to become more data-driven in practice


Drive BI maturity and adoption


Translate business needs into analytical structures and data requirements
Work with data engineers to prioritise and improve the data foundation
Identify and lead initiatives that increase adoption and value of BI across the organization
Increase adoption and value of BI and AI-enabled analytics across the organisation


What you'll bring

We're looking for someone who combines analytical leadership, strong execution, and stakeholder impact – with proven experience in Business Intelligence, analytics, and data-driven decision support, and the ability to own BI solutions end-to-end.

You'll bring:


a solid technical foundation
– you have strong experience with Power BI, semantic models and SQL, as well as a solid understanding of data modelling, dataset design, and analytical structures
a leadership profile
– you take ownership beyond individual deliverables and set direction, not just execution; you communicate complexity clearly and engage confidently with senior stakeholders; you drive progress in an environment without rigid structure
a curiosity beyond the core
– a genuine interest in how AI can extend BI, and ideally some exposure to Power Apps or other low-code tools


What we offer

This is neither a pure developer role nor a pure platform ownership role – and that's what makes it interesting. You'll own how BI works as a capability, act as the link between data foundation and business decisions and help shape how AI and agentic solutions become a natural part of how BI operates at Implement.

At Implement, you'll find few rules and guidelines, and that's on purpose. We aim to create the right conditions for talented people to do their best work, with a great deal of autonomy and a strong culture of learning, sparring, and peer development. You'll be part of an ambitious internal team where your work has direct and visible impact.

You'll know you're succeeding when reporting is trusted across the organisation, stakeholders reach for data before making decisions, and analytical discussions get sharper over time.

About Implement

Implement Consulting Group (founded 1996) is an international management consulting firm with over 1,800 employees. Headquartered in Copenhagen, we have 12 offices across the Nordics, the DACH region, and the United States, making us one of the largest European consultancies. We support organisations in delivering sustainable strategic, commercial, and operational transformations. To help our clients succeed with their most critical change initiatives, we aim to attract, develop, and promote the best talent by creating a unique, collaborative learning environment where people can follow their passion, shape their own careers, and choose their own leaders.

The application deadline is 10 August
. Interviews will be held during August, and we look forward to being in touch with shortlisted candidates shortly after the deadline.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Senior Analyst, Membership Performance
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you ready to elevate LEGO Membership's performance management by driving data-driven decisions in a global role within a values-driven company? Look no further!

Join us for an exciting career opportunity as a Senior Analyst, Membership Performance with the Membership & Performance Analytics team part of our Market & Channels Central Marketing organization.

Please note The position is based at our Copenhagen HUB, and relocation assistance is not available for this role.


Core Responsibilities





Lead analysis and reporting on app user behaviours to identify trends and opportunities.
Conduct in-depth analysis on user acquisition to drive growth in our rapidly scaling digital product.
Provide insights to the CRM performance management team to boost CRM communication effectiveness.
Develop audience models to refine targeting strategies.
Implement customer segmentation to personalize user experiences and guide strategic business decisions.
Generate actionable insights to enhance our fast-growing loyalty program.
Advance your skills as an analyst using a state-of-the-art tech stack, alongside talented colleagues in a dynamic, ambitious team.




Play your part in our team succeeding

As part of the Membership & Performance Analytics team, you'll deliver insightful analysis and reporting for the LEGO Insiders app and our membership proposition. Collaborate with dedicated analysts focused on the LEGO Insiders program, a leading loyalty initiative for LEGO enthusiasts worldwide. Your work will help teams assess intervention effectiveness, fostering continuous learning and program improvement.

Do you have what it takes?





Experience in data analysis within mobile app analytics, marketing, or CRM.
Proven ability to generate impactful insights from complex data sets.
Strong proficiency in SQL and data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Adobe Analytics) is a must.
Expertise in advanced analytical methodologies handling outliers, regression analysis, time series forecasting, clustering techniques.
Proactive "can-do" attitude and approach to overcoming obstacles.
Excellent communication skills to clearly present findings to stakeholders.




Additional Skills That Would Be Beneficial





Experience with app user behaviour, customer acquisition strategies, and customer segmentation.
Expertise in Databricks, including advanced features (e.g., metric views).
Familiarity with email marketing and audience modelling techniques.




Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

Title: Audience Insights Specialist
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Do you want to be a valued part of the critical infrastructure that serves communities across Europe and beyond? Are you looking for a position in which you can set direction and help shape our company? If so, then you could be the newest addition to our team as Audience Insight Specialist.

Be part of the movement – and play your part

About The Role

We serve b2c Ferry passengers globally and we are seeking an analytical thinker with strong strategic vision. You should be capable of stakeholder management, with a strong background in turning data into insight and delivering innovative value-added analysis.

It is imperative you are someone that is proactive and passionate about insights. You will need to have a technical skill set, a thorough understanding of manipulating raw data and building this into actionable insight using analytical and visualisation tools.

You Will Be Responsible For


Support the Insights strategy through the building of reports that answer key business questions and track the performance of success metrics
Support the passenger audience segmentation strategy, definitions and tactics for activation, through incorporating audience segmentation splits within reporting
Support the Audience Insights Lead in mapping the passenger customer journeys and build reports to measure efficiency at specific touchpoints in the customer journey
Ensure insights are available across all markets and routes and aligned with business needs
Support the Audience Insights and Strategic Development team in identifying business opportunities through the identification of trends within reporting
Guide your colleagues in any research needs, aligned to the trends/ opportunities identified through Audience Insights reporting
Adopt cross-functional alignment: partner with revenue, marketing, sales and routes to ensure reporting and metrics are standardised
You will be data literate and have a strong ability to identify trends alongside an inquisitive nature to understand the root cause of specific trends identified.


Your Capabilities


Strong analytical capabilities with experience in visualisation tools such as Power BI and preferably also data connectivity tools such as SQL or similar
Logical thinker with the ability to understand business needs
Business acumen: tie insights to revenue, retention, product-market fit, and prioritization/ROI
Storytelling and influencing without authority, utilising data to provide actionable insights
Presentation and communication skills – complex is easy, simple is hard
Experience with surveys and survey design
Experience with brand tracking and/or online panel/communities is a plus


About You

You will work in the Audience Insights and Strategic Development team, utilising the data available to you to build a reporting suite that is aligned to business needs, generating the insights needed to guide strategic decisions. You will work alongside colleagues aligning reporting with research projects and programmes (e.g. The Voice of The Passenger – NPS and CSAT) to provide holistic actionable insights. You will be a very analytical individual that is excited by turning data into insights and capable to utilise tools to improve data connectivity and increase automation across reports. You will be an enthusiastic, curious, passionate and collaborative individual who continually looks for opportunities, with a solution focussed attitude.

Skills And Experience We Imagine You Bring


An analytical thinker with the ability to turn data into insights
2-3 years of insight and data & analytics experience (essential)
Preferably technical with experience in Power BI or similar tool and maybe also SQL, python, and/or other relevant tools
Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise own and others workload.
Ability to communicate complex data and ideas into digestible information.
Ability to be flexible, and adapt to change and leverage trends
Strong communication and collaboration skills


We think the strongest teams are built on diversity and inclusivity. Contrasting opinions, varied skills and strong characters bring out the best in us all. And we cannot wait for you to join.

Working at DFDS

Be a valued member of a diverse team at DFDS and help drive change together.

We promote a great workplace with a diverse and vibrant community and a healthy work–life balance. The position can be located in either Newcastle or Copenhagen.

While you’re with us, we want you to develop. At DFDS, we believe that development happens first and foremost through activities in the workplace. We will also offer training courses that can support you in your role, if needed.

Are we a match? Then what are you waiting for? Apply Now.

If you believe your experience, mindset, and motivation align with this role, we would be happy to hear from you. Please submit your CV at your earliest convenience, as we review applications on an ongoing basis.

Please note that due to the summer holiday period, there might be a delay in response time, but rest assured that we will get back to you as soon as possible.

We look forward to hearing from you.
