You will be an enthusiastic, curious, passionate and collaborative individual who continually looks for opportunities, with a solution focussed attitude.

Skills And Experience We Imagine You Bring

  • An analytical thinker with the ability to turn data into insights
  • 2-3 years of insight and data & analytics experience (essential)
  • Preferably technical with experience in Power BI or similar tool and maybe also SQL, python, and/or other relevant tools
  • Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise own and others workload.
  • Ability to communicate complex data and ideas into digestible information.
  • Ability to be flexible, and adapt to change and leverage trends
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills

We think the strongest teams are built on diversity and inclusivity. Contrasting opinions, varied skills and strong characters bring out the best in us all. And we cannot wait for you to join.

Working at DFDS

Be a valued member of a diverse team at DFDS and help drive change together.

We promote a great workplace with a diverse and vibrant community and a healthy work–life balance. The position can be located in either Newcastle or Copenhagen.

While you’re with us, we want you to develop. At DFDS, we believe that development happens first and foremost through activities in the workplace. We will also offer training courses that can support you in your role, if needed.

Are we a match? Then what are you waiting for? Apply Now.

If you believe your experience, mindset, and motivation align with this role, we would be happy to hear from you. Please submit your CV at your earliest convenience, as we review applications on an ongoing basis.

Please note that due to the summer holiday period, there might be a delay in response time, but rest assured that we will get back to you as soon as possible.

We look forward to hearing from you.

Title: Student Data Engineer
Place: Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Do you enjoy working with data – building reliable foundations and turning complex information into clear insights? At Implement Consulting Group, we're looking for a Student Data Engineer to join our internal team and help develop our data platforms and analytics capabilities further.

What you’ll do

It's a hands-on opportunity for a curious, motivated student who wants to work with data, systems, and business challenges – and to grow into data engineering alongside experienced colleagues.

You’ll support the development and maintenance of our internal data foundation – helping ensure that data across Implement is structured, reliable, and accessible for analysis and decision-making.

Your work will include, but not be limited to:


Transforming and preparing data for analytics and reporting using SQL and Python
Helping develop, maintain, and optimise our data pipelines
Contributing to data modelling and structuring of datasets
Working with tools such as Power BI and modern data platforms
Collaborating with colleagues across teams to understand data needs and ensure quality


The role sits at the intersection of data engineering and analytics
, working with data from multiple source systems. You’ll work closely with experienced data engineers and consultants in a team where learning, sparring, and quality come first.

What you’ll bring

We’re looking for someone who is structured, curious, and motivated to learn. You take ownership of your tasks, ask questions when needed, and enjoy working with data.

You bring:


A solid foundation in SQL, including querying, transforming, and validating data
Experience with Power BI or similar analytics tools
A basic understanding of ETL/ELT processes
Familiarity with data modelling concepts, such as star schemas, fact tables, and dimension tables
A willingness to learn new tools and technologies quickly
Basic experience with Python for data ingestion and/or processing


It is also an advantage if you have:


Experience with cloud data platforms such as Azure, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks, or Snowflake
Knowledge of data orchestration tools such as Azure Data Factory, Fabric Pipelines, Airflow, or dbt
Experience with Git or version control tools
A basic understanding of APIs, JSON, and data integration


What we offer

At Implement, you’ll be part of a collaborative and ambitious environment where we take both learning and quality seriously. We believe in combining freedom with ownership, collaboration without hierarchy, and a strong focus on learning and long-term impact.

You can expect:


Hands-on experience with data engineering in practice
Work with modern data platforms and tools
Support from experienced colleagues who are invested in your development
Responsibility early on — with the right support and sparring
The opportunity to grow your role over time


How to apply

Please submit your application ASAP, as we review applications and conduct interviews on an ongoing basis.

Apply now and take the next step in your data journey with Implement.



Make your next move matter

About us

Implement Consulting Group (founded 1996) is an international management consulting firm with over 1,800 employees. Headquartered in Copenhagen, we have 12 offices across the Nordics, the DACH region and the United States, making us one of the largest European consultancies. We support organisations in delivering sustainable strategic, commercial, and operational transformations. To help our clients succeed with their most critical change initiatives, we aim to attract, develop, and promote the best talent by creating a unique, collaborative learning environment where people can follow their passion, shape their own careers, and choose their own leaders. and choose their own leaders.

Title: Student Assistant
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Would you like to work in a dynamic and international environment, and are you a person with a flair for numbers? There are plenty of possibilities to test and develop your skills in a company with increasing focus on data and analytics. This is an opportunity to gain valuable experience within performance optimisation and digitisation, in the maritime industry.




Job Overview




Navigator Gas manages a fleet of gas tankers, traded worldwide. In an ever-changing world where new regulations and standards challenge the industry, the ability to collect and analyse large quantities of data, and to conclude and act on the findings, is essential for being successful. We need you to assist us in this process.




As a company, we are dedicated and ambitious in the pursuit of ever more sustainable and efficient ways of running our business. You will be given a central role, that will be visible and impactful, and important in achieving our goals for 2027 and beyond. You will work with colleagues in multiple departments and with tasks related to analysis, reports, presentations, data organisation, scripting and various ad hoc jobs.




Among the tasks, you will have the opportunity to work with, are the following:




Develop Advanced Procurement Performance Analytics
Drive Predictive Insights & Trend Indicators
Benchmarking & Performance Transparency
Deep-Dive Analysis of End-to-End Process Efficiency
Map AI development opportunities
Cost & Logistics Optimization Insights
Support Data-Driven Decision Making in Procurement




You will report to our Head of Procurement and have involvement in trans-departmental projects and initiatives relevant for your skills set.




About you:




You are currently studying your Bachelor’s / Master’s degree in engineering, business administration, IT, economics, data science, computer science or other relevant education you cannot wait to get your hands even deeper into the practical analytical side. You are interested in working in a high paced international environment.




You’ll be working with data and IT tools to support our team and help turn analysis into real results.




You’re a good fit if you:




Like working with data and finding insights in numbers
Take pride in generating results by delivering your work at the set deadline without compromising quality
Are curious, tech-savvy, and eager to learn new tools and methods
Demonstrates ownership by following through on tasks and seeking clarification when needed.
Are confident engaging key stakeholders and challenge views effectively.
Know your way around Microsoft Excel, Power BI and other Office tools
Fluent in oral and written English as this is our corporate language




We offer a flexible working week of 10-20 hours, with more hours required during holiday periods. Furthermore, we believe in freedom and flexibility under responsibility so the office hours can be adjusted to accommodate your school schedule.




Application




Please submit your application and CV via the “Apply” button as soon as possible and no later than 2 August 2025. Applications will be reviewed ongoingly, and we will conduct interviews in the beginning of August.




Start date for the position will be September 2026.




Navigator Gas is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Prospective employees will receive consideration without discrimination because of race, colour, religion, creed, gender, national origin, age, disability, marital or veteran status, sexual orientation, genetic information, citizenship or any other legally protected status.




We kindly ask you to not have a picture on your CV as we wish to encourage an unbiased recruitment process.

Title: Data Scientist – Commodities – Leading Quantitative Hedge Fund – Aarhus – Excellent Compensation + Bonus
Description:
I am working with a leading global quantitative investment firm that is looking to hire a Data Scientist to join its Commodities business in Aarhus.




This is a front-office facing role sitting at the intersection of Research, Trading, and Engineering, focused on transforming complex datasets into high-value research and trading inputs used across systematic commodities strategies.




The successful candidate will work closely with traders, quantitative researchers, and data engineers to onboard, transform, and productionise both traditional and alternative datasets that directly support investment decisions.




What you'll be working on

Partnering with Trading and Research teams to understand and deliver data requirements
Designing and implementing scalable data pipelines supporting systematic commodities strategies
Onboarding and integrating new datasets into the firm's research platform
Developing data transformation processes to create high-value, investment-ready datasets
Building tools and workflows to extract, clean, normalise, and aggregate data from diverse raw sources and formats
Managing the end-to-end lifecycle of new data acquisitions and integrations
Driving data quality, validation, and monitoring across critical research datasets
Troubleshooting complex data challenges and reducing time-to-production for new data sources
Researching and implementing innovative methods for data extraction and acquisition
Working closely with stakeholders across Trading, Research, Engineering, and external data vendors




This is a highly visible position offering direct exposure to front-office teams and the opportunity to work on some of the most interesting data challenges within quantitative investing.




What they're looking for

3+ years of experience in Data Science, Data Engineering, Quantitative Analytics, or a related data-focused role
Advanced Python programming skills, including strong experience with Pandas and NumPy
Experience designing and maintaining data pipelines and data transformation workflows
Experience working with financial, market, commodities, or alternative datasets
Postgraduate degree in Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, or another quantitative discipline
Demonstrable interest in financial markets and data-driven investment processes
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to operate in a fast-paced, high-performance environment
Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical and business teams
Prior experience within systematic trading, quantitative finance, hedge funds, asset management, commodity trading, market data, or alternative data environments would be advantageous




Compensation

Highly competitive base salary
Discretionary performance bonus
Comprehensive benefits package
Relocation support available where appropriate




The search is active, and the firm is looking to move quickly for high-calibre candidates.




To apply, respond to this advert or contact me directly for a confidential discussion at gala.santaballa@mondrian-alpha.com.




All applications will be treated in the strictest confidence.

Title: Senior Specialist, Market Intelligence - Data Centers
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
The Impact You'll Make

Danfoss is proud to be at the forefront of driving a global transformation in digital and electric solutions that increase productivity and energy efficiency. We are truly passionate about investment, and innovation, as we continue to support our customers' journey towards a greener and lower-carbon future.

As a result of our ongoing growth agenda, we now have an exciting new opportunity to recruit a Senior Specialist, Market Intelligence, to support our Data Center liquid cooling function.

As a Senior Specialist, Market Intelligence, you will play a pivotal role in shaping Danfoss' growth strategy within the rapidly evolving data center liquid cooling market. Serving as the central source of market and technology insights for the incubation team, you will help identify opportunities, prioritize investments, and guide strategic decisions that accelerate innovation. You will transform complex market, competitive, and customer information into clear recommendations that support business case development, strategic roadmaps, and long-term growth initiatives. Your work in this role, collaborating with leaders, engineers, business development teams, and external partners to uncover new opportunities, evaluate emerging business models, and drive impactful strategic initiatives, will help strengthen Danfoss' position in one of the world's fastest-growing technology segments.

What You’ll Be Doing

Gather and synthesize customer, market, and competitive intelligence to provide actionable insights that support strategic decision-making.
Conduct and interpret market research, industry analysis, and technology assessments to identify trends, opportunities, and competitive risks.
Develop market sizing models, business cases, and strategic recommendations that help prioritize growth opportunities and innovation investments.
Lead special projects, market evaluations, and business improvement initiatives to support the Data Center Incubation team's objectives.
Facilitate strategy workshops and roadmap planning sessions that align stakeholders around growth priorities, capability development, and future investment themes.


What We're Looking For

Degree qualification in a relevent field such as Engineering, Economics, Business Administration, Computer Science, alongside proven experience in market intelligence, strategy, business analysis, consulting, product marketing, or a related analytical role.
Strong ability to conduct research, analyze complex information, and translate findings into clear business recommendations.
Proven skills in market sizing, competitive analysis, business case development, and strategic planning.
Advanced proficiency with Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint, with the ability to communicate insights effectively to senior stakeholders.
Working knowledge of data center infrastructure, cooling technologies, or adjacent technology markets.


What You'll Get From Us

We promote from within and support your learning with mentoring, training, and access to global opportunities.
You’ll have flexibility, autonomy, and support to do your best work while maintaining a healthy work-life balance. Your well-being matters to us.
We strive to create an inclusive work environment where people of all backgrounds are respected, and valued for who they are.
You’ll receive benefits like annual bonus, paid vacation, pension plans, personal insurance, and more. These vary by country and contract, but they’re worth asking about—we think they’re pretty great.


Ready to Make a Difference?

If this role excites you, we’d love to hear from you! Apply now to start the conversation and learn more about where your career can go with us.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or other protected category.

Title: Principal Data Scientist
Place: Ballerup Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Location - Ballerup

Your new department

The Principal Data Scientist in the Commercial AI & Data department is responsible for designing, developing, and implementing advanced AI and machine learning algorithms to process and analyse structured and unstructured data from diverse sources. You will be part of a business area that drives impact across the full Novo Nordisk value chain. We strive to stay close to our stakeholders in the business to ensure we deliver on their needs, helping reach millions of people living with serious chronic diseases. Our focus is on being at the forefront within our three areas; ensuring the highest quality for patients, being a trusted tech leader and an environmentally responsible business.

Your Key Responsibilities Include


Define Data Science Strategy
Develop and influence the organization's data science vision and roadmap.
Collaborate with senior leaders and executives to align AI/ML initiatives with business priorities.
Help determine where data science can create the greatest strategic value.
Lead Innovation and Advanced Analytics
Design and champion innovative AI, machine learning, and advanced analytics solutions.
Solve highly complex business problems through advanced statistical and computational methods.
Evaluate and introduce emerging technologies and methodologies.
Serve as a Technical Authority
Act as the organization’s subject matter expert for data science.
Establish best practices, standards, and frameworks for model development and deployment.
Provide expert guidance on methodologies, tools, and architectural decisions.
Shape Business Decisions
Translate business opportunities and challenges into data-driven strategies.
Communicate insights, risks, and recommendations to executive leadership.
Influence investment decisions and enterprise-wide initiatives using evidence-based approaches.
Mentor and Develop Talent
Coach senior data scientists and technical leaders.
Build organizational capability through mentoring, knowledge sharing, and establishing communities of practice.
Help develop future leaders in data science.
Drive Cross-Functional Collaboration
Work across business functions, technology, engineering, analytics, product, compliance, and external partners.
Lead large-scale initiatives that span multiple teams and organizational boundaries.
Represent the IT Organization across the Stakeholders’ communities
Bring stakeholders together on the DS strategy and vision
Build external networks and stay at the forefront of AI and data science advancements.
Position the Enterprise AI org. as a leader in data-driven innovation.


Your New Department

The Data Scientist in the Commercial AI & Data department is responsible for designing, developing, and implementing advanced AI and machine learning algorithms to process and analyse structured and unstructured data from diverse sources. You will be entrusted with making independent decisions on the selection of suitable methodologies, and techniques for data analysis and model development.

Skills & Qualifications.


Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field. Ph.D. is preferred.
A proven track record of innovation and thought leadership.
Minimum 10 years of experience in data science, machine learning, or AI, with a focus on developing and applying advanced algorithms.
Deep expertise in cutting-edge data science methodologies and technologies
Strong research capabilities and the ability to translate research into business value
Experience developing enterprise-scale data strategies and AI roadmaps.
Ability to provide visionary leadership for the data science function
Broad understanding of pharma industry and Commercial business processes
Strong awareness of ethical and responsible AI practices
Experience influencing strategic decisions through data science and analytics.
In-depth knowledge of machine learning concepts, data modeling techniques and AI solution design.
Experience working with cross-functional teams (data engineering, ML engineering, analytics, product, operations).
Familiarity with MLOps tools and practices (e.g., MLflow, model registries, CI/CD for models) and cloud‑agnostic deployment patterns.
In‑depth understanding and practical experience with architecture, implementation, DevOps and support for AI solutions.
Experience working with cross‑functional teams (data engineering, ML engineering, analytics, product, operations) and external vendors.
Experience in pharmaceutical or regulated industries is beneficial but not mandatory.


Working at Novo Nordisk

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

What We Offer

There is, of course, more on offer here than the uniqueness of our culture and the extraordinary results we produce. Being part of a global healthcare company means opportunities to learn and develop are all around us, while our benefits are designed with your career and life stage in mind.

Salary

For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 847,000.00 to 1,245,100.00 DKK, corresponding to the level of the position.

The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate’s skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.

Incentives and Benefits: The salary package may include short-term and/or long-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.

We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

Title: Data Scientist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Trustpilot, we're on an incredible journey. We're a profitable, high-growth FTSE-250 company with a big vision: to become the universal symbol of trust. We run the world's largest open customer review platform, and while we've come a long way, there's still so much exciting work to do. Come join us at the heart of trust!

A core value proposition of Trustpilot is Trust. We work hard to ensure that our platform has genuine content from real people with real experiences. That means eliminating fake reviews and acting on signs of suspicious behavior to protect our platform from fraudulent activity. Our Trust Applied AI team’s mission is to use advanced modeling to safeguard the integrity of online reviews, and we have several cross-functional teams devoted to Trust where Applied AI is integral to achieving our goals.

 

We are looking for a Data Scientist to join our Trust team. You and your team will work to develop, deploy, and maintain innovative models at scale, alongside a cross-functional team of software developers, product managers, ML engineers, and designers. You will have the opportunity to collaborate widely across the business with our Technology and Product teams. You will also work closely with stakeholders in Legal and Platform Integrity.

To succeed in this role, you should have proven experience in developing and deploying ML models, ideally using graph data. You need a strong technical foundation and hands-on experience in all stages of data preparation, exploration, and modeling. An adaptable mindset and understanding of the interface between Applied AI and engineering are essential as you will be working in cross-disciplinary teams. Experience with deploying solutions into production is expected, as you will be responsible for implementing end-to-end solutions—from data preprocessing to serving predictions.

 

What you will do:

Be hands-on in delivering the Applied AI / Data Science component of key strategic projects in Trust, from building and fine-tuning language models to sequence modeling, graph-based models, etc.
Develop, maintain, and deploy production-ready ML models, with tooling support from ML engineers
Engage with both technical and non-technical stakeholders and contribute to the Trust roadmap
Seize the chance to meaningfully influence our business through the latest advancements in Applied AI and keep Trustpilot on the edge of innovation when it comes to fake review detection
The opportunity to work with market-standard data engineering and deployment technologies including Google Agent Platform tools and Google BigQuery, and with leading Data Science tools and emerging technologies for model building and deployment
Opportunities to develop your career in a friendly, diverse, international team and workplace

 

Who you are:

Experience with analytical and quantitative problem-solving using advanced statistical techniques and machine learning methods. Particularly relevant experience: high-precision classification focus, graph neural networks, graph algorithms used within machine learning, fine tuning of large language models, working with large label sets with varying label quality, and adversarial machine learning.
Experience in building and deploying production-ready ML models at scale, and solid data engineering skills, ideally in GCP
Ability in Python and SQL for data manipulation, modeling, and scripting
Experience in working with large datasets, ideally from tech platforms, e-commerce, or SaaS-type businesses. Knowledge of behavioral data, detecting misbehavior and/or fraud at scale is a big plus
Knowledge of data pipelining and prior experience with cloud-based ML model deployments
Great communication skills - both with technical colleagues and with business stakeholders 
2-3 years or more of technical experience in a Data Science role, preferably in the technology sector or in a technical consultancy

 

What’s in it for you:

A range of flexible working options to dedicate time to what matters to you
Competitive compensation package + bonus
25 days holiday per year, with an extra 5 days holiday allocated after your 1 year anniversary (prorated) 
Two (paid) volunteering days a year to spend your time giving back to the causes that matter to you and your community
Rich learning and development opportunities are supported through the Trustpilot Academy and Blinkist
Comprehensive health package, pension, and full access to Headspace, a popular mindfulness app to promote positive mental health
Paid parental leave
Central office with  a laid-back vibe and constant buzz of different languages being spoken everywhere you go It’s complete with a coffee bar, canteen, and table tennis and has a wide variety of refreshments available - you can opt-in for breakfast and lunch at an affordable price
Regular opportunities to connect and get to know your fellow Trusties, including company-wide celebrations and events, ERG activities, and team socials
Opportunity to join the Trustpilot Social Club for a small monthly fee and attend regular social and leisure activities like go-karting, cooking classes, and escape rooms 
Employee discounts to restaurants and shops 






Still not sure?

We want to be a part of creating a more diverse, equitable, and inclusive world of work for all. We’re excited to hear about your experiences along with how you will contribute to our working culture. Even if you don’t feel you meet all the requirements, we'd still really like to hear from you!

About us

Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial — we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.

Today, we have more than 350 million reviews and 60 million monthly active users across the globe, with 149 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1000 employees and we’re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in  Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York. 

We’re driven by connection. It’s at the heart of what we do. Our culture keeps things fresh –– it’s built on the relationships we create. We talk, we laugh, we collaborate and we respect each other. We work across borders and cultures to be the universal symbol of trust in an ever-changing world. With vibrant office locations worldwide and over 50 nationalities, we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace with diverse perspectives and ideas. 

Our purpose to help people and businesses help each other is a tall order, but we keep it real. We’re a great bunch of humans, doing awesome stuff, without fuss or pretense. A successful Trustpilot future is driven by you –– we give you the autonomy to shape a career you can be proud of. If you’re ready to grow, let’s go.

Join us at the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive and where different viewpoints and experiences are valued and respected. Trustpilot will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, religious beliefs, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, neurodiversity, disability, age, parental or veteran status. Together, we are the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is a global company and our data practices are designed to ensure that your personally identifiable information is appropriately protected. Please note that your personal information will be transferred, accessed, and stored globally as necessary for the uses and disclosures stated in our Privacy Policy.

If you have a disability and would like to discuss any adjustments you might need either in submitting your application, or to the recruitment process more generally, please let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition Team (talent.acquisition@trustpilot.com) quoting the role you wish to apply for.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
VML Home

About Us Work What We Do Insights News Enterprise Solutions Careers Contact Us

Who We Are

VML, part of WPP, is a leading creative company that combines brand experience, customer experience, and commerce, creating connected brands to drive growth. VML is celebrated for its innovative and human first, award-winning work for blue chip client partners including AstraZeneca, Colgate-Palmolive, Dell, Ford, Microsoft, Nestlé, The Coca-Cola Company, and Wendy's. The agency is recognized by the Forrester Wave™ Reports, as a Leader among Marketing Creative and Content Service Providers, Commerce Services, Global Digital Experience Services, Global Marketing Services and, most recently, Marketing Measurement & Optimization. In addition, VML’s specialist health network, VML Health, is one of the world’s largest and most awarded health agencies. VML’s global network is powered by 26,000 talented people across 55+ markets, with principal offices in Kansas City, New York, Detroit, London, São Paulo, Shanghai, Singapore, and Sydney.

About WPP

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit WPP.com.

About Us

We believe data, creativity and technology work best when they work together. Our mission is to shape the next era of media — one that’s smarter, more connected, and powered by insight. We’re part of a global network that values openness, collaboration and bold ideas. Here, you’ll have the chance to make an impact and grow your career and skill set with some of the best minds in the industry.

The Role

We’re looking for a Data Engineer to contribute to our Open Intelligence data initiative, helping to build and maintain our core data capabilities. You’ll play a key role in building and maintaining the data connectors that feed data to Open Intelligence. This role requires good communication skills and an understanding of brand, adtech, and marketing data, as well as the benefits of well-engineered data pipelines. You will contribute to creating systems that keep our global media ecosystem running, ensuring that data is reliable, scalable, and reflects the needs of the business.

This is a hands-on technical role for someone who is comfortable delivering reliable data solutions and is eager to leverage new development and testing technologies. You will collaborate closely with team members and product stakeholders to ensure our data infrastructure meets evolving business needs.

The role is in our lovely Copenhagen offices at Nørrebro, where you will join a larger team of roughly 50 people working on Open Intelligence.

What You’ll Do


Build, manage and maintain data extraction pipelines with the help of AI coding assistants.
Pre-aggregate data in clean rooms using SQL-dialects when client or partner concerns demand it.
Fetch data through REST APIs using Python or Node.js when needed.
Collaborate with our Product organization regarding the addition of new data sources or changes to existing ones, and translate those requirements into maintainable code.
Contribute to identifying and addressing technical debt and inefficiencies within our data platform.
Support our regional teams in following best practices for adding their local datasets to Open Intelligence.
Collaborate closely with analysts and data scientists who leverage Open Intelligence, to make sure that data connectors work as expected.
Contribute to designing and building strong data governance and quality frameworks to ensure connectors produced are accurate and work consistently throughout their lifetime.
Stay curious, explore new tools, techniques, and technologies that can help us move faster and smarter.


What You’ll Bring


Strong communication skills — you can effectively explain technical concepts to various audiences.
2-4 years of experience in data engineering.
Familiarity with adtech- and/or martech data and terminology.
Interest in leveraging AI coding assistants and prompt engineering to enhance productivity.
Solid experience with data wrangling in a language such as SQL, Python, or Scala.
Interest in applying or learning the Scala language for data processing.
Understanding of strongly typed computer languages and compilers, and an appreciation for finding data processing errors early.
Proficiency in one or more modern data ecosystems — e.g., Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, InfoSum, or Redshift.
Experience working with CI/CD, version control, and testing best practices.
Experience with cloud-based platforms like GCP, AWS, or Azure, and deployment abstractions like Docker or Terraform.
A passion for data, technology, and computer languages.
A proactive approach to solving problems and improving systems.


What We Offer

We believe great work starts with great people. At WPP Media, you’ll find:


A supportive, inclusive culture built on trust, ambition and collaboration.
Access to world class learning and development opportunities across the WPP network.
Competitive salary, benefits and a focus on wellbeing.
The chance to make a real impact shaping how data powers the next generation of media.


We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office an average of four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

WPP (VML) is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

VML is a WPP agency (NYSE: WPP). For more information, please visit  www.vml.com, and follow along on Instagram, LinkedIn, and X.

When you click "Submit Application", this will send any information you add below to VML. Before you do this, we think it's a good idea to read through our Recruitment Privacy Policy. California residents should read our California Recruitment Privacy Notice. This explains what we do with your personal data when you apply for a role with us, and, how you can update the information you have provided us with or how to remove it.

  • Strong communication skills — you can effectively explain technical concepts to various audiences.
  • 2-4 years of experience in data engineering.
  • Familiarity with adtech- and/or martech data and terminology.
  • Interest in leveraging AI coding assistants and prompt engineering to enhance productivity.
  • Solid experience with data wrangling in a language such as SQL, Python, or Scala.
  • Interest in applying or learning the Scala language for data processing.
  • Understanding of strongly typed computer languages and compilers, and an appreciation for finding data processing errors early.
  • Proficiency in one or more modern data ecosystems — e.g., Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, InfoSum, or Redshift.
  • Experience working with CI/CD, version control, and testing best practices.
  • Experience with cloud-based platforms like GCP, AWS, or Azure, and deployment abstractions like Docker or Terraform.
  • A passion for data, technology, and computer languages.
  • A proactive approach to solving problems and improving systems.

What We Offer

We believe great work starts with great people. At WPP Media, you’ll find:

  • A supportive, inclusive culture built on trust, ambition and collaboration.
  • Access to world class learning and development opportunities across the WPP network.
  • Competitive salary, benefits and a focus on wellbeing.
  • The chance to make a real impact shaping how data powers the next generation of media.

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office an average of four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

WPP (VML) is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

VML is a WPP agency (NYSE: WPP). For more information, please visit  www.vml.com, and follow along on Instagram, LinkedIn, and X.

California residents should read our California Recruitment Privacy Notice. This explains what we do with your personal data when you apply for a role with us, and, how you can update the information you have provided us with or how to remove it.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
About the Company

Our client is a global, technology-driven systematic investment manager operating across all major liquid asset classes. With a strong culture of innovation and a scientific approach to investing, they combine cutting-edge data, research, and engineering to deliver consistent, high-quality returns.




The Role

We are looking for an exceptional Data Scientist to join a high-performing Commodities desk. Sitting at the intersection of Research, Trading, and Engineering, you will play a critical role in ensuring the firm leverages its data effectively to drive investment decisions.




What You'll Be Doing

Collaborating with the trading desk to design and implement robust data pipelines
Working alongside data engineers to onboard relevant datasets into the firm's infrastructure
Implementing data transformations to produce high-value datasets used directly in investment decisions
Writing clean, well-structured Python code to extract, clean, and aggregate data from a wide range of raw sources and formats
Managing the full end-to-end process of onboarding new datasets
Proactively identifying and solving data-related problems to minimise time to production
Experimenting with novel data extraction methods to continuously improve the firm's data toolkit




What We're Looking For

3+ years of experience as a Data Scientist or in a similar quantitative role; buy-side experience is a strong advantage
Postgraduate degree (MSc or PhD) in a quantitative discipline - Mathematics, Physics, Engineering or similar
Advanced Python skills, including strong proficiency with data handling libraries such as Pandas and NumPy
Experience working with both traditional and alternative financial datasets
A genuine interest in financial markets and data-driven analysis
Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively across Research, Trading, Engineering, and external vendors
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-performance environment




Why Apply?

This is a rare opportunity to join a world-class quantitative investment firm at the forefront of data-driven investing. The team offers a collaborative, intellectually rigorous environment where your work will have direct impact on the firm's investment performance.

Title: Ops Analyst
Place: Hvidovre, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Country: Denmark

Location: All Nordic locations

Function: Operations | Full time

We are strengthening our Ops Data & Analytics team and are looking for an Ops Analyst who wants to work with data beyond traditional reporting — building scalable solutions that support decision-making across Operations.

You will join a Nordic team supporting one of the bank’s largest functions, working with complex, high-volume data across customer journeys and operational processes.

Key Responsibilities


Build and maintain data pipelines and structured datasets
Develop and optimize Power BI reports and semantic models
Analyze data using SQL and Python to support business decisions
Drive automation and standardization of reporting
Ensure data quality, documentation and consistent KPI definitions
Enable self-service reporting for stakeholders
Translate business needs into scalable data solutions
Collaborate with Operations, Data Management and IT


Your profile


0–5 years of experience within Data & Analytics / BI
Experience with Power BI and SQL; exposure to Python and data modeling is a plus
Strong analytical and structured mindset
Comfortable working with complex datasets
Curious, proactive and motivated to develop technical skills
Able to translate data into clear insights for stakeholders


You are proactive, structured and motivated to develop your technical skills. You enjoy solving problems, collaborating across teams and turning data into actionable insights.

We encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all the requirements. If you have a strong analytical mindset, curiosity and a willingness to learn, we would like to hear from you.

We offer

A dynamic Nordic setup with strong collaboration across countries, where you will work with modern tools (Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, SQL, Python) and contribute to building scalable analytics capabilities in Operations.

For Questions, Please Contact

Philip Aarup, Team Leader Analytics & Reporting

Title: Junior Analyst, Modelling & Quantitative Analysis
Description:
Blue Power Partners A/S


Denmark (Aalborg, or Copenhagen)


Blue Power Partners is a global consultancy and project development company dedicated to developing renewable energy. We aim to accelerate the green transition by shaping the renewable technologies of tomorrow.

Do you have a strong analytical mindset and a passion for working with data in the world of renewable energy? Then you might be the Junior Analyst we are looking for.

About The Role

You will join our Project & Energy System Optimizations team, which delivers world-class data analysis and modelling solutions. The team brings together an eclectic mix of talent — from specialists to PhDs — across mathematics, energy engineering, finance, wind modelling, electrical engineering, and data science.

As a Junior Analyst, you will be involved in a variety of projects and have the opportunity to develop your skills in practice. Your day-to-day responsibilities will include:


Collecting, cleaning, and transforming large datasets to provide insight into renewable energy problems.
Supporting energy systems integration modelling using linear and mixed-integer linear programming (LP/MILP).
Assisting in the evaluation and optimisation of project business cases.
Developing analytical tools and automating data wrangling tasks through clean, structured code.
Preparing reports and presentations that summarise key findings for internal stakeholders.
Working closely with cross-functional teams to understand data needs and deliver analytical support.


In this role, we expect you to work 8-20 hours on average per week, possibly adding more hours if possible. It is essential that you take care of your studies, which means we are flexible in exam periods and when facing project deadlines.

About You

You are curious, analytical, and eager to learn. You enjoy working in an agile, fast-moving environment and bring a can-do attitude to everything you do. You are a team player who is happy to take direction and work collaboratively to get things done.

We expect you to bring the following experience and skills:


Currently enrolled in a bachelor's or master's programme in engineering, mathematics, data science, or a related field.
Proficiency in Python; experience with other programming language is a plus.
Able to write clean, structured code and work with large datasets — including transforming, mapping, and aligning data for analysis.
Familiarity with linear programming (LP/MILP) and constraint optimisation — able to run predefined routines with guidance.
Basic knowledge of statistical techniques for validating data patterns, and experience with version control (e.g. Git).
Strong communicator, fluent in English — both written and verbal, comfortable engaging with both internal and external stakeholders.
Curious about the electricity and commodity market, with a positive can-do attitude and a willingness to follow established processes and take ownership of assigned tasks


Practical Details


This is a student role, you are on your Bachelor's or Master's and expected to work 8-20hours on average.
We are flexible during exam periods and project deadlines.
Location: Aalborg or Copenhagen
Start date: Flexible.


Application & Contact

Submit your CV and application in English via our recruitment system. For questions about the role, feel free to reach out to the Ioannis Kountouris, Lead - Project & Energy Systems Optimization. iko@bluepp.dk

About Blue Power Partners

Blue Power Partners supports leading developers, asset owners, and technology companies worldwide across origination, development, construction, and performance optimization of onshore/offshore wind, solar, PtX, and storage projects.

Working at Blue Power Partners means joining an international, multicultural team with a strong, can-do, dynamic, and entrepreneurial spirit. Our people are our greatest asset — when our people grow, we grow.



APPLY

How to apply

Title: Senior Specialist, Market Intelligence - Data Centers
Place: Nordborg, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
The Impact You'll Make

Danfoss is proud to be at the forefront of driving a global transformation in digital and electric solutions that increase productivity and energy efficiency. We are truly passionate about investment, and innovation, as we continue to support our customers' journey towards a greener and lower-carbon future.

As a result of our ongoing growth agenda, we now have an exciting new opportunity to recruit a Senior Specialist, Market Intelligence, to support our Data Center liquid cooling function.

As a Senior Specialist, Market Intelligence, you will play a pivotal role in shaping Danfoss' growth strategy within the rapidly evolving data center liquid cooling market. Serving as the central source of market and technology insights for the incubation team, you will help identify opportunities, prioritize investments, and guide strategic decisions that accelerate innovation. You will transform complex market, competitive, and customer information into clear recommendations that support business case development, strategic roadmaps, and long-term growth initiatives. Your work in this role, collaborating with leaders, engineers, business development teams, and external partners to uncover new opportunities, evaluate emerging business models, and drive impactful strategic initiatives, will help strengthen Danfoss' position in one of the world's fastest-growing technology segments.

What You’ll Be Doing

Gather and synthesize customer, market, and competitive intelligence to provide actionable insights that support strategic decision-making.
Conduct and interpret market research, industry analysis, and technology assessments to identify trends, opportunities, and competitive risks.
Develop market sizing models, business cases, and strategic recommendations that help prioritize growth opportunities and innovation investments.
Lead special projects, market evaluations, and business improvement initiatives to support the Data Center Incubation team's objectives.
Facilitate strategy workshops and roadmap planning sessions that align stakeholders around growth priorities, capability development, and future investment themes.


What We're Looking For

Degree qualification in a relevent field such as Engineering, Economics, Business Administration, Computer Science, alongside proven experience in market intelligence, strategy, business analysis, consulting, product marketing, or a related analytical role.
Strong ability to conduct research, analyze complex information, and translate findings into clear business recommendations.
Proven skills in market sizing, competitive analysis, business case development, and strategic planning.
Advanced proficiency with Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint, with the ability to communicate insights effectively to senior stakeholders.
Working knowledge of data center infrastructure, cooling technologies, or adjacent technology markets.


What You'll Get From Us

We promote from within and support your learning with mentoring, training, and access to global opportunities.
You’ll have flexibility, autonomy, and support to do your best work while maintaining a healthy work-life balance. Your well-being matters to us.
We strive to create an inclusive work environment where people of all backgrounds are respected, and valued for who they are.
You’ll receive benefits like annual bonus, paid vacation, pension plans, personal insurance, and more. These vary by country and contract, but they’re worth asking about—we think they’re pretty great.


Ready to Make a Difference?

If this role excites you, we’d love to hear from you! Apply now to start the conversation and learn more about where your career can go with us.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or other protected category.

Title: Automationsingeniør - PLC
Description:
Brænder du for PLC‑programmering — og vil du være en del af et fagligt stærkt team, hvor sparring, kvalitet og godt kollegaskab går hånd i hånd?

Så er du måske vores nye automationsingeniør i Ringsted. Vi oplever stigende efterspørgsel og søger en kollega med stærke PLC‑kompetencer til vores team.

Din hverdag som projektingeniør i Init

Som automationsingeniør bliver du en central del af projektteamet, der udvikler skræddersyede automationsløsninger til vores kunder. Du vil arbejde med både store og små projekter af varierende kompleksitet og kan være med i hele projektforløbet – fra indledende kundedialog og design til implementering, test og aflevering.

Dine Primære Opgaver Vil Bl.a. Være At


Designe, specificere, programmere og implementere PLC‑løsninger (og evt. SCADA/HMI ved interesse).
Fejlsøge, optimere og videreudvikle eksisterende programmer og udstyr.
Bidrage til dokumentation, styringsbeskrivelser og procedurer.
Samarbejde tværfagligt både internt og eksternt – for at sikre succesfuld integration af dine automationsløsninger.


Om dig

Vi Søger En Kollega, Som Arbejder Struktureret, Tager Ansvar For Sine Leverancer Og Trives i Et Samarbejdende Team. Du Har Solide PLC‑kompetencer Og Motiveres Af At Udvikle Og Optimere Automationsløsninger, Som Forbedrer Kundernes Drift. Vi Lægger Vægt På, At Du


Har 3+ års praktisk erfaring med PLC‑programmering og gerne projektarbejde.
Har erfaring med Siemens (TIA/Step7) eller Rockwell.
Har relevant teknisk uddannelse f.eks. ingeniør, elektriker, automationsteknolog, maskinmester, eller lign.
Trives i en rolle, hvor du både arbejder selvstændigt og samarbejder tæt med kolleger og kunder
Kommunikerer godt på både dansk og engelsk.


Det Er Derudover En Fordel, Hvis Du Har


Kendskab til Schneider, HMI/SCADA eller industrielle netværk.
Erfaring fra fjernvarme- og spildevandsprojekter


Hvorfor vælge os?

Hos Init Bliver Du En Del Af Et Stærkt Fagligt Fællesskab, Hvor Vi Arbejder Med Spændende Automationsprojekter. Vi Tilbyder Bl.a.


Variererede projekter inden for moderne teknologier og automation.
Et team af dygtige kolleger, som du kan sparre med i hverdagen.
En kultur, hvor vi spiller hinanden gode – med plads til grin og fællesskab.
Nærværende ledelse og fokus på din faglige og personlige udvikling.
En selvstændig rolle med ansvar og stor frihed til at tilrettelægge dit arbejde
Konkurrencedygtig lønpakke


Er du vores nye kollega?

Vi glæder os til at læse dit CV og evt. ansøgning. Vi behandler ansøgninger i august.

Har du spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte Projektchef Nicolai Andersen (nwa@initgroup.com). Grundet sommerferieperiode kan der være længere svartid.

Hvem er Init?

Init har en ambition om at samle de bedste virksomheder – og mennesker - inden for industriel IT og automation. I dag er vi i Danmark mere end 400 medarbejdere fordelt på 21 kontorer. Vi lægger vægt på at give dig et sjovt, sikkert og inkluderende arbejdsmiljø. Vores motto er ”We are Init together” – og det gælder både, når vi samarbejder med kolleger om at gøre Init til en attraktiv arbejdsplads, og når vi samarbejder med kunder for at forme fremtiden inden for automation og IT. Læs mere på Initgroup.com/da

Title: Office Mood Manager
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Copenhagen, Denmark | 50% hours (20 hours per week)

It’s a great chance to gain experience in office culture, event coordination, and HR support.

Puzzel: The Low-Down 🔍

Puzzel is a leading provider of cloud-based contact centre solutions, empowering businesses to deliver exceptional customer service. Our platform combines omnichannel contact centre, workforce management, and AI-driven analytics to optimize customer interactions and operational efficiency.

With 25 years’ experience since our foundation in Norway, we’re already #1 in the Nordics; growing rapidly in the UK and we are already seeing some great success in the Netherlands since opening the entity in mid-2024, but our ambition is to become the clear European market-leader in the coming years 🚀

Why we’re proud


Recognised as an Innovation and Growth Leader in the 2025 EMEA Customer Experience Platforms Frost Radar
2024 CX Awards Winner, ‘Best Mid-Market Contact Center Platform’
Puzzel was recognised as the 'most innovative European-founded CCaaS provider' and top 3 globally by Frost & Sullivan in 2023
Best Practices Company of the Year 2023
Consistently high Glassdoor rating
Puzzel places high importance on work-life balance and flexible working hours - as recognised by Flexa, placing 16th overall for work:life balance in 2025!
We are working with Mercer on salary benchmarking, to ensure pay equality and market competitiveness


Read more about us here.

What You'll Do 🏡💻

Weare looking for a Mood Manager – someone who loves creating a positive, inspiring, and welcoming office environment. Your mission is simple: make our office the place where people feel it is a home away from home. From cozy vibes to fun activities, you’ll be the heartbeat of our workplace culture.

Key Responsibilities2. Culture & Engagement tasksThe must haves 💪The nice to haves ➕➕➕The location


Operational tasks
Handle onboarding/offboarding administration (checklists from managers, communication with IT department etc.)
Act as focal point for all local vendors
Receive, send for approval and upload all incoming local invoices
Occupational health coordination
Perform regular supervision of the premises
Have an overview of and order office supplies
Order services by vendors/landlord etc.
Administrate access cards
Handle inbound and outbound emails
Assist HR team in their day-to-day activities
Carry out other tasks assigned by the line manager
Organize social activities (breakfasts, after-work events, seasonal celebrations)
Create a welcoming and inspiring office atmosphere (plants, décor, music, lighting)
Gather feedback from employees and continuously improve the office vibe
Implement small initiatives that make the office inviting and enjoyable
Strong sense of initiative and ability to work independently
Service-minded and social – you genuinely enjoy making people feel welcome
Organized and detail-oriented, able to juggle multiple tasks
Excellent communication skills in English (Danish is a plus)
Basic knowledge of MS Office (Excel skills are a bonus)
Experience in event planning or office management
Creative flair for design and atmosphere
Interest in wellbeing and team-building activities.


Copenhagen office, with 3 days per week onsite. We are flexible with how you manage your hours, so you can do 3 days of 6 hours 40 minutes/day | 4 days of 5 hours | 5 days of 4 hours – whatever fits you best!

Personal traits we value


Proactive & self-driven – you don’t wait for instructions, you make things happen
Creative & resourceful – you find smart ways to create a great atmosphere
Empathetic & approachable – people feel comfortable coming to you
Positive energy – you lift the mood and inspire others


Reporting line

Reports to Nordics HR Manager (with close collaboration with local leadership)

What’s In it for You? 💰


Valuable hands-on experience in office management and workplace culture
Opportunity to develop organizational and communication skills in a professional setting
Learn how to coordinate events and create an engaging office environment
Be part of a supportive team and contribute to a positive work atmosphere
Flexible working hours adapted to your training plan
A chance to build confidence and prepare for future employment


What to expect from the interview process ❔


Screening call with Talent Acquisition
1st interview with HR Manager Nordic (Hiring Manager)
2nd interview (if needed) to clarify any remaining questions before confirming the start date


Puzzel Values 🎯


Built on Trust – trust is an intrinsic Nordic value, upon which Puzzel has been built. We trust each other and our customers and partners trust us.
Stronger Together – working together in a genuinely collaborative way, with a shared purpose, we have an empowered organisation that is better equipped to delight customers and partners.
Stay Hungry – have a continuous hunger to raise our game, innovate and be the best we can be professionally.


Diversity & Inclusion 🌍

We want everyone at Puzzel to be their true, authentic selves at work irrespective of nationality, race, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, physical ability, age, or economic background.

Whilst we are proud to already have a diverse workforce from across the globe, we are aware that things could always be improved – for example, we currently have a ratio of female 26:74 male employees, which whilst not uncommon for the tech industry (average in SaaS is 26% female), it’s far from ideal. So, what are we doing to improve this?


Our Global Leadership Team (C-suite) has shifted from 100% male to 50 male: 50 female in the past 18 months.
We have increased our % of female hires from 23.6% in 2021 to 38.5% in 2023.
We are partnering with the likes of Flexa, who have significantly higher-than-average talent pools of candidates from diverse backgrounds.
We are working with State Employment initiatives in Norway and Sweden to bring candidates through internship/apprenticeship routes and are investigating similar initiatives in the UK and Bulgaria.
By raising awareness and transparency, we are hoping to further attract a diverse workforce.


We are continuously striving to foster an inclusive and diverse environment, where everyone is celebrated for who they are. If there is anything we can do to support you in the interview process, or beyond, please let us know.

One Last Thing ☝🏻

Even if you feel you are only a 75% match for this role, we still want to hear from you. This list is purely indicative. Skills can always be learnt.

Please note that we are not able to provide sponsorship for this role, so you must have eligibility to work in the country you are applying for.

By applying you accept the terms of our Privacy Notice which can be found on our website. Puzzel are not considering candidates that do not have a work permit in the country we are hiring in.

Title: Front Desk Assistant
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Details

Description

JOB TITLE: Front Desk Assistant

General Summary Of Duties


Greeting patients
Performing check-in procedures
Maintaining current demographic and insurance information in computer and in the chart
Performing office opening procedures, petty cash balancing, co-pay/co-insurance collection, and assisting patients as needed
Primary focus is servicing patients in the lobby with backup for phones and other front office personnel


Requirements


High School Diploma, with 1-2 years experience in healthcare background
Knowledge of clinic policies and procedures
Knowledge of computer systems, programs
Knowledge of medical terminology
Must be able to multi – task
Must be able to express compassion and kindness to patients calling and being seen in the office
Must maintain a professional and upbeat attitude
Skill in written and verbal communication and customer relations
Ability to work with effectively with medical staff, Management, authorizations, external agencies and patients
Requires sitting and standing associated with a normal office environment
Normal busy office environment with much patient contact
Occasional evening or weekend work


Benefits


Employee Assistance Plan
401(k) retirement plan with a company match
And more!


Equal Employment Opportunity & Work Force Diversity

Title: Corporate Affairs Assistant
Place: Listed, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Corporate & Internal Events Administration


Plan and coordinate corporate-wide events, conferences, launches, and internal celebrations.
Ensure seamless logistics, promotions, and stakeholder coordination for both internal and external engagements.
Assist in protocol management and representation of the company in public functions.


Corporate and Community Relations


Assist in building and maintaining strong relationships with key stakeholders, partners, media organizations, and community leaders.
Represent the organization in community engagements and initiatives to strengthen public trust and visibility.
Assist in developing partnerships to expand outreach and impact.


Audience Engagement & Educational Programs


Coordinate with the Editorial team and supervise programs to raise awareness
and appreciation for newspaper readership and media literacy.
Assist in facilitating inquirer educational tours, student interviews, and internship/immersion programs, ensuring informative and engaging experiences for students and academic partners.
Support and oversee initiatives such as TLC (Tours, Leisure and Chow) and Playground for Minds to boost readership and strengthen relationships with hotels and affiliated schools.
Support in the coordination of events for key stakeholders e.g. debates, INQ seminar series, etc.


Inquirer Foundation & CSR Initiatives


Oversee the planning, execution, and reporting of CSR programs in collaboration with the Inquirer Foundation—such as Inqskwela and the Calamity Help Fund—while also developing strategies to solicit donations.
Organize employee volunteerism activities and advocate participation in social impact campaigns.
Assist in CSR-related communications and documentation.


Issues and Crisis Management Assistance


Support the Corporate Communications team during reputational or crisis events by assisting in media coordination, stakeholder communication, and situational reporting.


PR Support and Special Projects


Assist in executing PR strategies through content development and support event visibility, and stakeholder engagement.
Contribute to the successful delivery of cross-functional projects and initiatives as assigned by leadership.


Minimum Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Mass Communications, Public Relations, Marketing, or a related field.
At least 1-2 years of experience in corporate affairs, public relations, events/project management, or CSR programs.
Strong knowledge in the state of business and the industry connected it.
Strong written, interpersonal and verbal communication skills.
Excellent interpersonal and stakeholder engagement abilities.
Strong organizational and multitasking skills.
Excellent understanding of corporate communication, CSR programs, CRM and issues management.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced media environment.
Basic computer operations skills and digital and technology savvy.

Title: E-Business Assistant - internship
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Poste

Do you wish to join the House of Dreams?

In this House, you will continue to perpetuate the dream of our creator: that of a more beautiful and happier world.

You will also consolidate our vision: to become the most desirable House in the world of beauty, an absolute reference of French luxury, a symbol of joy and hope.

This Vision Is Carried By Our Six Values That You Will Embody Daily As Designers Of Dreams - Values That Engage And Define Us

Create Passionately

Craft together with joy

Excel for ultimate luxury

Lead with audacity

Cultivate Triumphant inclusivity

Commit and act to re-enchant the world.

Design your dreams with Dior.

Your Impact

Within the Marketing team, the E-Bussiness Intern is responsible for supporting the daily operations and strategic development of our e-retail and e-commerce initiatives.

This role is crucial for enhancing our online presence, optimizing sales performance across e-retail platforms and our own e-commerce channel, and gaining valuable insights into consumer behavior and market trends.

The holder of this position will work closely with various functional partners, including Marketing & Merchandising Team, Commercial Team, Back Office Team (Finance and Customer Service), Paris Headquarter Digital Departments, and External Partners

Missions

Day-to-Day Missions


Support on the daily operations of both e-retail and e-commerce.
Coordinate the setup of new products to be launched on e-retail websites (data collection, translations, material delivery).
Support on full year eCommerce local activation plan and new feature implementation & testing.
Support on e-trade activations for new launches with key e-retailers (translations, briefing, tracking, result reporting).
Support on retail media activations.
Assist with e-retail brand corner refreshes.
Assist in monthly reports, including analysis of e-retail and e-commerce sales, campaign performance, consumer behavior, and site benchmarks, among other KPIs.
Monitor and analyze ongoing local & global digital trends, competitor websites, benchmarks, and campaigns to ensure up-to-date strategies and insights.
Ad hoc tasks and requests (various updates, corrections on site, ad hoc projects, etc.).
The responsibilities outlined in this job description are subject to change based on the company's needs and ongoing projects


Profil


Ideally, you would have experience in the e-retail , e-commerce, and/or digital marketing domain.
Soft skills that align with our values:


Create Passionately


Creative & Innovative
Initiative
Resourceful
Resilience


Craft Together With Joy


Teamwork & Collaboration
Emotional Intelligence
Communication & Interpersonal skills


Cultivate Triumphant Inclusivity


Learning Agility
Integrity
Empathy


Excel For Ultimate Luxury


Attention to detail
Strategic Thinking
Work Ethic
Numeracy/Analytical


Commit & Act To Re-enchant The World


Change Management
Business Acumen
Adaptability
Problem Solving
Purpose Driven Management


Soft Skills Specifics


Enthusiastic, proactive, and a collaborative team player
Digitally savvy with a passion for emerging digital trends and platforms
Possesses an analytical and data-driven mindset
Strong organizational skills with an entrepreneurial spirit
Excellent communication abilities
Passion for luxury and the beauty sector


Hard Skills


Master’s degree in digital marketing, eCommerce or related field
English (Fluent in writing and speaking) Scandinavian languages are considered a plus
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel and Power Point) and mid-level proficiency in Adobe Pack (Photoshop mainly). Knowledge of PowerBI & Business Manager, Google Analytics platforms is considered a plus
Previous experience in e-retail, eCommerce and/or digital marketing positions will be considered a plus


Information à l'attention des candidats

Only internship agreement - NO Student Job

ID&E Footing

Inclusivity is not just a noble principle; it is the very heart of our excellence. In a world where our clients, talents, and ideas increasingly come from diverse backgrounds, our diversity is our strength.

At Parfums Christian Dior, this means being a House where everyone feels respected, welcomed, and valued, regardless of their uniqueness. Because we know well: a House that reflects this diversity is a sustainable, vibrant House, rooted in both dreams and reality. Inclusivity cannot be decreed; it is practiced daily.

(If you require any reasonable adjustments within the application process, feel free to indicate it in your application).

Informations à l'attention des candidats internes

Are you currently an LVMH employee? Please apply online directly on our internal job board: WORK @LVMH - Voices

Only internship agreement - NO Student Job

Etapes de recrutement

Submit your application via our website. A recruiter will review your application. If your profile aligns with the requirements defined by our Hiring Team, you will be contacted for an initial HR interview. Subsequently, you will have an interview with the Hiring Manager.

only internship agreement - NO Student Job

Compétences à développer


Soft and Hard Skills you will develop in this role.
Project Management: You will learn to manage multiple projects simultaneously, from planning to execution, in a fast-paced environment.
Digital Marketing Expertise: You will gain hands-on experience in e-retail strategies, content optimization, and online brand management.
Stakeholder Communication: You will enhance your communication and collaboration skills by working with various internal teams, external agencies, and retailers.
Adobe Creative Suite Proficiency: You will develop or refine your skills in Photoshop and InDesign for creating engaging digital assets.
Potential Career Path From this role
E-Commerce Specialist
Digital Marketing Coordinator
Brand Marketing Assistant
Junior E-Business Manager

Title: Business Intelligence Consultant
Company: SameSystem - Retail WFM | Part of ECIT
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
BI Consultant




Please note that fluency in Danish is a requirement for this position.

Due to the summer holidays, please expect a longer processing time.




SameSystem

Are you ready to turn data into real commercial value for some of Europe's largest retail and hospitality brands? At SameSystem, we empower companies like Bestseller, Nike, Normal, and Matas to optimize their operations daily with our cutting-edge AI-driven Workforce Management Solution. We're looking for a BI Consultant who is as comfortable building a clean dashboard as sitting across the table from a customer explaining what it means for their business.

This is a full-time role, and you'll join our Customer Success team in Copenhagen. You'll own the business intelligence and reporting side of our platform: building and maintaining reports in our BI tool, Sisense, and being the person our customers and colleagues turn to when a number needs to make sense. We've deliberately placed this role in Customer Success rather than in a back-office data team, because the reporting is only worth as much as the commercial insight it drives. This role suits someone who is happy behind the screen building things properly, but who does not want to only sit behind the screen.

This is a new role, so the right person will have real influence over how we build, package, and deliver the analytics side of our platform.




Key responsibilities

Building the reports

Design, build, and maintain BI reports and dashboards in Sisense, with strong attention to data integrity, performance, and usability
Translate customer and internal requirements into robust data models and reporting logic
Own reporting quality across accounts, including validation, troubleshooting, and iterative improvement
Develop new reporting capabilities and scope custom development work as our customers' needs grow




Working with customers

Join customer meetings and represent the BI and analytics part of the SameSystem platform
Talk specific development and scoping with customers, and translate their business questions into what we can actually build
Help customers see the value in their data, and support Customer Success and Sales in retention, upselling, and expansion through analytics
Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Sales, and Support to feed customer needs back into the platform




What we're looking for

Proven experience building reports and dashboards in a BI tool. Experience with Sisense is a strong plus; solid experience with a comparable tool (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, Qlik) is very welcome
Enough experience under your belt to own your work end to end
Strong SQL and data modelling skills, and the instinct to build things properly rather than just quickly
Commercial acumen. You understand that a report exists to drive a decision, and you're comfortable talking value and scope with customers
Confidence in client-facing settings, presenting clearly to both operational and managerial stakeholders
The balance we keep coming back to: someone who can do focused, hands-on build work and also step into a customer meeting and hold their own
A self-starter who takes ownership and isn't afraid to share ideas. Execution matters
Nice to have: comfort scoping and pricing development work. If pricing is new to you, that's fine, we can teach it
Fluency in Danish and English; additional European languages are a plus
Familiarity with retail operations or workforce management is a plus




What you'll get

At SameSystem, we are committed to transforming retail and hospitality through cutting-edge technology. Joining our team means:

Working in a dynamic environment where innovation drives success.
Contributing to the success of over 185,000 employees across 12,500+ stores in 24 countries.
Access to professional growth opportunities in a company that values expertise and collaboration.
Being part of a supportive culture that prioritizes work-life balance and employee satisfaction.
Lots of social activities and fun parties as well as "clubs" run by employees where SameSystem sponsors going to a sauna, playing badminton, drinking port wine, or whatever else the team can think of.
Lunch and as much coffee, soda or energy drink as you can muster.




If you have any questions about this position, please reach out to Nikolai May Yde at nikolai.yde@samesystem.com.
