Title: Regnskabskonsulent
Place: Odense, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Til velrenommeret regnskabskontor i Odense

dense

Hos Nomia i Odense finder du en dynamisk virksomhed, hvor vi drives af fællesskab, frihed og ansvar. Vi lægger høj vægt på det personlige ansvar og den personlige udvikling, og vi brænder for at levere enestående service.

Vi er ambitiøse, og vi vil gerne være dem kunderne går til, når de har brug for hjælp.

Om Nomia

Nomia er eksperter i bogføring, lønadministration og tilsvarende services i alle aspekter af en virksomheds økonomifunktion.

Vi er et team af dedikerede bogholdere med kompetente kolleger fordelt på kontorer landet over.

Vi tilbyder vores kunder en personlig og nærværende regnskabsmæssig assistance, hvilket skaber en stærk relation baseret på tillid og tryghed.

Med “Overskud til dig” mener vi fokus på at skabe værdi og positive resultater for kunder og medarbejdere.

Om stillingen

Sammen med et team af servicemindede kollegaer, der vil gøre alt for at hjælpe deres kunder i en travl hverdag, kommer du til at servicere små- og mellemstore virksomheder med alle aspekter af deres økonomiopgaver.

Du organiserer selv din arbejdsdag, og du får dine egne kunder, hvor du har ansvaret for:


Bogføring og afstemninger
Lønadministration
Momsregnskaber
Klargøring til revisor samt rapporteringsanalyser til dine kunder


Du kommer til at arbejde med processen omkring elektronisk håndtering af bilag via e-conomic, Uniconta, Corpay One og Pleo samt bruge systemer med automatiske integrationsprocesser og analyseværktøjer.

Er Nomia Odense noget for dig?

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller vil du læse mere, så har du masser af muligheder

Ansættelse sker i samarbejde med TARGET Headhunting, og har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller vil du læse mere, så har du masser af muligheder via linket herunder.

Læs den fulde beskrivelse her (åbner i nyt vindue hos Target Headhunting)

Det er en unik stilling i et nærende arbejdsmiljø, og vi glæder os til at høre fra dig.

Læs mere om Nomia i Odense

Title: Data Analyst - 10-month maternity cover
Description:
Driving Digital Transformation Through Insightful Analytics – Viby J.

Do you enjoy turning complex data into trusted insights that help an international business make better decisions? We are looking for a Data Analyst to join STARK Group IT in a 10-month maternity cover position from 1 October 2026 until 31 July 2027. As part of one of Northern Europe's largest digital transformation programmes, you will help turn business needs into trusted data, actionable insights, and scalable analytics solutions used across STARK Group.

If you are motivated by creating clarity from complexity and enjoy diving into data with a delivery focused mindset, this could be the role for you.

This role is based in Viby J, Aarhus.

Your Impact And What You Will Deliver

You will join the STARK Group IT Data Management team, consisting of four Data Analysts and two Data Management Specialists, where you will collaborate closely with experienced colleagues and contribute to continuously strengthening our data management and data analysis capabilities.

While you will be part of the Data Management team, your day-to-day work will be closely integrated with project teams and business stakeholders. The role follows a hybrid working model with three days per week in the office and two days working from home. To support collaboration and knowledge sharing, the Data Management team works together from the office every Tuesday. The remaining office days are typically spent alongside your assigned project team and key stakeholders.

In this role, you will work closely with Product Owners, business stakeholders, architects, data engineers, and BI developers. You will play a key role in translating business needs into data requirements, identifying and validating data sources, establishing source-to-target mappings, and helping ensure a common understanding of business rules, KPIs, and data definitions across business and technical teams.

A key assignment during this maternity cover will be supporting reporting initiatives within Athene, STARK Group's large-scale digital transformation programme consisting of 16 interconnected projects across the business. Working across multiple projects and stakeholders, you will help ensure that reporting needs are understood early, data dependencies are identified, and future reporting solutions are supported by reliable and well-structured data.

In this role you will:


Translate business needs into clear data requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria, and supporting documentation.
Identify, assess, and validate data sources, source-to-target mappings and data transformation logic together with business and technical stakeholders.
Use SQL and analytical tools to explore, analyse, and validate data while identifying patterns, discrepancies, and data quality issues.
Define and document KPIs, metrics, calculation logic, business rules, and data definitions to ensure a shared understanding across teams.
Support Agile delivery activities such as backlog refinement, sprint planning, and change assessments by providing analytical input and data expertise.


You will be successful in this role when stakeholders experience you as a trusted analytical partner who brings structure, clarity, and practical insight into complex data topics. By ensuring that requirements are actionable solutions, data mappings and definitions are well understood, and data is reliable and fit for purpose, you help create confidence in both the data and the decisions built upon it.

What You Bring

We are looking for someone who combines strong analytical skills with curiosity, drive, and a desire to make an impact from day one. You are comfortable navigating ambiguity, asking questions when requirements need clarification, and balancing business value, data quality, and pragmatic delivery.

You bring:


Experience working as a Data Analyst, Business Analyst, or in a similar data-focused role, preferably within a complex or international organization
Strong analytical skills, including experience working with requirements gathering, data validation, data mapping data quality, KPIs, metrics, and business rules.
Solid SQL skills and experience analysing and validating data.
Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to communicate effectively across business and technical stakeholders.
A structured, collaborative, and delivery-focused mindset, with the ability to work independently and drive analysis activities from clarification to implementation.


You will work with tools including Azure Databricks, Power BI, Azure DevOps, Kafka, and Open Metadata (Data catalog). To set you up for success in this role, we expect you to bring hands-on experience with several of these technologies and the ability to quickly apply your knowledge in a business context.

In addition, experience with Python, ServiceNow, Scrum or Kanban, or finance-related business processes is an advantage but not a requirement. We expect you to hold a relevant Masters degree within IT, Data & Analytics, Business Administration, Finance, Engineering, or a related field. Equivalent practical experience may also be considered.

Welcome to Group IT

When you join Group IT, you join a collaborative international organisation where technology and business go hand in hand. We are replacing legacy systems and building a modern, modular IT landscape to support STARK Group's continued growth. You will become part of a community that values knowledge sharing, professionalism, and building solutions that make a clear difference to colleagues across Europe.

Want to know more and apply?

We will be happy to answer any questions you may have regarding the position and your options in STARK Group. You are welcome to contact Kristine Grann at Kristine.grann@stark.dk for more information.

As many of our colleagues will be on summer holiday throughout July, please do not expect your application to be reviewed during this period. We will resume the recruitment process in early to mid-August.

STARK Group 

STARK Group is a growing private equity-owned company with a strong legacy. STARK Group was first established in Denmark in 1896 and has today evolved into a leading European retailer and distributor of heavy building materials with net sales of approx. EUR 9 billion. With a dedicated team of more than 20,000 employees and +1,150 branches and distribution centres across the Nordics, Greenland, Germany, Austria and the UK, we live and breathe to serve professional craftsmen with heavy building materials all the way from the Austrian Alps to Tromsø, close to the Arctic circle and everywhere in between. STARK Group is committed to reach net zero emissions by 2050 across the entire business, is a signatory to the UN Global Compact, and works within fully 2030 validated science-based targets to keep global warming below 1.5°C At STARK Group IT, we believe in the richness diversity brings. We invite individuals of all backgrounds to join us in creating and inclusive and welcoming environment. We believe in a workplace where everyone can bring their authentic selves to work. 

 

Agency Policy 

STARK Group IT appreciates the interest of all candidates. However, we prefer to directly engage with individuals seeking employment opportunities. Therefore, we kindly request recruitment agencies, head-hunters, and third-party staffing services to refrain from contacting us regarding this job posting. All applications should be submitted directly by the candidates through our official application process. We value direct communication and look forward to reviewing your application.

Title: Sommerferieafløser
Place: Helsingør, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Velkommen til en værdidrevet organisation!

Vi søger timeafløsere, samt sommerferie afløsere


Det er både dag/aften og nattevagter
Vi søger vagter, som kan tiltræde, i alle vagtlag.
vagterne vil blive lagt ud i vores omsorgsbemanding
kørekort er ikke et krav.


Passer beskrivelsen på dig? Ansøg i dag!

Du vil gerne arbejde på en arbejdsplads hvor du og dit team kan gøre en forskel og dit engagement værdsat. Du nyder at skabe gode relationer till beboerne og vil gerne skabe værdi for dem og deres pårørende.

Du vil beskrive dig selv som en serviceorienteret og ansvarlig person, der brænder for at arbejde med ældre.

Krav


Du behøver ikke at være faglært for at kunne søge en stilling som ufaglært hos os, men må meget gerne have kendskab til pleje af mennesker.


Du skal kunne tale og forstå dansk.

Vi arbejder i et journalsystem, som hedder Nexus, og her er det et krav, at du kan gå ind og læse om den beboer du skal ind til, da der på alle vores beboere er skrevet handlingsanvisning for at sikre en kvalitets sikret pleje.

Det er vigtigt at du er mødestabil, og samarbejder godt på tværs i vores hus

Det er, hvad vi kan tilbyde dig


mulighed for at få en masse vagter på et stort center og skabe gode relationer
digitale arbejdsredskaber
ambitiøst og struktureret kvalitetsarbejde
flad organisation med korte beslutningsbeføjelser
Mulighed for at udvikle dig fagligt


Om Attendo

Attendo er Nordens største leverandør af velfærdsydelser til personer med handicap samt omsorg til udsatte børn og deres familier samt ældrepleje. Vi er kendt som pioner inden for velfærdsydelser og har fokus på, at vores borgere får den omsorg, hjælp og tryghed, de har behov for. Attendo var bl.a. den første i branchen til at udgive et kvalitetsregnskab, hvor alle virksomhedens kvalitetsdata er samlet. Alle medarbejdere er involveret i kvalitetsarbejdet, der drejer sig om at udføre, følge op og udvikle på kvaliteten i hele virksomheden.

Attendo beskæftiger cirka 24.000 mennesker i Danmark, Sverige, Norge og Finland, heraf cirka 250 i Danmark. Hos Attendo er borgerne i centrum. Vi er her for dem, og uden dem var vi her ikke. Derfor tænker vi service, pleje og hensyn ind i hele vores hverdag. Det gælder også de af os, der ikke har den nære kontakt med borgerne. Derfor er det helt naturligt for os at være udadvendte, opmærksomme og smilende overfor for såvel borgere som pårørende.

Attendos værdier er omsorg, kompetence og engagement.

Søg stillingen

Vi behandler løbende ansøgningerne og ansætter, så snart vi har fundet den rigtige profil. Læs mere om Attendo på www.attendo.dk og send din ansøgning på job.attendo.dk. Ønsker du at høre mere, kan du kontakte virksomhedschef

Louise Ivanhoe Nielsen

Louise.nielsen@attendo.dk

telefon: 26772588

Eller souschef

Kristine Jensen

kristine.jensen@attendo.dk

26759577

Title: Multimedia Reporter (Entertainment)
Place: Listed, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Writes daily entertainment news and/or features, local and foreign, based on relevant topics, current trends, story requests and/or assigned coverages.
Regularly produces entertainment special reports and long/short features; pitches topics and/or works on topics assigned.
Attends all assigned entertainment events for coverage, whether face-to-face or online (ex. beauty pageants, movie premieres, press conferences, project launches, etc.), and must write multiple breaking reports and/or special features from these; must also be on the lookout for upcoming showbiz-related events and pitch these for coverage.
Can do live on-camera reporting of any assigned event; has very good live interview skills; must be visually presentable with a good reportorial voice
Always willing to work on a flexible and frequently-changing schedule; covers ALL assigned events even on weekends and holidays, or in the late evenings to early morning
Assists the editors and fellow writers on tasks that may be assigned on occasion (copyediting, layout, photo selection, monitoring, sourcing, research, coordination with columnists/contributors, etc.)


Minimum Qualifications


Graduate of broadcasting, journalism, multimedia studies, performing arts, and/or film/music with excellent reportorial skills.
Interested in entertainment, most especially Philippine showbiz and Filipino celebrities
Basic knowledge of videography and photography
Basic SEO and CMS knowledge
Internet and social media savvy
Must have excellent command of English language, both for writing stories and speaking on-cam

Title: Data Quality Analyst
Description:
Location: The locations for this role are Newport, Titchfield (Fareham), and Darlington.

We operate a flexible hybrid working model across the UK, with colleagues linked to one of our contractual locations and working between office and remote throughout the week. As part of the hybrid working arrangement there is a 40% minimum office attendance requirement.

Salary: £34,587

Working Patterns: All our vacancies are offered as a flexible option of Full time, Part time, Flexible working, Job Share

Closing Date: Apply before 11:55pm on Monday 3rd August 2026







Important - For Office for National Statistics (ONS) the requirement for SC clearance is to have been present in the UK for 3 consecutive years immediately prior to applying.







Person specification

Essential Criteria:

Strong experience in data management, data analysis, or data engineering that can be applied to assessing and improving data quality.
Strong communication and influencing skills. This includes the ability to present on technical subjects to a range of audiences, influence stakeholders, and clearly brief more senior management.
Experience of working with different stakeholders from different parts of the organisation and with different roles, building positive collaborative working relationships.
Actively seeks opportunities to improve services and ways of working, demonstrating innovation and adaptability, and delivering positive, measurable improvements to performance and outcomes.
Experience of working with government data, and/or with official statistics in accordance with the Code of Practice for Statistics.







APPLICATION PROCESS

The application process requires writing a Personal Statement. Your personal statement should be no longer than 1250 words. You should use this space fully to provide evidence for each Essential Criteria listed in the Person Specification. As the criteria are scored, we would recommend that you give clear examples for each including the impact of your actions using the STAR method.

• Situation - the situation you had to deal with

• Task - the task you were given to do

• Action - the action you took

• Result - what happened as a result of your action and what you learned from the experience.







In return we offer you:

✔Hybrid working and flexi-time so you can work around your life, not the other way around!
✔A market leading pension scheme - our employer contribution rate is around 29%
✔A choice of working patterns *for every role* full-time, part-time, job-share.
✔Maternity, adoption or shared parental leave of 26 weeks full pay (subject to qualifying criteria)
✔Opportunities to learn new technology & skills on the job, utilising blocked out Protected Learning Time in your weekly schedule and taking advantage of the support of our Communities of Practice
✔Employee Assistance Programmes
✔Diversity Network Groups
✔Mental Health Allies
✔Civil Service Sports and Social club
✔Generous holiday allowance – 25 days annual leave, rising to 30 days after 5 years service in addition to 9 public holidays




For more information about this role, and to apply, please hit APPLY to be taken to Civil Service Jobs.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking to recruit an additional Data Analyst to support our systems and improvement work in Plymouth City Council’s Adults, Health and Communities Directorate. Reporting to the Assurance and Service Improvement Manager, you will support the effective collection, recording, management and use of data.

Working with highly complex and wide-ranging data, you will develop and embed effective data management systems. You will also support others to be able to interpret and use the data effectively to support their work in providing the best possible services for Adult Social Care. This will include contributing to the Directorate’s digitalisation plan and Power BI reporting.

A key focus of this post is in maintaining and developing the departments use of data to support informing future commissioning intentions and ensuring compliance with the CQC Assurance framework for Local Authority Assessments.

Please note This is an office based role and the expectation is that the successful candidate will be office based for 3 days per week at Crownhill Court Plymouth.

We are a flexible employer and provide a variety of working arrangements tailored to the needs of each role.

Plymouth City Council is an equal opportunities employer. All applicants will be considered for employment regardless of age, care experience, disability, gender reassignment, marriage/civil partnership, pregnancy/maternity, race, religion/belief, sex or sexual orientation.

As part of our commitment to promoting equality we offer guaranteed interviews to those who meet the essential criteria and are considered disabled, care experienced or are an Armed Forces Service Leaver within the last two years.

Please note, this role is not eligible for visa sponsorship and we can only consider applicants with existing right to work in the UK

For a conversation about the role please contact the Assurance and Service Improvement Manager, Jodie.Myles@plymouth.gov.uk

Closing Date: Tuesday 4 August

Title: Logistics Data Analyst
Description:
Overview

Permanent

Up to £50,000 + Pension + PMI + ShareSave + 6.6 weeks holiday + Hybrid Working ( 1per week in the office)

Southampton, Store Support Office

We believe anyone can improve their home to make life better. From our Southampton Store Support office (SSO) we equip our stores, our people, and our whole business with everything it takes to help our millions of customers create a home they’ll love. Join us as a Logistics Analyst and you’ll be a big part of this.

The wider Analytics & Data Science team is growing to support more areas of the business. As part of this, we’re creating new analyst roles focused on Logistics .

In this role, you’ll work closely with stakeholders to understand real business challenges, translate them into analytical problems, and deliver insight that drives measurable impact—whether that’s improving availability, optimising stock flow, or enhancing operational efficiency.

You’ll be part of a collaborative, forward-thinking analytics community that values curiosity, creativity, and continuous improvement. We encourage new ideas , experimentation, and smart risk-taking to evolve how we use data to support better decisions across our operations.

What's the job?


Use data from logistics network (HD and retail) and from outside the network to uncover insights that drive better business decisions and help solve logistics strategy problems
Translate complex datasets into clear, compelling stories and actionable recommendations for operational stakeholders
Engag e with the  logistics  operation to ensure a grounded knowledge and to make sure analysis and the operation are in alignment 
Tackle a wide range of problems, from performance optimisation to understanding availability drivers
Deliver deep-dive analysis, highlighting risks, opportunities and areas for continuous improvement
Build strong relationships with stakeholders, influencing and supporting decision-making at all levels
Work collaboratively with analysts and data scientists to deliver scalable and impactful analytical solutions
Contribute to a culture of continuous improvement, innovation and data-driven thinking across supply chain and logistics
Keeping up to date with best practice in the logistics sector, understanding emerging technology and having a view about how it could be applied in the B&Q network to improve efficiency, capacity or service 


What We Need


Experience working with Supply Chain and/or Logistics in an analytical role
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with experience working with complex datasets
Proficiency in SQL, with experience in Python and/or R (desirable)
Experience working in cloud-based data environments (e.g. Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery )
Proven ability to translate data into clear insights and compelling, actionable recommendations
Creating and delivering presentations communicating the problem, context, potential and recommended solutions
Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build relationships across operational and commercial teams
Curiosity, creativity, and a willingness to challenge the status quo to improve ways of working
A proactive mindset — comfortable taking ownership, testing ideas, and driving improvements
High attention to detail and a strong commitment to data quality and integrity
Confident decision maker


What's in it for me?

As well as a competitive salary, our benefits package includes:


Award-winning pension scheme
Bonus
ShareSave options
6.6 weeks holiday
Payroll giving
Employee Assistance Programme
Shopping discounts
Colleague wellbeing benefits …and much more.


We welcome applications from all candidates; however, please note that we are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role, and applicants must have the right to work in the UK.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
📊 Data Analyst | Boots Media Group at SMG

📍 Hybrid - Nottingham (2 days per week)

💰 From £40,000 + 10% Bonus




Do you love solving problems with data?




Are you someone who gets genuine satisfaction from writing a great SQL query, uncovering the story behind the numbers and turning insights into meaningful business decisions?




If so, we'd love to hear from you.




We're looking for a Data Analyst to join our award-winning partnership with Boots Media Group, helping some of the UK's biggest brands understand exactly how their omnichannel campaigns are performing.




This isn't just another reporting role.




You'll be working with Boots Advantage Card data, analysing campaign effectiveness, uncovering customer insights and helping shape the future of retail media. You'll collaborate with Data Scientists, Agency teams and Boots stakeholders to deliver analysis that genuinely influences marketing decisions.




What you'll be doing:

Writing SQL queries to extract and analyse large datasets.
Transforming complex data into clear, meaningful insights.
Managing agency data requests and becoming a trusted point of contact.
Identifying trends, answering commercial questions and uncovering the "so what?" behind the numbers.
Building client-ready reports and presenting findings with confidence.
Working alongside Insights, Agency and Boots teams to improve campaign performance for leading global brands.




We'd love to hear from you if you have...

✔ Strong SQL experience and confidence querying large datasets.

✔ Experience cleaning, manipulating and analysing data.

✔ A naturally curious mindset with excellent problem-solving skills.

✔ The ability to communicate technical findings to non-technical audiences.

✔ Python experience is a bonus, but not essential.




Why SMG?

At SMG, we're changing the way brands, retailers and shoppers connect.

We've been leading the retail media space since 2008 and continue to push the boundaries with cutting-edge technology, data and innovation. We're proud to be recognised as the #1 Best Marketing & Media Agency, and we're just getting started.




We're looking for people who enjoy building, improving and challenging the status quo. People who thrive in fast-moving environments, embrace change and want to keep learning.




What's in it for you?

🎁 10% discretionary bonus

💚 £1,800 annual wellbeing fund

📚 £500 annual learning fund

🧠 Free Headspace subscription

🌴 25 days holiday + bank holidays + 4 wellbeing days

⏰ 4pm finishes every Friday

🏡 Flexible hybrid working

🎉 Summer & Winter conferences, regular socials and celebrations throughout the year

And so much more.




Don't tick every box? Please apply anyway.




We're passionate about building diverse teams and know that great people don't always match every requirement on paper. If this role excites you, we'd love to hear your story.




Ready to use data to shape the future of retail media? Apply today.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Business Intelligence Analyst

Kilmarnock, Ayrshire | Full-Time | Permanent




We're looking for a commercially minded Business Intelligence Analyst to play a pivotal role in transforming data into meaningful insights that drive business performance. This is an exciting opportunity to join a growing multi-site business where data is at the heart of strategic and operational decision-making.




As part of a significant investment in data, reporting, and analytics capabilities, you'll lead the development of a central data warehouse and help establish a single source of truth across the organisation. Working closely with Finance, Operations, IT, and senior stakeholders, you'll influence how data is captured, managed, and leveraged to support growth.




What You'll Be Doing




Build Strong Data Foundations

Design and develop a central data warehouse integrating multiple business systems.
Create scalable data models and structures to support reporting and analytics.
Improve data quality, governance, and consistency across the organisation.
Work with internal teams and external providers to develop and maintain data pipelines.




Deliver Insight That Drives Performance

Build dashboards and reporting solutions covering key operational and financial metrics.
Analyse trends, risks, and opportunities to support strategic decision-making.
Enable self-service reporting while maintaining robust data governance.
Present complex findings in a clear, actionable way for non-technical stakeholders.




Support Commercial Growth

Partner with Finance and Operations teams to understand business performance.
Support budgeting, forecasting, investment decisions, and expansion projects.
Identify opportunities to improve profitability, efficiency, and customer outcomes through data.




Drive Innovation

Explore AI-enabled analytics, automation, and predictive modelling opportunities.
Support the transition from historical reporting to forward-looking insights.
Help prepare data infrastructure for future AI and advanced analytics initiatives.




What We're Looking For




Essential

Proven experience in a Business Intelligence, Data Analyst, or similar analytics role.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with structured datasets.
Experience designing or working with data warehouses and centralised data models.
Advanced Excel and analytical skills.
Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualisation tools.
Ability to communicate complex data clearly to a wide range of stakeholders.




Desirable

Experience within hospitality, retail, or other multi-site environments.
Exposure to cloud data platforms such as Azure, AWS, or Snowflake.
Experience working with data from systems such as EPOS, CRM, reservations, payroll, or finance platforms.
Knowledge of forecasting, predictive analytics, or AI-driven solutions.




About You




You are:

Curious and commercially focused.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and evolving priorities.
Passionate about creating structure and improving processes.
Detail-oriented, with the ability to simplify complexity.
Enthusiastic about innovation, automation, and continuous improvement.




Why Join Us?




Opportunity to build and shape a modern BI function.
Significant influence on business-wide decision-making.
Exposure to senior stakeholders and strategic projects.
A forward-thinking environment investing in data, technology, and AI.
Competitive salary and benefits package.




Ready to turn data into impact? Apply today and help build the foundations for smarter business decisions.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Data Analyst

Up to £50,000 + benefits

Newcastle – Hybrid (3 days in office)




We are partnering with a well-established and growing organisation that is continuing to invest in its data and technology capabilities. With data playing an increasingly important role in business decision-making, they are looking to expand their analytics function with the addition of a Data Analyst.




This role will sit within a collaborative technology team and focus on turning complex data into meaningful insights that support operational performance, reporting and strategic initiatives. You will work closely with stakeholders across the business, helping to understand requirements, develop reporting solutions and uncover trends that drive informed decision-making.




The position offers exposure to modern reporting technologies, cloud-based data sources and large datasets, making it an excellent opportunity for someone looking to develop their analytical skills within a forward-thinking environment.




This opportunity is ideal for a Data Analyst who enjoys working with stakeholders, creating impactful visualisations and using data to solve real business challenges.




Responsibilities

Analyse and interpret data from multiple sources to deliver actionable business insights
Write SQL queries to extract, manipulate and analyse data from enterprise systems
Work with stakeholders to gather reporting requirements and translate business needs into analytical solutions
Develop and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI
Present insights, trends and recommendations through clear visualisations
Perform detailed analysis to identify patterns, anomalies and opportunities for business improvement
Deliver training and support to users to maximise adoption of reporting tools and dashboards




Skills and Experience

Previous experience working as a Data Analyst or in a similar analytical role
Strong SQL skills with experience extracting and analysing data from relational databases
Experience developing dashboards and visualisations using Power BI
Experience working with cloud-based data sources
Ability to translate complex data into meaningful business insights for non-technical stakeholders
Understanding of data modelling




What's on Offer

Salary up to £50,000
Opportunity to join a growing and data-driven organisation
Exposure to modern analytics, reporting and cloud technologies
Work with large and varied datasets across multiple business functions
Collaborative and supportive team environment
Ongoing professional development and learning opportunities

Title: Retail Media - Data Analyst
Description:
Overview

Permanent

Up to £50,000 pa + Pension + PMI + ShareSave + 6.6 weeks holiday + Hybrid Working (1 days per week in the office)

Southampton, Store Support Office

We believe anyone can improve their home to make life better. From our Southampton Store Support office (SSO) we equip our stores, our people, and our whole business with everything it takes to help our millions of customers create a home they’ll love. Join us as a Retail Media Analyst and you’ll be a big part of this.

What's the job?


Deliver high-quality analysis across Retail Media, CRM and Loyalty with a clear focus on customer behaviour, audience performance, and commercial outcomes.
Build and evaluate customer audiences and segments for Retail Media use cases, supporting activation through group-supported platforms and partner channels.
Measure and report on campaign performance, including incrementality, uplift, ROI, and contribution to sales, margin, and customer engagement.
Deliver post-investment reviews (PIRs) for CRM and Retail Media activity, identifying what worked, what didn’t, and what should change next time.
Support the definition and tracking of customer health metrics, including acquisition, engagement, frequency, churn, and long-term value.
Partner with Retail Media teams to help demonstrate the value of first-party data to internal and external stakeholders, including suppliers.
Produce clear, decision-ready insights that influence campaign design, audience strategy, and prioritisation, not just reporting for reporting’s sake.
Work within a matrix of analysts and data specialists, contributing to shared analytical products and scalable approaches rather than one-off solutions.
Support the growth of B&Q’s Retail Media proposition with robust insight and performance measurement.
Bring external perspective on Retail Media, CRM, and personalisation, keeping up with industry trends, competitor activity, and emerging best practice.


What We Need


Previous experience gained from working within the Retail industry is essential
You can write complex SQL queries and have had experience working with other languages such as Python or R.
You have experience of marketing campaign design and analysis, working with customer data to find key insights to inform and drive change within the business.
You can design, execute, and evaluate marketing campaigns, optimise customer journeys, and increase marketing ROI
You can also use statistical methods such as hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, and p-values to draw valid conclusions and recommendations
You have familiarity with cloud-based analytics platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake, or BigQuery.
You are comfortable working in a matrix and agile environment, adapting to changing priorities


What's in it for me?

As well as a competitive salary, our benefits package includes:


Award-winning pension scheme
Bonus
ShareSave options
6.6 weeks holiday
Payroll giving
Employee Assistance Programme
Shopping discounts
Colleague wellbeing benefits …and much more.


We welcome applications from all candidates; however, please note that we are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role, and applicants must have the right to work in the UK.

  • You can write complex SQL queries and have had experience working with other languages such as Python or R.
  • You have experience of marketing campaign design and analysis, working with customer data to find key insights to inform and drive change within the business.
  • You can design, execute, and evaluate marketing campaigns, optimise customer journeys, and increase marketing ROI
  • You can also use statistical methods such as hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, and p-values to draw valid conclusions and recommendations
  • You have familiarity with cloud-based analytics platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake, or BigQuery.
  • You are comfortable working in a matrix and agile environment, adapting to changing priorities

What's in it for me?

As well as a competitive salary, our benefits package includes:

  • Award-winning pension scheme
  • Bonus
  • ShareSave options
  • 6.6 weeks holiday
  • Payroll giving
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Shopping discounts
  • Colleague wellbeing benefits …and much more.

We welcome applications from all candidates; however, please note that we are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role, and applicants must have the right to work in the UK.

Equality and Inclusion

We want to ensure that all employees, future employees, and applicants to all Kingfisher companies are treated equally regardless of age, gender, marital or civil partnership status, colour, ethnic or national origin, culture, religious belief, philosophical belief, political opinion, disability, gender identity, gender expression, or sexual orientation.

Find out more about us, including the benefits we offer and our community, sustainability, and inclusion projects at bandqcareers.com.

If you require any additional support or adjustments to help you make an application, please contact us at:

  • ssorecruitment@b-and-q.co.uk
  • recruitment@b-and-q.co.uk

Title: Data Analytics Engineer
Description:
We are looking for a highly skilled Data Analytics Engineer to support product analytics, product marketing, customer support, and operational teams. This role enables data-driven decision-making by developing scalable data models and delivering high-quality insights across the business. The position works cross-functionally with engineering, data science, and business stakeholders and plays a key role in ensuring the accessibility, quality, and reliability of data.

The successful candidate will become a specialist in our modern data stack, including Snowflake, dbt, Looker, and Hightouch, and will contribute to building a robust analytics capability at scale.

In This Role You Will

Data Modeling & Engineering


Build and maintain scalable data models using dbt and Snowflake
Transform raw data into clean, reliable datasets for analysis
Apply engineering best practices to analytics code to ensure scalability and maintainability


Insight & Business Impact


Translate data models into actionable insights for stakeholders across product, marketing, and operations
Partner with business teams to define analytics requirements and measurable success outcomes


Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement


Work collaboratively with data engineers, data scientists, and business stakeholders
Support cross-functional teams by delivering high-quality data solutions


Data Governance & Quality


Maintain data documentation, definitions, and lineage
Advocate for and uphold data quality and coding standards through code reviews


Tools & Technology


Utilise Snowflake, dbt, Looker, Git, and modern data tooling to deliver analytics solutions


Core Competencies For Success


Collaborates – Builds strong partnerships across technical and business teams to achieve shared outcomes
Drives Results – Consistently delivers high-quality outputs in a fast-paced, data-driven environment
Ensures Accountability – Takes ownership of data quality, accuracy, and delivery standards
Tech Savvy – Adopts and applies modern data technologies to improve efficiency and scalability
Manages Complexity – Analyses complex datasets and translates them into clear, actionable insights


Credentials And Experience


3+ years of experience in a data analyst, analytics engineer, data engineer, or similar role
2+ years of experience working with product and/or customer data
3+ years of hands-on experience writing and optimising SQL for data analysis and transformation
Experience using Python, R, or similar tools for data analysis and manipulation
1–3 years of experience working with modern data warehouses and transformation tools (e.g., Snowflake, dbt)
Experience transforming raw data into clean, production-ready datasets and maintaining technical documentation
Experience working with large-scale datasets (e.g., high-volume event or behavioural data)
Exposure to API integrations and data ingestion processes is desirable
Demonstrated ability to work effectively in a remote or distributed team environment
Legal right to work in the United Kingdom


This role is hybrid based in Bath, UK. As the office is a listed building, we do not have a lift so please make us aware so we can make necessary accommodations.

The hiring range for this position is between £35,000 - £39,400 annually, however, base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and location. Additionally, this position is eligible for an Annual Bonus.

At Discovery Education, we are fulfilling our mission of preparing learners for tomorrow by creating innovative classrooms connected to today's world.

Serving more than 4.5 million educators and 45 million students, available in approximately half of U.S. classrooms and primary schools in the UK, and more than 140 countries around the globe; we are on a journey to transform teaching and learning though innovative partnerships with school districts, states, ministries of education, and other like-minded organizations.

We have built an award-winning and highly reputable organization because of talented and driven people who work together to accelerate student achievement by tapping into students' natural curiosity and desire to learn by capturing their minds and imagination through the fascination of Discovery.

Why Join Discovery Education?

Discovery Education believes empowering each team member is vital to cultivating high performing, highly engaged teams. Being a part of our team means we partner together to achieve mutual goals. To this end, Discovery Education offers inclusive and robust offerings. You can find a complete listing on our Career Page.

Team Member Wellness – When you feel great, you do great work!


28 days leave plus UK bank holidays
Annual Winter Holiday Break (typically the last week of December)
Income protection to support you during long term illness
4x salary Life Assurance
Medicash cash back plan
Private Medical Insurance
Vivup cash back and discounted plan for 1000s of high street stores
Cycle to work scheme
Paid eye test and money towards glasses
Numerous Employee Assistance Programs
A social committee that helps us team build and celebrate wins


Career Development Ownership - Be the driver of your success!


Continuing Education AND Tuition Reimbursement Programs for when you choose to study further in your field as you work
Mentorship program and collaboration with veteran leaders
Constant opportunities for cross-functional training and skill building
Uncapped career growth
Unlimited LinkedIn Learning
Leadership training for people mangers


This opportunity is perfect for people interested in joining a high-performing, collaborative team, who enjoy a dynamic industry, and are excited about high-impact, project-based work.

Inspired? We would love the opportunity to review your candidacy! Select the blue “Apply Now” button at the bottom left of the screen to begin your application.

Discovery Education is an equal opportunity employer. Discovery Education is committed to being an employer of choice, not just a good place to work, but a great and inclusive place to work. To that end, we strive to recruit and maintain a workforce that meaningfully represents the communities we serve. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, protected veteran status or disabled status or, genetic information.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data should drive decisions, not sit in spreadsheets gathering dust. If you believe that too, we want to talk to you.

BSS, part of the Travis Perkins Group, is looking for a Data Analyst to play a central role in the management and migration of our master data as part of a major ERP programme. This is not a back office number-crunching job. It is hands-on, high-visibility work that shapes how the business manages its product and supplier information for years to come.

From day one you can expect to be trusted with real responsibility, because this role genuinely matters to the success of the business.

This is a HYBRID position, being based at either Magna Park, Lutterworth, or Crosspoint in Coventry

What you'll be doing

You will be the person who turns raw, messy data into something the business can actually trust and act on. Day to day, that means:


Using automated tools to extract master data from primary and secondary sources, then analysing it to understand and improve data quality
Owning data governance, including managing dependencies across projects and the wider TP Group
Keeping the project on track by monitoring the schedule and phasing, flagging progress and challenges clearly along the way
Supporting the business with data cleanse activities using internal software and portals
Translating stakeholder requirements into clear reporting documentation, and preparing final analysis reports that help leaders make confident, fact-based decisions
Supporting the wider group on a single solution for product and supplier master data management
Running user acceptance testing across the data stream, including documentation, communication and issue resolution


What we're looking for

Essential:


Previous experience working in a data environment, with good business acumen and a real appreciation of data governance
Strong SQL ability alongside excellent Google and data analysis skills
A genuinely excellent communicator who can relate to a broad range of people and write clear technical documentation
Someone who works well on their own initiative with minimal supervision
Sharp attention to detail, forward thinking and naturally innovative


Nice to have:


An understanding or interest in Artificial Intelligence
Superior organisational skills, with the ability to plan and prioritise your own workload
A deep understanding of working in a similar data environment


Why BSS

You will join a collaborative Data Team within Finance, working closely with stakeholders across multiple brands and tackling problems with real complexity and reach. You will need to balance the big picture with the fine detail, and your work will directly influence decisions at group level.

If you want data work that has genuine impact and the trust to get on and deliver it, we would love to hear from you. Apply today.

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Product Analyst

Function: Data

Location: London/ Peterborough

Curious about what’s next?

So are we. Join Compare the Market and help to make financial decision making a breeze for millions.

At Compare the Market, we’re a purpose-driven business powered by tech and AI. We’re building high-performing, results-driven teams with the skills, mindset, and ambition to deliver outcomes at pace. Every role here plays a part in driving our mission forward, and we create an environment where you can bring your authentic self, grow a truly characterful career, and see the direct impact of your work on the lives of our customers.

We’ve carved a meerkat-shaped niche and we’re looking for ambitious, curious thinkers who thrive in a fast-moving, high-impact environment. If you love accountability, embrace challenge, and want to make a real difference, you’ll fit right in.

What You’ll Be Doing


Analyse customer behaviour and product performance using techniques such as funnel analysis, cohort analysis, segmentation, and behavioural analytics to identify opportunities and drive improvements
Support Product Managers in understanding customer journeys, user engagement, conversion, and retention, helping to shape product strategy and priorities
Conduct exploratory analysis to answer product and business questions, translating complex data into clear, actionable recommendations
Define and measure product success metrics and KPIs, evaluating the impact of product changes and communicating outcomes to stakeholders
Design, execute, and analyse A/B tests and product experiments, helping to embed a culture of experimentation and evidence-based decision-making
Build and maintain dashboards and reporting solutions, proactively monitoring trends, identifying risks, and surfacing growth opportunities
Partner with Analytics Engineering teams to ensure KPI definitions, business logic, and product instrumentation are accurate, scalable, and trusted
Apply AI and machine learning techniques, including LLM-assisted analysis, predictive modelling, and automated insight generation, to enhance product understanding and measurement
Collaborate closely with Product Managers, Designers, Engineers, and Data teams to ensure decisions are grounded in robust analysis and customer insight


What We’re Looking For

You’ll have strong analytical skills and experience working with large datasets to uncover insights that drive better customer and business outcomes. You’ll be highly proficient in SQL and comfortable analysing customer and behavioural data to support product decisions and strategic priorities.

You’ll bring experience with experimentation and A/B testing, alongside a solid understanding of statistical concepts and product analytics techniques such as funnel analysis, cohort analysis, and segmentation. Experience with visualisation tools such as Looker, Tableau, or Power BI is essential, as is the ability to communicate complex findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.

You’ll have an understanding of digital product development and customer experience optimisation, with a curiosity for how emerging technologies, including AI, can improve product measurement, experimentation, and customer understanding. Experience using Python or R for analysis, and familiarity with product analytics platforms such as FullStory, Mixpanel, or Amplitude, would be advantageous.

We’re looking for someone who combines analytical rigour with product curiosity, thrives in collaborative environments, and enjoys helping teams make better decisions through data.

Why Compare the Market?

We’re a business built for pace and performance. Here, you’ll be encouraged to think differently, act boldly, and deliver brilliantly in a culture that values results and rewards progress.

We believe diverse teams make better decisions, and we’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels empowered to grow, contribute, and thrive.

If you’re ready to stretch yourself, raise the bar, and grow with a team that’s serious about performance, innovation, and purpose, we’d love to hear from you.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
ARNE is a UK-based apparel and footwear brand based in Birchwood Park, near Warrington. We design and develop all our products in the UK, working with trusted manufacturing partners across both the near and far east.

All our products are available directly to customers through ARNEclo.com, offering a seamless online shopping experience. We're also proud to be stocked in Selfridges, both in-store and online, and we've recently opened our first permanent retail store at Liverpool ONE - a huge milestone for the brand.

Looking ahead, we have ambitious goals for the next decade, and the team firmly believes they're all achievable. From launching new product categories and expanding internationally to investing in paid media, CRM, and growing our retail presence — the next chapter for ARNE is shaping up to be an exciting one. And this is only the beginning!

We've come a long way from starting out in Mum's bungalow back in 2018 to our all-new, fully bespoke HQ. This space will be our home for at least the next 5-10 years, and while it's not quite our style to shout too loudly, it's here that we plan to reach our goals, take ARNE global, and further establish ourselves as a truly iconic UK brand.

We are now looking for a Data Analyst to join the team and support ARNE by delivering accurate, reliable data that enables informed decision-making and supports key business workstreams.

Your day-to-day role and responsibilities include:


Creating and maintaining dashboards in Power BI and creating SQL queries to provide daily, weekly, monthly and adhoc insights with the end goal of having an automated reporting suite that enables a self-serve data platform across the business
Working with key stakeholders to capture key data needs which then allows for more efficient reporting to be built
Acting as a key support to the Analytics Engineer to ensure data is mined, cleansed and validated including the monitoring of data pipelines
Ensuring data integrity, security and reliability across systems
Working closely with Head of Financial Planning and Analysis and finance team in the creation of financial models and forecasts and streamline month end processes
Respond to ad hoc requests, acting as a support function for the wider business and as a point of contact should there be data issues


Requirements


Previous experience working in a fast-paced fashion, retail or DTC e-commerce brand is essential
Experience in using SQL, Power BI or similar relational databases / AI tools to build queries, data models and reporting solutions
Strong understanding of e-commerce and marketing data ecosystems such as Shopify, Klaviyo and Meta ads
Excellent communication and organisational skills, capable of handling complex issues with structured thinking and analytical abilities
Strong Microsoft Excel user
Strong attention to detail and ability to manage competing deadlines independently


Benefits


We are proud to be recognised as a Medium sized Sunday Times Best Place to Work two years a row!
32 Days Holiday (including Bank Holidays) + your Birthday off
Holidays increasing up to 36 days with service
Option to buy / sell holidays
Generous staff discount
Annual and quarterly bonus schemes based on company performance
Private Healthcare including Dental & Optical Plans after probation
Health Cash Plan from day one
Life assurance paying 5x annual salary from day one
Discounted marketplace with money off/cash back from 100's of places
Employee Assistance Programme
Cycle to Work Scheme
Tech Scheme


ARNE are an equal opportunities employer. We believe that diverse people bring different skills and are committed to ensuring our business support the needs of potential colleagues from Ethnic Minority backgrounds and colleagues who may be disabled or neurodiverse. We are committed creating a great place to work through diversity and inclusivity and we strongly encourage applications from candidates from all backgrounds to join our growing team.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Place: Brighton And Hove, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Legal & General (L&G) is a leading UK financial services group and major global investor.



We’ve been safeguarding people’s financial futures since 1836, and strive to build a better society, while improving the lives of our customers and creating value for shareholders.



We are one of the world’s largest asset managers and provide powerful asset origination capabilities. Together, these underpin our retirement and protection solutions: we are an international player in pension risk transfer, in UK and US life insurance, and in UK workplace pensions and retirement income.



Our Group Functions provide the services that all areas of the business need. This requires a talented and diverse team behind the scenes, who enable everyone at L&G to do what they do best. 



Joining us means helping to improve the lives of our customers and contributing to the success of the business every day.





Job Description



We’re looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our Institutional Retirement Client Services team.



This role is all about using data carefully and confidently to support Payroll & Reinsurance activity, Pension Risk Transfer transactions and wider operational decision-making. You’ll be working with data from end to end, helping to cleanse, validate, transform, compare and document information so that it can be used accurately by the right teams and systems.



You’ll be joining a supportive team that works closely together, with opportunities to collaborate with payroll consultants, internal stakeholders and wider data teams across Legal & General. We’re looking for someone who enjoys detail, takes pride in accuracy, and is curious about finding better ways to do things.



What you’ll be doing:



Managing operational data across cleansing, validation, transformation, integration and mining activities
Supporting Pension Risk Transfer transaction data from end to end
Gathering requirements from payroll consultants and internal stakeholders
Improving data quality so teams can make accurate and timely decisions
Testing and documenting processes to support consistent client delivery
Maintaining clear audit trails across the full data lifecycle
Supporting and upskilling less experienced team members in new processes
Sharing best practice with wider data teams and identifying better ways of working

Who we’re looking for:



Advanced Excel skills and confidence working with detailed data
Experience in data analysis, operational data management or data process improvement
Strong attention to detail and a right-first-time mindset
Confidence communicating with stakeholders and gathering requirements
Ability to manage tight timescales, prioritise work and escalate when needed
Curiosity, resilience and confidence challenging where appropriate
Familiarity with Alteryx, Python, SQL or SAS would be beneficial
Understanding of defined benefit pensions, Pension Risk Transfer or pension-related data would be beneficial

Whatever your role, we reward performance and behaviour with a package that looks after all the things that are important to you. Here are some of the benefits we offer:



The opportunity to participate in our annual, performance -related bonus plan and valuable share schemes
Generous pension contribution
Life assurance
Healthcare Plan (permanent employees only)
At least 25 days holiday, plus public holidays, 26 days after 2 years’ service. There’s also the option to buy and sell holiday
Competitive family leave
Participate in our electric car scheme, which offers employees the option to hire a brand-new electric car through tax efficient salary sacrifice (permanent employees only)
There are many discounts we offer – both for our own products and at a range of high street stores and online
We’re creating net‑zero carbon workplaces by 2030 by investing in our sustainable, modern offices across the UK, all designed to bring people together and elevate the in‑person experience


Additional Information



At L&G, we believe it's possible to generate positive returns today while helping to build a better future for all.



If you join us, you’ll be part of a welcoming, inclusive culture, with opportunities to collaborate with people of diverse backgrounds, views, and experiences. Guided by leaders with integrity who care about your future and wellbeing. Empowered through initiatives which support people to develop their careers and excel.



We care passionately about outcomes rather than attendance and are therefore open to discussing all kinds of flexible working options including part-time, term-time and job shares. Although some roles have limited flexibility due to customer demand, we accommodate requests when we can.



It doesn’t matter if you don’t meet every single criterion in this advert. Instead, think about what you excel at and what else you can bring in terms of strengths, potential and connection to our purpose.

Title: BI Developer
Description:
We’re looking for a Business Intelligence Developer to help strengthen our reporting and insights capability by delivering high-quality, trusted BI solutions across the organisation. In this role, you’ll work at the heart of our data platform, supporting key business processes and ensuring our reporting is accurate, reliable and aligned to governance standards.

You’ll collaborate closely with stakeholders across Technology and the wider business to develop and maintain reports, validate requirements and ensure solutions meet both technical and business needs. You’ll play an important role in supporting the ongoing development of our BI, data and insights capability.

Working across data, reporting and analytics teams, you’ll contribute to improving how data is structured, accessed and used to support decision-making.

What We Can Offer You


We offer a competitive salary and benefits which are designed to promote our employees financial wellbeing. Employees are also eligible to participate in a bonus plan.
Our employees enjoy a generous holiday entitlement and an opportunity to ‘buy’ or ‘sell’ some holiday entitlement.
Private Healthcare and Doctor@Hand (remote GP service).
Our employees can join a defined contribution pension plan. Employees contribute either 4% or 5% of salary, the company contributes 8% or 10% depending on the employee contribution. Employee contributions can be made through salary sacrifice.
Our Employee Assistance Programme offers practical, impartial support on everyday matters ranging from medical, financial and legal to home and family issues.
Our Life Assurance cover is a multiple of eight times your annual basic salary.
Product allocation so that you can enjoy our fantastic portfolio of brands.
Our Cycle to Work scheme allows you to hire a bike for an agreed length of time, and then snap it up for a fraction of its original value. All while making savings (at least 25%) and spreading the cost.
Every employee has the opportunity to claim up to £1,000 per year for a charity or charities for which they have raised money, volunteered their time or personally donated.
Learning resources to help you be your best self.


Main Responsibilities


Ensuring the integrity and governance of the data platform to deliver trusted reporting and insights solutions.
Reviewing and validating reporting requirements, identifying risks, gaps and impacts on existing data models.
Designing and developing BI solutions using strong data modelling and platform knowledge.
Creating and maintaining documentation, including report design specifications and testing templates.
Supporting the delivery of reporting solutions across key business cycles such as forecasting, budgeting and year-end processes.
Managing the development and deployment of BI content through the full lifecycle.
Ensuring all delivered solutions align with governance principles and service delivery standards.
Contributing to improvements in process efficiency, system performance and overall BI quality.
Supporting and engaging stakeholders to encourage adoption of BI tools and data-led decision-making.
Maintaining clear documentation and sharing knowledge across the team.
Providing end-to-end support for BI applications, ensuring system availability and service levels are met.


Our Ideal Candidate

You understand how data supports effective decision-making and take pride in building reliable and well-structured BI solutions.

You’re detail-oriented, collaborative and comfortable working with both technical teams and business stakeholders.

You bring a practical and structured approach, with a strong focus on governance, quality and continuous improvement.

This role would suit


A BI Developer looking to expand their experience within a modern data platform environment.
A Data Analyst or SQL specialist wanting to develop deeper expertise in BI and data modelling.
Someone confident working with complex datasets and supporting financial or operational reporting cycles.
An individual who values documentation, structure and strong development practices.
A strong communicator who can clearly explain technical concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences.


About William Grant & Sons

A HOME FOR RARE CHARACTERS

William Grant & Sons: a home where Rare Characters thrive.

We value every employee for their rare character, distinctive skills, experience and perspectives. Every one of our colleagues has a role to play in helping us to achieve our growth ambitions.

At William Grant & Sons, our vision is to be A home where rare characters thrive. We value all colleagues for their rare character, distinctive skills, experience and perspectives. Diversity & Inclusion is at the heart of how we do things at William Grant & Sons, fully aligned to our purpose and our company values. We strive to create an environment where we can all be our best and bring our whole selves to work.

OUR AGILE WORKING PHILOSOPHY

Our agile working philosophy is to “Have your best work day everyday”.

Built on trust, we empower our rare characters to have their best work day every day. Where flexibility and positive working experiences help employees to feel connected and release potential across our teams.

We are open to discussing possible agile/flexible working options as part of the recruitment process.

INCLUSIVE RECRUITMENT PROCESS

Diversity & Inclusion is at the heart of how we do things at William Grant & Sons, fully aligned to our purpose and our company values. We want to ensure that our recruitment process is inclusive.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
Description:
hackajob is collaborating with Opencast Software to connect them with exceptional professionals for this role.

Want to help shape decisions with the power of data?

Do you want to turn data into insights that make a real difference? Are you ready to use analytics, KPIs and storytelling to solve complex problems for our clients in government, healthcare and purpose-driven businesses?

If so, Opencast could be the place for you. We’re a growing tech consultancy that creates user-centred solutions with purpose. As a Data Analyst, you’ll guide better decisions, helping clients to act with insight - and help shape our data practice for the future.

The role

You’ll help clients improve their services by selecting the appropriate techniques to turn data into insights, by working within a product delivery team to develop performance measuring frameworks to track goals, user needs, or KPIs. You’ll operate within the client's requirements and the appropriate legal, technical and ethical constraints.

Leading by example and promoting Opencast’s approach, you’ll mentor junior colleagues, provide constructive feedback, and demonstrate an understanding of each product delivery stage and how to apply appropriate measurements for that phase of work.

Ensure that work is carried out in accordance with Opencast's quality, environmental, and information security policies and procedures, as well as applicable customer policies, standards, and procedures while working on client.

Requirements

Experience in delivering digital services for the UK government is highly valuable for many of our projects. Therefore, familiarity with the GDS toolkit will be essential.

Due to the nature of this role, applicants must either currently hold active Security Check (SC) Clearance or be eligible to obtain it.

Eligibility typically requires UK citizenship and continuous residence in the UK for the past five years, with no single absence exceeding 28 consecutive days. As a result, we are unable to progress applications from candidates who do not meet these requirements.

Essential Experience


Scripting and programming languages such as SQL, Python, R etc
Visualising data using Power BI, Tableau, QlikView, Power Query or similar
Designed and implemented a performance framework including KPIs or OKRs for a service or product
Analytical skills in identifying the right data sources and then validate and understand how to interpret the results
Collected, collated, cleansed, and interpreted data to derive meaningful and actionable insights
An ability to use the most appropriate medium to visualise data to tell compelling and relevant actionable stories
Created hypotheses that can be tested, and developed insights based on your findings


Desirable Experience


Security and data privacy
Making recommendations for service changes
Experience with the GDS or NHS design principles


Salary

Consultant salary range: £44,001 - £50,000 per annum

What We Offer

A competitive package including a 7% employer pension contribution, share options and a discretionary bonus. You’ll also benefit from flexible working hours (aligned to client commitments), 25 days’ holiday, and access to 3 annual “life happens” days to support unexpected situations. We also offer wellbeing support, ongoing professional development, volunteering opportunities and the chance to be part of a genuinely supportive team.

Where you’ll work

This role requires regular on-site attendance across our HQ, hub offices, client sites and home. Applicants must be able to commute to offices and client sites as part of their normal working arrangements, as travel is essential to fulfilling the responsibilities of the role.

We include you

We welcome candidates from all identities, attributes, ways of thinking and backgrounds to thrive in a career at Opencast. Our aim is for the diversity of our people to be reflected in the solutions we deliver, to help create a fairer society for all.

Interview

Interviews will be conducted via Microsoft Teams. We’re happy to make adjustments to support your needs.

Want to know more?

Visit our website or email careers@opencastsoftware.com

Title: Data and Information Analyst
Description:
Wheatley Solutions




Salary: £52,176 - £56,270

Contract: Permanent

Work pattern: Monday - Friday

Location: Agile home-based worker with travel requirement to Wheatley offices




Do you want to work with a values-driven organisation that makes a difference to people’s lives? Come and join Wheatley as a Data and Information Analyst.




About the role

We are looking for a talented Data and Information Analyst to join our Data and Information Team and play a key role in delivering high-quality business intelligence, performance reporting and data-driven insights across the organisation.

This is an exciting opportunity for a data professional who is passionate about transforming complex data into meaningful intelligence that supports strategic decision-making, service improvement and organisational performance.

This role offers the opportunity to work in a fast-paced environment where your expertise will directly influence business outcomes and help deliver better services for our customers and communities.

Who are we looking for?

We're looking for an analytical and proactive data professional who can turn complex information into meaningful insights that drive performance and decision-making.




You'll have:

strong analytical, numerical and problem-solving skills;
expertise in SQL and data visualisation tools such as Power BI or Tableau;
experience working with large and complex datasets, using analytical tools such as Python, R, Stata or SAS;
experience with, or an interest in developing skills in, Microsoft Fabric and Data Engineering;
excellent communication skills, with the ability to present technical information to non-technical audiences;
strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills;
a customer-focused approach and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment; and
a commitment to continuous learning and professional development.




You'll also be collaborative, innovative and driven to deliver excellent outcomes, while building strong relationships and supporting continuous improvement across the organisation.

Knowledge of the housing or care sectors, and relevant data or analytical qualifications, would be advantageous.




The benefits we offer

We offer a sector-leading benefits package.

The successful candidate will receive:

a rewarding career with a competitive salary;
access to a contributory pension scheme;
excellent leave entitlements;
enhanced maternity, paternity, adoption and shared parental leave;
contributions towards dental, optical, driving lessons and more;
access to staff inclusion networks promoting an inclusive workplace; and
24/7 access to our employee assistance programme.




Committed to inclusion

At Wheatley, we are always looking to improve diversity within our teams. We want to create an inclusive environment where everyone can contribute their best work and achieve their full potential.

We actively celebrate our individual differences and recognise the collective strength this brings our organisation. It’s important our teams represent the communities we serve, and we welcome applications from any under-represented groups.

Wheatley Solutions

Contract: Permanent

Work pattern: Monday - Friday

Do you want to work with a values-driven organisation that makes a difference to people’s lives? Come and join Wheatley as a Data and Information Analyst.


About the role

We are looking for a talented Data and Information Analyst to join our Data and Information Team and play a key role in delivering high-quality business intelligence, performance reporting and data-driven insights across the organisation.

This is an exciting opportunity for a data professional who is passionate about transforming complex data into meaningful intelligence that supports strategic decision-making, service improvement and organisational performance.

This role offers the opportunity to work in a fast-paced environment where your expertise will directly influence business outcomes and help deliver better services for our customers and communities.

Who are we looking for?

We're looking for an analytical and proactive data professional who can turn complex information into meaningful insights that drive performance and decision-making.


You'll have:

  • strong analytical, numerical and problem-solving skills;
  • expertise in SQL and data visualisation tools such as Power BI or Tableau;
  • experience working with large and complex datasets, using analytical tools such as Python, R, Stata or SAS;
  • experience with, or an interest in developing skills in, Microsoft Fabric and Data Engineering;
  • excellent communication skills, with the ability to present technical information to non-technical audiences;
  • strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills;
  • a customer-focused approach and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment; and
  • a commitment to continuous learning and professional development.


You'll also be collaborative, innovative and driven to deliver excellent outcomes, while building strong relationships and supporting continuous improvement across the organisation.

Knowledge of the housing or care sectors, and relevant data or analytical qualifications, would be advantageous.


The benefits we offer

We offer a sector-leading benefits package.

The successful candidate will receive:

  • a rewarding career with a competitive salary;
  • access to a contributory pension scheme;
  • excellent leave entitlements;
  • enhanced maternity, paternity, adoption and shared parental leave;
  • contributions towards dental, optical, driving lessons and more;
  • access to staff inclusion networks promoting an inclusive workplace; and
  • 24/7 access to our employee assistance programme.


Committed to inclusion

At Wheatley, we are always looking to improve diversity within our teams. We want to create an inclusive environment where everyone can contribute their best work and achieve their full potential.

We actively celebrate our individual differences and recognise the collective strength this brings our organisation. It’s important our teams represent the communities we serve, and we welcome applications from any under-represented groups.

Title: Quality Systems Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Quality Systems Analyst

Pay: £280 per day PAYE £390 per Umbrella

Location: Hinkley Point C, Somerset




A Quality Systems Analyst is required for this business, that are delivering a significant package of Civil Works at Hinkley Point C in Taunton



The Quality Systems Analyst will support the Quality Systems team with producing data analytics, alignment and visualisation of outputs, reporting results and analysis from multiple digital quality systems to internal senior management, and stakeholders





Develop, maintain, and improve data analytics dashboards and reports using tools like Qlik Sense and Power BI to provide meaningful insights into quality performance and help to drive efficiency across the board.





Ensure data accuracy, completeness, and consistency across all systems, helping to drive improvements in both data gathering methods and processes.

Establish strong working relationships with all key stakeholders and lead projects from initial scoping requirements and development all the way through to final delivery.

Discuss IT systems and architecture, data formats & systems integration opportunities with both technical and non-technical colleagues.

Familiar with SQL, Python (preferable, but not essential).

Understand Data Visualisation tools (knowledge of Qlik Sense or Power BI is preferable).

Enjoy working with large datasets.

Be comfortable working with tabular data in csv format, excel etc. and proficient in Microsoft O365.

This role is for immediate start
