Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
Role: Marketing Data Analyst

Salary: Dependant on skills and experience

Hours: 35 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Matchday working included in role too.

Location: American Express Stadium, Brighton

Contract Type: Permanent

Deadline Day: 22nd July 2026




About Brighton & Hove Albion FC

We compete at the highest levels of football on a global stage while embracing our Sussex community spirit. At the heart of everything we do is our commitment to high performance, professional excellence, and making a positive impact. We are proud to have placed in the Sunday Times Best Place to Work 2026.




Turn raw data into match-winning commercial strategy.

Are you ready to be the "single point of insight" at a forward-thinking football club? We are looking for a highly strategic data professional to own our metrics, rules, and logic. In this role, you won’t just be crunching numbers, you will be translating complex datasets into the deep context and actionable recommendations that drive our global growth. This role will work across our marketing and ticketing team.




The role will also include matchday working to run and automate the tout identification models and review post-match accounts, therefore there will be an element of evening and weekend working involved.




To find out more about this role, please click here to view the job description.




Ready to make your mark off the pitch?

If you are a proactive problem solver with a passion for driving commercial success through data, we want you on our team. We are looking for someone with strong skills in Google Cloud Platforms, advanced SQL skills for complex data queries and strong proficiency in Python for building, maintaining and automating data models.




Our values are essential to our success

Our values serve as guiding principles for how we connect with one another, our fans, partners and sponsors. As a club, we embody these values in everything we do:




🔵 Treat People Well

⚪ Exceed Expectations

🔵 Aim High. Never Give Up

⚪ Act with Integrity

🔵 Make it Special

⚪ Be Fan Focused







How we say thank you

In appreciation for your hard work and dedication, we are pleased to offer you a range of additional benefits, including but not limited to:




🥗 Complimentary breakfast and lunch at both sites

🏝️23 days holiday rising with length of service (pro rata for part time staff)

⚡Rewards platform, which includes access to 900+ retailers, a wellbeing centre and more!

👶 Enhanced family policies

🎾 Access to a gym and padel court at our training ground

🎫 Priority access to match tickets and access to free WSL tickets for 2026/27 season

🎓 In-house training programme and CPD opportunities

💰 Exclusive discounts and benefits from our partners and local businesses




Our commitment to EDI

We take pride in being an equal opportunity workplace that reflects the diversity of our society and the world around us. Our core values foster a strong ethical culture, enabling us to meet the needs of our diverse audience, both on and off the pitch. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age, disability, sex, gender reassignment, sexual orientation, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, and marriage or civil partnership. If you require any reasonable adjustments to support your application, please contact teamtalent@brightonandhovealbion.com




Safeguarding is part of everything we do

We are committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children, young people and adults at risk. We expect all colleagues and volunteers to share this commitment.




This position is UK-based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All job offers are conditional upon background checks, including a full 3-year reference history, DBS checks, and verification of your eligibility to work. Please note, we do not sponsor applicants without the right to work in the UK.

Title: Analytics Officer / Analytics Business Partner
Description:
We have an exciting role on offer within our Policy, Governance & Information Service as an Analytics Officer / Analytics Business Partner.

An opportunity has arisen in our team for the right candidate as we increase our ambitions around use of data and analytics. This role is perfect if you’re already working in a data analysis environment and want to widen your professional growth. Whilst an understanding of Local Government analytics is preferable, it’s not essential.

The post holder will support the work of the Neighbourhoods Teams as well as Middlesbrough Community Safety Partnership. This post will involve the collation, analysis and reporting of information that is relevant to monitoring levels of crime and antisocial behaviour across the Borough. You will be working within a multi-agency environment.

You will be working with all levels of the organisation including our senior management team. You will need to be confident, focussed and be able to collect requirements, design, and build business intelligent solutions to meet the needs of the organisation.

What you will be doing: -


Reporting and dashboarding to create data visualisations and predictive analytics. We use Power BI and Alteryx.
Collaborating on reporting data, identifying and solving problems, and develop a whole system approach.
Facilitating data extraction with external agencies, using data to map ‘as is’ processes, identify opportunities to improve and map the ‘to be’ processes to support programme improvement.


You will be: -


Highly experienced in the use of technology and have experience of using related software, including Power BI, ETL tools (ideally Alteryx), Excel.
Confident, articulate and able to adapt your approach to suit your audience.
Passionate about data analytics.
Focussed on the detail, with excellent analytical skill and delivering the required products and results.
An excellent communicator, creative and adaptive in your approach.
Creative and proactive, contributing towards changes and seeking improvements.


Please see person specification for more information.

In return, we can offer you the chance to work in a new state of the art building, with a blend of home working (if that’s what you want). There is a strong offer of training and development opportunities, and we have family friendly policies, flexible hours, generous annual leave, an electric car scheme, various employee discounts and benefits plus that all important great pension.

If you want any further information regarding this role, please speak to Dee Evans on 01642 728636.

Title: Risk Analyst
Description:
Closing Date

2026-08-28

Job title: Risk Analyst

Location: Hybrid – Sussex area (2/3 days a week in office)

Salary: £50,000 + benefits

Help Shape the Future of Wastewater Risk and Resilience

At Southern Water, we're committed to delivering resilient wastewater services that protect our customers, communities, and the environment. We're looking for a Risk Analyst to play a pivotal role in helping us understand, prioritise, and manage risk across our wastewater assets and operations.

This is an exciting opportunity to work at the heart of our Asset Management function, using data-driven insight to influence investment decisions, improve operational resilience, and support the delivery of our long-term performance commitments.

Working closely with colleagues from Asset Management, Operations, Engineering and Planning, you'll help create a clear picture of risk across our wastewater business and ensure investment is targeted where it delivers the greatest value and benefit.

What You'll Be Doing

As our Asset Risk & Resilience Analyst, you will:


Analyse asset, operational, environmental, customer, and financial data to identify current and emerging risks across the wastewater network.
Develop and maintain a comprehensive view of wastewater asset risk, ensuring risks are accurately recorded, monitored, and tracked.
Support key regulatory and investment planning frameworks, including the Drainage and Wastewater Management Plan (DWMP) and PR29 submissions.
Collaborate with Operations, Engineering, Asset Management, and Planning teams to improve risk identification, reporting, and management practices.
Integrate reporting systems, operational intelligence, and subject matter expertise to provide a consistent and robust understanding of risk.
Produce meaningful risk reports, dashboards, and performance insights for a variety of stakeholders, including senior leadership teams.
Monitor trends and identify areas where performance may be outside agreed risk appetite levels.
Support the prioritisation of investment programmes and mitigation activities based on risk, resilience, and value.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of risk methodologies, reporting frameworks, and analytical processes.
Help drive better decision-making that improves customer outcomes, environmental performance, and asset resilience.


Essential

What We're Looking For


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to interpret complex data and turn it into actionable insights.
Experience assessing, analysing, and communicating business, operational, or asset-related risks.
Ability to work independently, manage competing priorities, and take ownership of deliverables.
A proactive mindset with a focus on identifying opportunities for improvement and driving positive change.
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills and experience working with large datasets.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to present complex information clearly and effectively.


Desirable


Experience within the water, wastewater, utilities, infrastructure, or other asset-intensive sectors.
Knowledge of Power BI, SQL, Alteryx, or other data analysis and visualisation tools.
Experience using asset management, risk management, or investment planning systems.
Understanding of resilience planning, investment prioritisation, and regulatory performance frameworks.
Familiarity with wastewater asset management and operational performance reporting.


Southern Water is at the forefront of transforming Britain’s water industry, investing significantly to enhance resilience, sustainability, and service excellence. With £7.8bn planned investment for 2025-30, this is an unparalleled opportunity to join a business committed to delivering a generational shift in the way water services are managed.

You will be joining at a time of significant change, working alongside a highly skilled leadership team with a clear vision for the future. We offer an environment where senior professionals can make a meaningful impact, influence major strategic decisions, and drive long-term value creation.

"At Southern Water, we believe diverse perspectives drive innovation. If you’re passionate about making a positive impact and think you can bring value to our team, we’d love to hear from you—even if you don’t tick every box. Your unique skills and experiences could be exactly what we need."

If this role isn’t quite what you’re looking for but are keen to be contacted about opportunities at Southern Water, you can register your details here: Introduce Yourself (myworkdaysite.com- Introduce Yourself)

Our Commitment to Diversity

We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, religion, or any other protected characteristic. If you need reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Description:
Data and Reporting Analyst

Job Reference schooltalent/TP/11692/2730

Apply for this job

1

Remuneration is dependant on skills and experience

Edinburgh

27/07/2026

Department:

ICT

Region / Division:

ESMS

Job Role:

Operational/Professional Services

The Role

Erskine Stewart Melville are currently recruiting for an Data and Reporting Analyst to join the ICT Team on a permanent basis as soon as possible (subject to satisfactory pre-employment checks). The role is full-time, working 36.25 hours per week.

The salary range for the role is £34,023 to £37,690 per annum, dependant on skills and experience.

The role is based across the Queensferry Road and Ravelston sites.

The Purpose Of The Role Is To Support The School's Data Systems And Reporting Processes As Well As Developing And Maintaining Reporting Tools. The Role Will Include Duties Such As:


Supporting collection, storage and management of data
Analysing data to identify trends, patterns and insights
Developing and maintaining dashboards, reporting tools and data models.


The successful candidate must have experience of working will large and complex data sets and in developing and maintaining reports and dashboards. The successful candidate should hold a degree in a related field and possess strong analytical and problem solving skills. Experience with cloud-based data platform, familiarity with DPIAs and a relevant qualification in Power BI, SQL or Data Analytics is desirable, but not essential.

To learn more about this role, please access the job description provided.

The closing date for this role is Monday 27 July at 12 noon. We anticipate interviews will be held shortly after.

Eligibility

ESMS is unable to sponsor the employment of international workers in this role. International applicants will therefore be unable to apply for and secure a Skilled Worker visa. The successful candidate will only be able to take up this role if they can demonstrate an alternative right work in the UK.

Benefits

Employees get access to a range of benefits including:


Fee remission from Nursery to S6
Free school lunches
Free Sports’ Club membership, including access to pool and fitness suites
25 days annual holiday leave plus 10 days statutory holiday at Christmas and Easter, when the Schools are closed
Paid moving house day
Employer pension contributions of up to 8%
Death in service benefit
Family friendly policies, including enhanced maternity leave
Membership of the ESMS Discount and Benefits Scheme, including a range of discounts at 130,000 retail and entertainment locations
Employee Assistance Service
Cycle to work scheme
Car leasing salary sacrifice scheme
Annual professional review process
Regular salary reviews
Funding towards professional qualifications


Values

We are looking for individuals who can embody and promote ESMS values of Kindness, Confidence, Resilience, Integrity, and Curiosity while role modelling behaviours in line with our school pillars of Ambition, Innovation, and Community.

About ESMS

Welcome to the ESMS, a vibrant family of independent schools, set in beautiful grounds in the heart of Edinburgh. We offer a co-educational nursery and junior school, single-sex but closely twinned secondary education, and a co-educational sixth form. 

We are a consistently award-winning School, with an educational legacy that stretches back more than 300 years. Our Schools have always been pioneers of rounded education. Children’s education is underpinned by our values: kindness, confidence, resilience, integrity and curiosity. From virtual reality headsets in the classroom to developing one of the first outdoor learning programmes in Scotland, we are always searching for new ways to develop the whole child.

The people that work for us are passionate, pioneering and strive for excellence in everything they do. We value the incredible contributions they make to school life, we invest in their careers and we support their ambitions so that they flourish professionally and personally.

The closing date for this role is Monday 27 July at 12 noon. We anticipate interviews will be held shortly after.

Eligibility

ESMS is unable to sponsor the employment of international workers in this role. International applicants will therefore be unable to apply for and secure a Skilled Worker visa. The successful candidate will only be able to take up this role if they can demonstrate an alternative right work in the UK.

Benefits

Employees get access to a range of benefits including:

  • Fee remission from Nursery to S6
  • Free school lunches
  • Free Sports’ Club membership, including access to pool and fitness suites
  • 25 days annual holiday leave plus 10 days statutory holiday at Christmas and Easter, when the Schools are closed
  • Paid moving house day

We are looking for individuals who can embody and promote ESMS values of Kindness, Confidence, Resilience, Integrity, and Curiosity while role modelling behaviours in line with our school pillars of Ambition, Innovation, and Community.

About ESMS

Welcome to the ESMS, a vibrant family of independent schools, set in beautiful grounds in the heart of Edinburgh. We offer a co-educational nursery and junior school, single-sex but closely twinned secondary education, and a co-educational sixth form. 

We are a consistently award-winning School, with an educational legacy that stretches back more than 300 years. Our Schools have always been pioneers of rounded education. Children’s education is underpinned by our values: kindness, confidence, resilience, integrity and curiosity. From virtual reality headsets in the classroom to developing one of the first outdoor learning programmes in Scotland, we are always searching for new ways to develop the whole child.

The people that work for us are passionate, pioneering and strive for excellence in everything they do. We value the incredible contributions they make to school life, we invest in their careers and we support their ambitions so that they flourish professionally and personally.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
💰 Salary: £33,000 – £43,000 per year (depending on experience)

📍 Location: UK / Hybrid / Remote




🕒 Job type: Full-time, Permanent

👤 Reporting to: Data & Analytics Manager




⏳ Closing date: 5th August 2026, 11:55pm

Apply Now

About Guha Infotech

Guha Infotech is a technology-driven company delivering smart data and IT solutions to clients across a range of industries. We believe good decisions start with good data, and we're building a team of talented people who share that belief. As we continue to grow, we're investing in our analytics capability to help our clients and our own business unlock the full value of their data.

About the role

We're looking for a skilled, curious, and detail-oriented Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you'll be responsible for collecting, cleaning, analysing, and visualising data to produce insights that drive real business decisions.

You'll work across the full data lifecycle — from writing SQL queries and building automated pipelines, through to designing polished Power BI dashboards that stakeholders actually use. You'll partner with teams across the business to understand their questions, translate them into analytical problems, and deliver clear, reliable answers.

This is an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys working with modern data and cloud tools, takes pride in accuracy, and wants to keep growing their technical skills in a supportive environment.

Key responsibilitiesData analysis and reporting

Write, optimise, and maintain SQL queries to extract and transform data from multiple sources
Analyse large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities
Build, maintain, and enhance interactive dashboards and reports in Power BI, using DAX for calculated measures
Produce regular and ad-hoc reports for stakeholders across the business

Automation and data processing

Use Python to automate repetitive data tasks, clean datasets, and carry out deeper analysis
Support the design and maintenance of data pipelines and ETL processes
Work with Azure technologies (such as Azure Data Factory, Databricks, or Synapse) to help store, process, and manage data efficiently

Data quality and governance

Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across all reporting
Investigate and resolve data discrepancies and quality issues
Follow and help improve data governance and documentation standards

Collaboration and communication

Work closely with stakeholders to gather requirements and understand business needs
Present findings and recommendations clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Document processes, data definitions, and reporting logic for the wider team

Essential skills and experience

Strong, hands-on experience writing SQL (complex queries, joins, aggregations, and ideally stored procedures and views)
Proficiency in Python for data cleaning, analysis, and automation (e.g. pandas, NumPy)
Demonstrable experience building reports and dashboards in Power BI, including data modelling and DAX
Advanced Excel skills — formulas, pivot tables, lookups, and data modelling
Working knowledge of Azure data technologies and cloud-based data workflows
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with excellent attention to detail
Ability to communicate complex data clearly and confidently
Good organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities

Desirable skills

Relevant Microsoft certifications (e.g. PL-300 Power BI Data Analyst, DP-203 Azure Data Engineer, DP-700 Fabric Analytics Engineer)
Experience with data warehousing concepts and ETL/ELT pipelines
Familiarity with version control (e.g. Git) and Agile ways of working
A degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related numerate field (or equivalent practical experience)

Technologies you'll work with

SQL Python pandas NumPy Power BI DAX Advanced ExcelAzure Data Factory Databricks Synapse ETL / ELT Git

What we offer

Competitive salary of £33,000 – £43,000, depending on experience
Ongoing training, certifications, and professional development support
Clear opportunities for career progression as the team grows
A friendly, collaborative, and inclusive working culture
Pension scheme
Hybrid / flexible working arrangements
Annual leave allowance

Who we're looking for

You're someone who genuinely enjoys working with data and gets satisfaction from turning messy information into clear insight. You're comfortable working independently but also thrive as part of a team. You ask good questions, care about getting the details right, and are always looking to learn something new.

Whether you're an experienced analyst or someone with a strong foundation looking to take the next step, we'd love to hear from you.

Closing date: 5th August 2026, 11:55pm.

Title: Information Analyst
Description:
Joining us as an Information Analyst, you'll play a key role in helping Children's Services use data and insight to improve outcomes for children, young people and families. You’ll turn complex information into meaningful intelligence that supports decision-making, drives service improvement and helps ensure resources are targeted where they can have the greatest impact.




You'll work closely with colleagues and stakeholders across the service to develop reporting solutions, identify trends and opportunities, and improve the quality and use of data. Through your analytical skills, curiosity and commitment to continuous improvement, you'll help shape how information is used to support Early Help services and deliver positive change for our communities.




What you'll do

Analyse and interpret data to identify trends, issues and opportunities that support service improvement and decision making.
Develop reports, dashboards and data visualisations that provide clear and accessible insight for a range of audiences.
Support improvements to Early Help and Best Start in Life reporting, data quality and information management processes.
Work collaboratively with colleagues and stakeholders to deliver data-focused projects and develop solutions to business challenges.
Troubleshoot data and reporting issues, recommend practical solutions and support continuous improvement initiatives.




What we're looking for

Experience using Microsoft Excel at an advanced level and reporting or data querying tools such as SQL.
Good knowledge of a range of data tools and the ability to manipulate, interpret and present data effectively.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, capable of identifying trends, drawing meaningful conclusions and producing high-quality outputs.
Ability to communicate complex information clearly and build effective working relationships with a range of stakeholders.
Proactive, organised and adaptable, with a commitment to continuous learning and improving outcomes through data and insight.




Why join us?

Play a vital role in helping Children's Services make informed decisions that improve outcomes for children, young people and families.
Be part of a collaborative and supportive team that values innovation, development and the effective use of data to drive positive change.
Access to Health Assured's comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme to support your physical and mental wellbeing, including 24/7 telephone support, a suite of online resources, and legal and financial advice.
Competitive benefits package, including generous annual leave entitlement, occupational sick pay, and access to the Local Government Pension Scheme.




To learn more about this role, please review our Candidate Pack available on our website. Please click on the Apply button for details.




Please note: We are unable to offer sponsorship for this role and therefore it is essential that you already have the right to work in the UK.




Other job titles you may be searching for include: Data Analyst, Reporting Analyst, Insight Analyst, Performance Analyst, Business Intelligence Analyst, BI Analyst, Data & Insight Analyst.

Title: Ecommerce Data Analyst
Description:
hackajob is collaborating with Next Ltd to connect them with exceptional professionals for this role.

We are looking for an eCommerce Data Analyst to join our International eCommerce Third Parties team, to be at the forefront of our global growth! Based at NEXT Head Office in Leicestershire!

The Role

The International eCommerce Trading team fuels global growth by managing and optimising online product sales across 72 international markets.

Within this, the Third Parties team help connect NEXT with other large selling platforms such as Zalando and La Redoute to sell thousands of products. These platforms delivered strong growth for Next last year and to continue to support this growth we need ever improving data points and manipulation.

As an eCommerce Data Analyst, you will be the heart of our third party team, turning data from our partners into powerful insights and clearing the path for successful new launches. Your natural curiosity and commercial business sense will allow you to master new information quickly, helping you focus on what truly matters to our customers. By combining your passion for new technology like AI with a talent for building relationships across the company, you will lead the charge in using data to grow the business and find creative ways to solve everyday challenges.

What You’ll Take On


Be the knowledge centre of all available product and sales related data available to Third Parties.
Support the creation of partner dashboards conveying key partner information.
Deal with new partner data requirements.
Problem solve issues that cause items to be offline using data.
Leverage AI and automation tools to detect, predict, and prevent product errors and speed up time to online.
Build reports and visualisations for wider team and business visibility.
Communicate analysis to the wider team, share knowledge and understanding.
Build, maintain and grow relationships with all relevant internal and external stakeholders.
Explore the latest technologies available and select the right tools for the job.
Seek opportunities to continually modernise the way we work.


What You’ll Bring


Proven experience as a data analyst, specifically with the ability to ingest external data and map internal data to facilitate successful third party partner launches.
Hands-on experience with Databricks and Power BI to enable seamless collaboration with cross functional data teams.
A strong commercial background with the ability to distinguish business critical data.
A keen interest in AI and its application in solving complex data sharing and integration challenges.
An inquisitive nature that allows you to master new data structures quickly and easily.
Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to network business wide and champion the growth and use of data across the organization.
Degree-level education with prior experience in a dedicated data related role.
A self-motivated, highly organised problem solver with a meticulous eye for detail and the adaptability to tackle evolving challenges.


So if you’ve got experience in this field, are a data driven analyst with a commercial mindset, have strong experience working with Databricks, Power BI and different AI tools and are looking for the next challenge, this is the role for you!

Benefits


Competitive salary range
Flexible working options
Annual company performance based bonus
Sharesave scheme, which allows you to invest in NEXT and claim your share of our success
Annual Pay Reward
Pension 3% Employer/ 5% Employee
25 days holiday plus bank holidays (can also buy/sell up to 3 days)
Early finish on a Friday
25% staff discount and free next day delivery to desk for online purchases
Management Share Save Scheme
A newly refurbished subsidised restaurant and bar
Staff shops in Yorkshire and Leicestershire
Juice bar and coffee shop

Title: Data Analytics Consultant
Description:
About You




Do you have an analytical mind and enjoy working with data?




Are you passionate about delivering a great service to customers?




Are you an enthusiastic problem solver who enjoys working as part of a team?




Then this could be the role for you!




You will be…




Tech savvy and great with people
Passionate about data; comfortable analysing, manipulating, visualising and interpreting large data sets
Experienced in producing management information (MI) and working with databases
Proficient in SQL / Python or have an aptitude for other data interrogation or programming languages.
Eager to be part of a trusted team; bringing new ideas and sharing your thoughts with your team and the wider business
Able to lead at taking high level ideas, mapping out high level tech solutions, and turning them into technical data solutions
Able to understand and consider technical concepts and constraints
Experienced in software testing and/or user support
Great at working with different stakeholders including managing external customers’ expectations




A basic understanding of the various Heywood software and solutions as well as internally used systems would be advantageous.




The Role




Working on our Business Intelligence (BI) and Data Visualisation solution, Heywood Insights, you will primarily be working on designing, developing, testing, and rolling out content to our customers using the underlying software, Tableau. This could be core content or working with a specific customer (external or internal) on bespoke requirements.




You may also be required to support implementations of the product to new customers; completing deployments, product configuration and delivering customer training.




No two days will be the same; working on a variety of tasks providing a fantastic mix between technical and consultancy skills. With the support and expertise of the team around you, from day one you will be working with our customers and our award-winning product set.




Our culture nurtures continuous improvement and you will be encouraged to further your development through professional accreditations alongside internal training and knowledge transfer. There will be opportunities for you to work with different areas of the business, helping you gain a full understanding of who we are and what makes us successful.




About Us




Heywood Pension Technologies (‘HPT’) combines a passion for software with Agile methodologies to create modern software solutions for businesses, pension providers and third parties that help transform how their members manage their lifelong financial journeys.




Working for an expanding established market leader, you will have a real voice to influence our evolution. Continued learning and progression is ingrained in our daily life, encouraged through a variety of forums from e-learning subscriptions and a monthly down-tools day (“Hive Day”) and communities of practice for learning and experimentation. Our open culture encourages wide participation and innovation.




We also reward our hard work through regular socials, organised by our people. Socials events include fiercely competitive bake-offs, Pride month office parties, sporting events, games nights and much more!




We are committed to a hybrid working model, combining remote and office-based working.




Discover more at www.heywood.co.uk/careers




Legal Stuff




https://www.heywood.co.uk/privacy-notices

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
📊 Data Analyst | Boots Media Group at SMG

📍 Hybrid - Nottingham (2 days per week)

💰 From £40,000 + 10% Bonus




Do you love solving problems with data?




Are you someone who gets genuine satisfaction from writing a great SQL query, uncovering the story behind the numbers and turning insights into meaningful business decisions?




If so, we'd love to hear from you.




We're looking for a Data Analyst to join our award-winning partnership with Boots Media Group, helping some of the UK's biggest brands understand exactly how their omnichannel campaigns are performing.




This isn't just another reporting role.




You'll be working with Boots Advantage Card data, analysing campaign effectiveness, uncovering customer insights and helping shape the future of retail media. You'll collaborate with Data Scientists, Agency teams and Boots stakeholders to deliver analysis that genuinely influences marketing decisions.




What you'll be doing:

Writing SQL queries to extract and analyse large datasets.
Transforming complex data into clear, meaningful insights.
Managing agency data requests and becoming a trusted point of contact.
Identifying trends, answering commercial questions and uncovering the "so what?" behind the numbers.
Building client-ready reports and presenting findings with confidence.
Working alongside Insights, Agency and Boots teams to improve campaign performance for leading global brands.




We'd love to hear from you if you have...

✔ Strong SQL experience and confidence querying large datasets.

✔ Experience cleaning, manipulating and analysing data.

✔ A naturally curious mindset with excellent problem-solving skills.

✔ The ability to communicate technical findings to non-technical audiences.

✔ Python experience is a bonus, but not essential.




Why SMG?

At SMG, we're changing the way brands, retailers and shoppers connect.

We've been leading the retail media space since 2008 and continue to push the boundaries with cutting-edge technology, data and innovation. We're proud to be recognised as the #1 Best Marketing & Media Agency, and we're just getting started.




We're looking for people who enjoy building, improving and challenging the status quo. People who thrive in fast-moving environments, embrace change and want to keep learning.




What's in it for you?

🎁 10% discretionary bonus

💚 £1,800 annual wellbeing fund

📚 £500 annual learning fund

🧠 Free Headspace subscription

🌴 25 days holiday + bank holidays + 4 wellbeing days

⏰ 4pm finishes every Friday

🏡 Flexible hybrid working

🎉 Summer & Winter conferences, regular socials and celebrations throughout the year

And so much more.




Don't tick every box? Please apply anyway.




We're passionate about building diverse teams and know that great people don't always match every requirement on paper. If this role excites you, we'd love to hear your story.




Ready to use data to shape the future of retail media? Apply today.

💰 From £40,000 + 10% Bonus


Do you love solving problems with data?


Are you someone who gets genuine satisfaction from writing a great SQL query, uncovering the story behind the numbers and turning insights into meaningful business decisions?


If so, we'd love to hear from you.


We're looking for a Data Analyst to join our award-winning partnership with Boots Media Group, helping some of the UK's biggest brands understand exactly how their omnichannel campaigns are performing.


This isn't just another reporting role.


You'll be working with Boots Advantage Card data, analysing campaign effectiveness, uncovering customer insights and helping shape the future of retail media. You'll collaborate with Data Scientists, Agency teams and Boots stakeholders to deliver analysis that genuinely influences marketing decisions.


What you'll be doing:

  • Writing SQL queries to extract and analyse large datasets.
  • Transforming complex data into clear, meaningful insights.
  • Managing agency data requests and becoming a trusted point of contact.
  • Identifying trends, answering commercial questions and uncovering the "so what?" behind the numbers.
  • Building client-ready reports and presenting findings with confidence.
  • Working alongside Insights, Agency and Boots teams to improve campaign performance for leading global brands.


We'd love to hear from you if you have...

✔ Strong SQL experience and confidence querying large datasets.

✔ Experience cleaning, manipulating and analysing data.

✔ A naturally curious mindset with excellent problem-solving skills.

✔ The ability to communicate technical findings to non-technical audiences.

✔ Python experience is a bonus, but not essential.


Why SMG?

At SMG, we're changing the way brands, retailers and shoppers connect.

We've been leading the retail media space since 2008 and continue to push the boundaries with cutting-edge technology, data and innovation. We're proud to be recognised as the #1 Best Marketing & Media Agency, and we're just getting started.


We're looking for people who enjoy building, improving and challenging the status quo. People who thrive in fast-moving environments, embrace change and want to keep learning.


What's in it for you?

🎁 10% discretionary bonus

💚 £1,800 annual wellbeing fund

📚 £500 annual learning fund

🧠 Free Headspace subscription

🌴 25 days holiday + bank holidays + 4 wellbeing days

⏰ 4pm finishes every Friday

🏡 Flexible hybrid working

🎉 Summer & Winter conferences, regular socials and celebrations throughout the year

And so much more.


Don't tick every box? Please apply anyway.


We're passionate about building diverse teams and know that great people don't always match every requirement on paper. If this role excites you, we'd love to hear your story.


Ready to use data to shape the future of retail media? Apply today.

Title: Portfolio Analyst
Description:
Contract type: Permanent

Hours: Full-time, 35 hours

Location: Head Office, Nottingham (Hybrid working, minimum 2 days per week)

Application process: Please apply via the application button which will direct you to our careers site. If you require any adjustments to assist you in applying, please contact careers@thenottingham.com

At Nottingham Building Society our talent acquisition approach is rooted in openness and inclusive hiring, so even if you don’t feel you tick every box, we’d still genuinely love to hear from you.




We are looking for a data driven Portfolio Analyst to join our Portfolio Management Office and support the Senior Manager. You will play a key role in delivering clear insight across the change portfolio and improving how decisions are made.




This role combines demand management, portfolio reporting, benefits tracking and PMO analysis into one modern position. You will maintain accurate portfolio data, develop automated dashboards in JIRA and provide meaningful insight for governance forums.




Focusing on moving away from manual reporting, you will help create a more streamlined, data led approach that improves transparency, flow and decision making.




Here’s a taste of what you will be doing as a Portfolio Analyst at Nottingham Building Society:

Portfolio data and insight: Maintain accurate portfolio data, produce clear management information, and analyse trends, risks, and improvement opportunities to support decision making.
Automation and dashboards: Build and enhance automated dashboards in JIRA, ensuring consistent data capture while reducing reliance on manual reporting.
Demand and planning: Support demand intake, manage pipeline visibility, and assist with capacity and prioritisation to improve portfolio flow.
Benefits and value tracking: Capture, track, and report benefits from inception to realisation, maintaining clear views of forecast and delivered value.
Governance and assurance: Support governance through high quality data and analysis, carry out portfolio health checks, and uphold reporting standards.
Collaboration and continuous improvement: Partner with delivery, finance, and technology teams to provide insight and drive transparency and ongoing improvement across the PMO.




About You




Data and analytical expertise: Strong ability to work with complex data sets, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and meaningful insight.
Tools and portfolio knowledge: Hands on experience with JIRA and a solid understanding of portfolio, programme, and project management including governance and benefits tracking.
Insight and communication: Able to present complex information clearly and tailor messaging for different stakeholders.
Organisation and delivery: Structured and methodical approach to managing multiple priorities, data sources, and deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Portfolio and PMO experience: Proven experience supporting complex change portfolios, delivering reporting and insight for senior stakeholders, and improving processes through automation and standardisation.
Mindset and behaviours: Curious, detail focused, and collaborative, with confidence to challenge data, work through ambiguity, and build strong relationships across teams.




Reward & Benefits:




Competitive Package: Fair salary benchmarked against market data, annual discretionary bonus, and 29 days holiday plus bank holidays.
Health & Wellbeing: Access to Medicash, mental health first aiders, and a suite of wellbeing resources to support you inside and outside of work.
Work-Life Balance: 35-hour working week for full-time roles, with flexibility to help you perform at your best.
Career Growth: Ongoing personal and professional development, we’ll support your ambitions and help you grow your potential.
Inclusive Culture: Be part of a friendly, values-led team that genuinely cares about doing the right thing for colleagues and customers.
Giving Back: Use two paid volunteering days each year to support causes close to your heart, through our Samuel Fox Foundation.
Sustainability Focus: Join a business committed to reducing its carbon footprint and making a positive impact on the environment.
Free access to Octopus Money: Financial coaching & tools that help you plan, manage, and make the most of your money.




Embracing Diversity Together:




We proudly embrace and celebrate diversity as a fundamental cornerstone of our values. We believe that a diverse and inclusive workplace is not just essential for our success but is also a reflection of the vibrant communities we serve. Our commitment to diversity extends beyond our internal culture to the way we approach advertising and engage with our customers.




Our commitment means actively working to eliminate barriers and biases that may hinder equal opportunities within our organisation. We strive to ensure that all individuals, regardless of background, have an equal chance to thrive and advance in their careers.




We acknowledge that diversity is not just a goal to be achieved but a continuous journey toward creating an environment that embraces differences and promotes equal opportunities for all. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages collaboration, creativity, and a sense of belonging for every member of our community.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Xylem is a Fortune 500 global water solutions company dedicated to advancing sustainable impact and empowering the people who make water work every day. As a leading water technology company with 23,000 employees operating in over 150 countries, Xylem is at the forefront of addressing the world's most critical water challenges. We invite passionate individuals to join our team, dedicated to exceeding customer expectations through innovative and sustainable solutions.

Business Intelligence Analyst

Overview

Xylem is a leading global water technology company committed to solving the world’s most critical water challenges. We are seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst to support data-driven decision-making across the business by developing insightful reporting, dashboards, and analytical tools.

This role is ideally suited to a candidate with strong Power BI expertise and a good understanding of finance processes, who can bridge the gap between data, finance, and business performance. The successful candidate will help transform complex datasets into clear, actionable insights that support operational and strategic decision-making.

Key Responsibilities


Design, build, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports in Power BI for finance and business stakeholders.
Partner with FP&A, finance, and operational teams to understand reporting requirements and translate them into effective BI solutions.
Analyse financial and operational performance data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities.
Support budgeting, forecasting, and monthly performance review processes through robust reporting and insight generation.
Develop and optimise data models, datasets, and reporting structures to improve reporting accuracy and efficiency.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to improve data quality, consistency, and governance.
Automate manual reporting processes where possible to reduce effort and improve timeliness.
Provide variance analysis and ad hoc reporting to support decision-making across the business.
Ensure reporting outputs are accurate, reliable, and aligned with business definitions and KPIs.
Support continuous improvement initiatives in reporting, analytics, and performance management.


Required Qualifications And Experience


Proven experience in a Business Intelligence Analyst, Reporting Analyst, or similar analytical role.
Strong hands-on experience with Power BI, including dashboard development, data modelling, DAX, and Power Query.
Experience working with large and complex datasets from multiple sources.
Solid understanding of financial reporting, budgeting, forecasting principles.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to interpret data and communicate meaningful insights.
Advanced Excel skills and experience with reporting tools and data extraction processes.
Experience working with ERP or finance systems is desirable.
Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to work across finance and non-finance teams.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present technical findings in a clear and business-friendly way.
Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or a related discipline preferred.


Preferred Experience


Experience supporting finance team in a commercial or industrial environment.
Familiarity with SQL and other data querying / transformation tools.
Experience in data governance, master data, or reporting standardisation initiatives.
Exposure to international or matrix organisations.
Experience in manufacturing, engineering, utilities, or related sectors would be advantageous.


Key Competencies


Data visualisation and storytelling
Commercial and financial acumen
Attention to detail and accuracy
Continuous improvement mindset
Stakeholder collaboration and influence
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment


Join the global Xylem team to be a part of innovative technology solutions transforming water usage, conservation, and re-use. Our products impact public utilities, industrial sectors, residential areas, and commercial buildings, with a commitment to providing smart metering, network technologies, and advanced analytics for water, electric, and gas utilities. Partner with us in creating a world where water challenges are met with ingenuity and dedication; where we recognize the power of inclusion and belonging in driving innovation and allowing us to compete more effectively around the world.

Title: Commercial Analyst
Description:
Nottingham Trent House (95002), United Kingdom, Nottingham, Nottinghamshire

Commercial Analyst

About This Role

This is an exciting opportunity to join a high-performing team who are passionate about driving our co-brand credit card partnerships to market-leading results.

As the champion of your partnership from the ground up, you’ll not only be hands-on (pulling data using SQL and structuring analysis to support your ideas), but effective at managing and influencing stakeholders of all levels up to C-suite.

What You’ll Do


Work directly with SQL to interrogate vast datasets and analyse trends to spot gaps for improvement and growth
Own external relationships and become the internal subject matter expert on all things relating to your partner
Deliver the roadmap and marketing strategy for your partner - from managing product launches to shaping the direction for marketing campaigns
Collaborate with teams across the business and be proactive to drive growth that mutually benefits your partner and Capital One
Ensure all aspects of your partnership are managed with the utmost attention to detail, from performance monitoring and forecasting to invoicing and regulatory compliance


We often rotate our associates around , so you may have the opportunity to work on some of our other existing co-brand partnerships in the future.

What We’re Looking For


Excellent interpersonal skills with a positive and collaborative approach, and confidence navigating challenging conversations with stakeholders
A resilient mindset, with a desire to work in a fast paced environment, balancing ever-changing priorities
Strong mathematical and creative problem-solving skills, with the ability to turn complex analysis into clear stories and actions
A structured and well-organised approach to all aspects of the role, showing careful attention to detail at all times


Desirable (not essential)


Experience using SQL (or similar) to query large databases - we primarily use Snowflake
Experience working within a regulated environment
Background in owning marketing channel performance, third party relationships and/or operational elements (e.g. invoicing, contract negotiations, change delivery)


Where And How You'll Work


This is a permanent position and can be based in either our Nottingham or London offices.
Our hybrid working model offers you the flexibility to work from our offices and from home, when you need to.
We're big on collaboration and connection, and so generally encourage our associates to use our offices on Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Thursdays.
The number of days you spend in the office will usually be led by the type of work you’re doing, and the hybrid working patterns of the people you partner most closely with.
Many of our associates have flexible working arrangements, and we're open to talking about an arrangement that works for you.


What’s In It For You


Bring us all this - and you’ll be well rewarded with a role contributing to the roadmap of an organisation committed to transformation
We offer high performers strong and diverse career progression, investing heavily in developing great people through our Capital One University training programmes (and appropriate external providers)
Immediate access to our core benefits including pension scheme, bonus, generous holiday entitlement and private medical insurance – with flexible benefits available including season-ticket loans, cycle to work scheme and enhanced parental leave
Open-plan workspaces and accessible facilities designed to inspire and support you. Our Nottingham head-office has a fully-serviced gym, subsidised restaurant, mindfulness and music rooms. In London, you can heighten your mood with a run on our rooftop running track or an espresso at the Workshop Coffee café


What You Should Know About How We Recruit

We pride ourselves on hiring the best people, not the same people. Building diverse and inclusive teams is the right thing to do and the smart thing to do. We want to work with top talent: whoever you are, whatever you look like, wherever you come from. We know it’s about what you do, not just what you say. That’s why we make our recruitment process fair and accessible. And we offer benefits that attract people at all ages and stages.

We Also Partner With Organisations Including The Women In Finance And Race At Work Charters, Stonewall And UpReach To Find People From Every Walk Of Life And Help Them Thrive With Us. We Have a Whole Host Of Internal Networks And Support Groups You Could Be Involved In, To Name a Few


REACH – Race Equality and Culture Heritage group focuses on representation, retention and engagement for associates from minority ethnic groups and allies
OutFront – to provide LGBTQ+ support for all associates
Mind Your Mind – signposting support and promoting positive mental wellbeing for all
Women in Tech – promoting an inclusive environment in tech
EmpowHER - network of female associates and allies focusing on developing future leaders, particularly for female talent in our industry


Capital One is committed to diversity in the workplace.

If you require a reasonable adjustment, please contact ukrecruitment@capitalone.com All information will be kept confidential and will only be used for the purpose of applying a reasonable adjustment.

For technical support or questions about Capital One's recruiting process, please send an email to Careers@capitalone.com

Capital One does not provide, endorse nor guarantee and is not liable for third-party products, services, educational tools or other information available through this site.

Capital One Financial is made up of several different entities. Please note that any position posted in Canada is for Capital One Canada, any position posted in the United Kingdom is for Capital One Europe and any position posted in the Philippines is for Capital One Philippines Service Corp. (COPSSC).

Who We Are

At Capital One, we're building a leading information-based technology company. Still founder-led by Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Richard Fairbank, Capital One is on a mission to help our customers succeed by bringing ingenuity, simplicity, and humanity to banking. We measure our efforts by the success our customers enjoy and the advocacy they exhibit. We are succeeding because they are succeeding.

Guided by our shared values, we thrive in an environment where collaboration and openness are valued. We believe that innovation is powered by perspective and that teamwork and respect for each other lead to superior results. We elevate each other and obsess about doing the right thing. Our associates serve with humility and a deep respect for their responsibility in helping our customers achieve their goals and realize their dreams. Together, we are on a quest to change banking for good.

Title: Compliance Data Specialist
Description:
Join Biffa Producer Compliance Solutions as a Compliance Data Specialist, where you'll play a key role in ensuring accurate, compliant regulatory submissions for a portfolio of producer customers.

This is a predominantly data-focused role (approximately 80%), centred on analysing, validating and standardising customer-submitted data to meet regulatory requirements. Alongside this, you'll act as a trusted point of contact for customers, providing clear guidance and support throughout compliance submission periods.

We're looking for someone highly analytical, detail-oriented and confident working with complex Excel datasets — as well as comfortable communicating clearly with customers when needed.

A quick look at the role.

As a Compliance Data Specialist, you will take ownership of customer data from receipt through to final submission. Customer datasets often vary in structure and format, so you'll use advanced Excel skills and logical problem-solving to cleanse, manipulate and standardise information in line with regulatory requirements.

You will work closely with internal teams to ensure smooth submission cycles, resolve data queries efficiently, and maintain high standards of accuracy and documentation.

Your Core Responsibilities.


Receive and review customer data submissions in various Excel formats
Cleanse, validate and standardise datasets to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements
Perform calculations, reconciliations and data checks with high accuracy
Identify inconsistencies or gaps in data and proactively resolve issues
Act as the main point of contact for allocated customers during submission periods
Provide clear guidance on data requirements and submission timelines
Support customers during Environment Agency audits, including preparing and reviewing required data and documentation
Collaborate with internal teams to ensure efficient and documented processes
Contribute to continuous improvement of data handling and compliance processes
Support internal colleagues with new business leads as required



Requirements

Our essential requirements.


Highly proficient in Microsoft Excel (including formulas, data manipulation and validation techniques)
Naturally analytical with strong problem-solving ability
Meticulous, organised and comfortable working with large datasets
Confident communicating clearly and professionally with customers
Able to manage multiple priorities during busy submission periods
Proactive and self-motivated



(Prior knowledge of environmental regulations, such as Extended Producer Responsibility and Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment, is helpful but not required — full training will be provided).

Benefits

And here's why you'll love it at Biffa.


Ongoing career development, training and coaching - Because if you don't grow, we don't grow
Competitive salary
Generous pension scheme
Retail and leisure discounts
Holiday and travel discounts
Life cover



About Biffa -

At Biffa, we love working with waste. Whether we're turning it into sustainable power, finding new ways to recycle it or simply keeping it off the streets, we believe every day is an opportunity to improve the lives of millions. It's a view that's shared by our 11,500+ people around the country, who trust us to provide them with a career that's always rewarding, often challenging, but never dull.

Dedicated to Diversity

Being inclusive is core to our culture at Biffa; we believe different ideas, perspectives and backgrounds are key to developing a creative and effective working environment that represents our communities and generates the best outcomes for colleagues, customers and stakeholders, which is why you'll find us championing diversity, equity and inclusion at every turn.

HTML:
Join Biffa Producer Compliance Solutions as a Compliance Data Specialist, where you'll play a key role in ensuring accurate, compliant regulatory submissions for a portfolio of producer customers.

This is a predominantly data-focused role (approximately 80%), centred on analysing, validating and standardising customer-submitted data to meet regulatory requirements. Alongside this, you'll act as a trusted point of contact for customers, providing clear guidance and support throughout compliance submission periods.

We're looking for someone highly analytical, detail-oriented and confident working with complex Excel datasets — as well as comfortable communicating clearly with customers when needed.

A quick look at the role.

As a Compliance Data Specialist, you will take ownership of customer data from receipt through to final submission. Customer datasets often vary in structure and format, so you'll use advanced Excel skills and logical problem-solving to cleanse, manipulate and standardise information in line with regulatory requirements.

You will work closely with internal teams to ensure smooth submission cycles, resolve data queries efficiently, and maintain high standards of accuracy and documentation.

Your Core Responsibilities.

  • Receive and review customer data submissions in various Excel formats
  • Cleanse, validate and standardise datasets to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements
  • Perform calculations, reconciliations and data checks with high accuracy
  • Identify inconsistencies or gaps in data and proactively resolve issues
  • Act as the main point of contact for allocated customers during submission periods
  • Provide clear guidance on data requirements and submission timelines
  • Support customers during Environment Agency audits, including preparing and reviewing required data and documentation
  • Collaborate with internal teams to ensure efficient and documented processes
  • Contribute to continuous improvement of data handling and compliance processes
  • Support internal colleagues with new business leads as required


Requirements

Our essential requirements.

  • Highly proficient in Microsoft Excel (including formulas, data manipulation and validation techniques)
  • Naturally analytical with strong problem-solving ability
  • Meticulous, organised and comfortable working with large datasets
  • Confident communicating clearly and professionally with customers
  • Able to manage multiple priorities during busy submission periods
  • Proactive and self-motivated


(Prior knowledge of environmental regulations, such as Extended Producer Responsibility and Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment, is helpful but not required — full training will be provided).

Benefits

And here's why you'll love it at Biffa.

  • Ongoing career development, training and coaching - Because if you don't grow, we don't grow
  • Competitive salary
  • Generous pension scheme
  • Retail and leisure discounts
  • Holiday and travel discounts
  • Life cover


About Biffa -

At Biffa, we love working with waste. Whether we're turning it into sustainable power, finding new ways to recycle it or simply keeping it off the streets, we believe every day is an opportunity to improve the lives of millions. It's a view that's shared by our 11,500+ people around the country, who trust us to provide them with a career that's always rewarding, often challenging, but never dull.

Dedicated to Diversity

Being inclusive is core to our culture at Biffa; we believe different ideas, perspectives and backgrounds are key to developing a creative and effective working environment that represents our communities and generates the best outcomes for colleagues, customers and stakeholders, which is why you'll find us championing diversity, equity and inclusion at every turn.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
📊 Data Analyst | Boots Media Group at SMG

📍 Hybrid - Nottingham (2 days per week)

💰 From £40,000 + 10% Bonus




Do you love solving problems with data?




Are you someone who gets genuine satisfaction from writing a great SQL query, uncovering the story behind the numbers and turning insights into meaningful business decisions?




If so, we'd love to hear from you.




We're looking for a Data Analyst to join our award-winning partnership with Boots Media Group, helping some of the UK's biggest brands understand exactly how their omnichannel campaigns are performing.




This isn't just another reporting role.




You'll be working with Boots Advantage Card data, analysing campaign effectiveness, uncovering customer insights and helping shape the future of retail media. You'll collaborate with Data Scientists, Agency teams and Boots stakeholders to deliver analysis that genuinely influences marketing decisions.




What you'll be doing:

Writing SQL queries to extract and analyse large datasets.
Transforming complex data into clear, meaningful insights.
Managing agency data requests and becoming a trusted point of contact.
Identifying trends, answering commercial questions and uncovering the "so what?" behind the numbers.
Building client-ready reports and presenting findings with confidence.
Working alongside Insights, Agency and Boots teams to improve campaign performance for leading global brands.




We'd love to hear from you if you have...

✔ Strong SQL experience and confidence querying large datasets.

✔ Experience cleaning, manipulating and analysing data.

✔ A naturally curious mindset with excellent problem-solving skills.

✔ The ability to communicate technical findings to non-technical audiences.

✔ Python experience is a bonus, but not essential.




Why SMG?

At SMG, we're changing the way brands, retailers and shoppers connect.

We've been leading the retail media space since 2008 and continue to push the boundaries with cutting-edge technology, data and innovation. We're proud to be recognised as the #1 Best Marketing & Media Agency, and we're just getting started.




We're looking for people who enjoy building, improving and challenging the status quo. People who thrive in fast-moving environments, embrace change and want to keep learning.




What's in it for you?

🎁 10% discretionary bonus

💚 £1,800 annual wellbeing fund

📚 £500 annual learning fund

🧠 Free Headspace subscription

🌴 25 days holiday + bank holidays + 4 wellbeing days

⏰ 4pm finishes every Friday

🏡 Flexible hybrid working

🎉 Summer & Winter conferences, regular socials and celebrations throughout the year

And so much more.




Don't tick every box? Please apply anyway.




We're passionate about building diverse teams and know that great people don't always match every requirement on paper. If this role excites you, we'd love to hear your story.




Ready to use data to shape the future of retail media? Apply today.

Title: Portfolio Analyst
Description:
Contract type: Permanent

Hours: Full-time, 35 hours

Location: Head Office, Nottingham (Hybrid working, minimum 2 days per week)

Application process: Please apply via the application button which will direct you to our careers site. If you require any adjustments to assist you in applying, please contact careers@thenottingham.com

At Nottingham Building Society our talent acquisition approach is rooted in openness and inclusive hiring, so even if you don’t feel you tick every box, we’d still genuinely love to hear from you.




We are looking for a data driven Portfolio Analyst to join our Portfolio Management Office and support the Senior Manager. You will play a key role in delivering clear insight across the change portfolio and improving how decisions are made.




This role combines demand management, portfolio reporting, benefits tracking and PMO analysis into one modern position. You will maintain accurate portfolio data, develop automated dashboards in JIRA and provide meaningful insight for governance forums.




Focusing on moving away from manual reporting, you will help create a more streamlined, data led approach that improves transparency, flow and decision making.




Here’s a taste of what you will be doing as a Portfolio Analyst at Nottingham Building Society:

Portfolio data and insight: Maintain accurate portfolio data, produce clear management information, and analyse trends, risks, and improvement opportunities to support decision making.
Automation and dashboards: Build and enhance automated dashboards in JIRA, ensuring consistent data capture while reducing reliance on manual reporting.
Demand and planning: Support demand intake, manage pipeline visibility, and assist with capacity and prioritisation to improve portfolio flow.
Benefits and value tracking: Capture, track, and report benefits from inception to realisation, maintaining clear views of forecast and delivered value.
Governance and assurance: Support governance through high quality data and analysis, carry out portfolio health checks, and uphold reporting standards.
Collaboration and continuous improvement: Partner with delivery, finance, and technology teams to provide insight and drive transparency and ongoing improvement across the PMO.




About You




Data and analytical expertise: Strong ability to work with complex data sets, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and meaningful insight.
Tools and portfolio knowledge: Hands on experience with JIRA and a solid understanding of portfolio, programme, and project management including governance and benefits tracking.
Insight and communication: Able to present complex information clearly and tailor messaging for different stakeholders.
Organisation and delivery: Structured and methodical approach to managing multiple priorities, data sources, and deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Portfolio and PMO experience: Proven experience supporting complex change portfolios, delivering reporting and insight for senior stakeholders, and improving processes through automation and standardisation.
Mindset and behaviours: Curious, detail focused, and collaborative, with confidence to challenge data, work through ambiguity, and build strong relationships across teams.




Reward & Benefits:




Competitive Package: Fair salary benchmarked against market data, annual discretionary bonus, and 29 days holiday plus bank holidays.
Health & Wellbeing: Access to Medicash, mental health first aiders, and a suite of wellbeing resources to support you inside and outside of work.
Work-Life Balance: 35-hour working week for full-time roles, with flexibility to help you perform at your best.
Career Growth: Ongoing personal and professional development, we’ll support your ambitions and help you grow your potential.
Inclusive Culture: Be part of a friendly, values-led team that genuinely cares about doing the right thing for colleagues and customers.
Giving Back: Use two paid volunteering days each year to support causes close to your heart, through our Samuel Fox Foundation.
Sustainability Focus: Join a business committed to reducing its carbon footprint and making a positive impact on the environment.
Free access to Octopus Money: Financial coaching & tools that help you plan, manage, and make the most of your money.




Embracing Diversity Together:




We proudly embrace and celebrate diversity as a fundamental cornerstone of our values. We believe that a diverse and inclusive workplace is not just essential for our success but is also a reflection of the vibrant communities we serve. Our commitment to diversity extends beyond our internal culture to the way we approach advertising and engage with our customers.




Our commitment means actively working to eliminate barriers and biases that may hinder equal opportunities within our organisation. We strive to ensure that all individuals, regardless of background, have an equal chance to thrive and advance in their careers.




We acknowledge that diversity is not just a goal to be achieved but a continuous journey toward creating an environment that embraces differences and promotes equal opportunities for all. We are committed to creating an inclusive culture that encourages collaboration, creativity, and a sense of belonging for every member of our community.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Xylem is a Fortune 500 global water solutions company dedicated to advancing sustainable impact and empowering the people who make water work every day. As a leading water technology company with 23,000 employees operating in over 150 countries, Xylem is at the forefront of addressing the world's most critical water challenges. We invite passionate individuals to join our team, dedicated to exceeding customer expectations through innovative and sustainable solutions.

Business Intelligence Analyst

Overview

Xylem is a leading global water technology company committed to solving the world’s most critical water challenges. We are seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst to support data-driven decision-making across the business by developing insightful reporting, dashboards, and analytical tools.

This role is ideally suited to a candidate with strong Power BI expertise and a good understanding of finance processes, who can bridge the gap between data, finance, and business performance. The successful candidate will help transform complex datasets into clear, actionable insights that support operational and strategic decision-making.

Key Responsibilities


Design, build, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports in Power BI for finance and business stakeholders.
Partner with FP&A, finance, and operational teams to understand reporting requirements and translate them into effective BI solutions.
Analyse financial and operational performance data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities.
Support budgeting, forecasting, and monthly performance review processes through robust reporting and insight generation.
Develop and optimise data models, datasets, and reporting structures to improve reporting accuracy and efficiency.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to improve data quality, consistency, and governance.
Automate manual reporting processes where possible to reduce effort and improve timeliness.
Provide variance analysis and ad hoc reporting to support decision-making across the business.
Ensure reporting outputs are accurate, reliable, and aligned with business definitions and KPIs.
Support continuous improvement initiatives in reporting, analytics, and performance management.


Required Qualifications And Experience


Proven experience in a Business Intelligence Analyst, Reporting Analyst, or similar analytical role.
Strong hands-on experience with Power BI, including dashboard development, data modelling, DAX, and Power Query.
Experience working with large and complex datasets from multiple sources.
Solid understanding of financial reporting, budgeting, forecasting principles.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to interpret data and communicate meaningful insights.
Advanced Excel skills and experience with reporting tools and data extraction processes.
Experience working with ERP or finance systems is desirable.
Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to work across finance and non-finance teams.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present technical findings in a clear and business-friendly way.
Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or a related discipline preferred.


Preferred Experience


Experience supporting finance team in a commercial or industrial environment.
Familiarity with SQL and other data querying / transformation tools.
Experience in data governance, master data, or reporting standardisation initiatives.
Exposure to international or matrix organisations.
Experience in manufacturing, engineering, utilities, or related sectors would be advantageous.


Key Competencies


Data visualisation and storytelling
Commercial and financial acumen
Attention to detail and accuracy
Continuous improvement mindset
Stakeholder collaboration and influence
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment


Join the global Xylem team to be a part of innovative technology solutions transforming water usage, conservation, and re-use. Our products impact public utilities, industrial sectors, residential areas, and commercial buildings, with a commitment to providing smart metering, network technologies, and advanced analytics for water, electric, and gas utilities. Partner with us in creating a world where water challenges are met with ingenuity and dedication; where we recognize the power of inclusion and belonging in driving innovation and allowing us to compete more effectively around the world.
